# Janji di Bukit Aqabah

## 🔍 Mencari Teman Baru

Setelah melewati tahun yang sangat sedih dan menyakitkan, **Nabi Muhammad SAW** tidak menyerah. Beliau terus mencari orang-orang yang mau mendengarkan tentang Islam. Di Makkah, para pemimpin Quraisy sudah menutup telinga mereka. Tapi Nabi tahu bahwa di luar Makkah, masih banyak orang baik yang belum mendengar tentang Allah dan Rasul-Nya.

Setiap tahun, ada musim haji — waktu ketika orang-orang dari seluruh Jazirah Arab datang ke Makkah. Nabi Muhammad SAW mendatangi kemah-kemah mereka satu per satu. Beliau memperkenalkan diri, membacakan ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an yang indah, dan mengajak mereka beriman kepada Allah. Sebagian orang menolak. Ada juga yang mengusir beliau. Tapi Nabi tidak pernah putus asa!

Lalu pada suatu hari, beliau bertemu dengan enam orang dari kota Yatsrib — sebuah kota yang jauh di utara Makkah. Ketika mereka mendengar Al-Qur'an, mata mereka berbinar-binar. Mereka saling berbisik: "Ini dia nabi yang selalu diceritakan oleh orang-orang Yahudi! Ayo kita beriman kepadanya sebelum yang lain!"

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Tenda-tenda putih jamaah haji di padang pasir dekat Makkah pada sore hari. Nabi Muhammad SAW duduk bersila di depan enam orang laki-laki dari Yatsrib yang mendengarkan dengan penuh perhatian, wajah mereka kagum. Di tangan Nabi, isyarat lembut saat berbicara. Warna putih tenda, emas pasir, biru langit sore. Mood penuh harapan dan kegembiraan. Komposisi melingkar — Nabi dan enam orang duduk membentuk lingkaran kecil, tenda-tenda di latar belakang.*

---

## 🤝 Dua Belas Orang Pemberani

Setahun kemudian, pada musim haji berikutnya, datanglah dua belas orang dari Yatsrib untuk menemui Nabi Muhammad SAW! Mereka datang secara diam-diam di malam hari ke sebuah tempat bernama **Aqabah** — sebuah lembah kecil dekat Mina.

Dua belas orang ini sangat istimewa. Sepuluh orang dari suku Khazraj dan dua orang dari suku Aus. Tahukah ? Kedua suku ini sudah bermusuhan dan berperang selama bertahun-tahun! Tapi sekarang, karena Islam, mereka duduk bersama-sama di depan Nabi Muhammad SAW, bersatu sebagai saudara.

Di tempat yang sunyi dan gelap itu, dua belas orang pemberani ini mengucapkan janji yang sangat penting. Mereka berjanji:

- Tidak menyembah selain Allah
- Tidak mencuri
- Tidak berbohong
- Tidak berlaku jahat
- Selalu patuh kepada Rasulullah dalam kebaikan

Ini bukan janji biasa, . Ini adalah janji yang mengubah sejarah! Janji ini disebut **Bai'at Aqabah Pertama**.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Malam hari di lembah Aqabah yang sempit. Nabi Muhammad SAW duduk di atas batu, dua belas orang laki-laki berlutut di depannya, tangan mereka terulur untuk berjabat tangan dengan Nabi sebagai tanda bai'at. Cahaya bulan sabit menerangi wajah-wajah penuh tekad. Warna biru gelap malam, perak cahaya bulan, coklat batu. Mood penuh keberanian dan kesakralan. Komposisi setengah lingkaran — dua belas orang mengelilingi Nabi, bulan sabit di langit.*

---

## 📖 Guru Pertama di Yatsrib

Setelah bai'at, Nabi Muhammad SAW membuat keputusan yang sangat cerdas. Beliau mengutus seorang pemuda bernama **Mush'ab bin Umair** untuk pergi ke Yatsrib dan mengajarkan Islam di sana.

Mush'ab dulunya adalah pemuda kaya dari Makkah. Bajunya selalu bagus, wanginya selalu harum, dan hidupnya sangat mewah. Tapi ketika ia memeluk Islam, ia meninggalkan semua kemewahan itu. Orangtuanya marah dan mengusirnya. Tapi Mush'ab tidak peduli — baginya, Islam lebih berharga dari semua harta di dunia.

Di Yatsrib, Mush'ab tinggal di rumah **As'ad bin Zurarah** dan mulai mengajar orang-orang membaca Al-Qur'an. Ia sangat sabar dan lemah lembut. Ia tidak pernah memaksa siapa pun — ia hanya membacakan ayat-ayat Allah dengan suara yang indah, dan orang-orang datang sendiri untuk mendengarkan.

Salah satu orang penting yang masuk Islam berkat Mush'ab adalah **Sa'd bin Mu'adz**, pemimpin besar suku Aus. Ketika Sa'd masuk Islam, hampir seluruh suku Aus ikut masuk Islam juga! Satu guru muda berhasil mengubah seluruh kota.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Halaman rumah sederhana di Yatsrib pada pagi hari cerah. Mush'ab bin Umair — pemuda dengan pakaian sederhana namun wajah bercahaya — duduk di bawah pohon kurma, memegang lembaran mushaf Al-Qur'an. Di depannya, sekelompok orang dewasa dan anak-anak duduk melingkar mendengarkan dengan khusyuk. Warna hijau dedaunan kurma, coklat hangat tacahaya pagi keemasan. Mood penuh kedamaian dan ilmu. Komposisi melingkar — Mush'ab di tengah, murid-murid mengelilinginya di bawah naungan pohon.*

---

## 🌟 Benih yang Tumbuh Menjadi Pohon Besar

Kisah Bai'at Aqabah Pertama mengajarkan kita pelajaran yang sangat indah. Hanya dua belas orang. Jumlah yang sangat sedikit. Tapi dari dua belas orang inilah, Islam tumbuh dan berkembang di kota Yatsrib.

Setahun kemudian, bukan dua belas lagi yang datang — tapi **tujuh puluh lima orang**! Mereka melakukan Bai'at Aqabah Kedua dan berjanji akan melindungi Nabi Muhammad SAW seperti mereka melindungi keluarga sendiri.

Dan tidak lama setelah itu, Nabi Muhammad SAW hijrah ke Yatsrib. Kota itu kemudian berganti nama menjadi **Madinah** — kota Nabi. Di sanalah Islam berkembang pesat, di sanalah masjid pertama dibangun, dan di sanalah masyarakat Islam yang adil dan damai terbentuk.

Semua bermula dari dua belas orang pemberani yang berani mengucapkan janji di malam hari di lembah Aqabah. Seperti benih kecil yang ditanam di talalu tumbuh menjadi pohon besar yang rindang dan berbuah lebat.

Pelajarannya untuk kita: jangan pernah merasa bahwa kebaikan yang kita lakukan itu kecil. Bisa jadi satu kebaikan kecil akan tumbuh menjadi sesuatu yang sangat besar. Yang penting, kita berani memulainya!

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Gambaran split — di kiri, dua belas orang berdiri di lembah Aqabah yang gelap pada malam hari; di kanan, kota Madinah yang ramai dan bercahaya dengan masjid Nabawi di tengahnya pada siang hari cerah. Di antara keduanya, sebuah pohon tumbuh dari kiri ke kanan — dari benih kecil menjadi pohon rindang. Warna transisi dari biru gelap (kiri) ke emas terang (kanan). Mood transformatif dan penuh harapan. Komposisi horizontal split — perjalanan waktu dari kiri ke kanan.*
e13-baiat-aqabah.general-en:
# The First Pledge of Aqabah — 12 People from Medina Swear Allegiance (621 CE)

## Context

After *'Am al-Huzn* (the Year of Sorrow) and the rejection at Ta'if, **Prophet Muhammad SAW** faced the reality that preaching in Mecca had reached a point of saturation. Tribal protection had been lost with the death of **Abu Talib**, and **Abu Lahab**, who succeeded him as head of Banu Hashim, had withdrawn protection from the Prophet. The situation in Mecca grew increasingly unsafe for him and his followers.^1

Under these circumstances, Prophet Muhammad SAW began shifting his preaching strategy. He utilized the pilgrimage season — when people from across the Arabian Peninsula flocked to Mecca — to present Islam to various tribal delegations. He visited the tents of pilgrims, introduced himself, recited the Qur'an, and invited them to believe. Most rejected him, some even drove him away. Yet he never stopped trying.^2

The city of **Yathrib** (which would later become Medina) had a unique socio-political situation. It was inhabited by two large Arab tribes — **Aws** and **Khazraj** — who had been feuding for several generations. Their last war, the Battle of Bu'ath (617 CE), had exhausted both sides and created a longing for peace and leadership that could unite them.^3

Furthermore, the people of Yathrib were already familiar with the concepts of prophethood and scripture through their interactions with three Jewish tribes living in the city — **Banu Qaynuqa'**, **Banu Nadir**, and **Banu Qurayzah**. The Jews would frequently mention the coming of a final prophet, and this made the Arab inhabitants of Yathrib more receptive to the idea of prophethood than other Arab tribes.^4

## The Events

### The Initial Meeting During the Pilgrimage Season (620 CE)

A year before the First Pledge of Aqabah, during the pilgrimage season of the eleventh year of prophethood (620 CE), Prophet Muhammad SAW met a small group from the Khazraj tribe near Aqabah. They numbered six: **As'ad ibn Zurarah**, **Awf ibn al-Harith**, **Rafi' ibn Malik**, **Qutbah ibn Amir**, **Uqbah ibn Amir**, and **Jabir ibn Abdullah ibn Ri'ab**.^5

When the Prophet recited the Qur'an and explained Islam to them, they exchanged glances and said: "By Allah, this is the prophet that the Jews have been promising. Let us not let them precede us in believing in him!"^6

These six men embraced Islam and returned to Yathrib with burning zeal. They began spreading Islam in their city, so that scarcely a household in Yathrib was left without discussion of Muhammad.^7

### The First Pledge of Aqabah (621 CE)

During the following pilgrimage season, the twelfth year of prophethood (621 CE), twelve men from Yathrib came to meet Prophet Muhammad SAW at Aqabah — a narrow pass between Mina and Mecca which served as the location for the stoning of the *jamarat*. Of these twelve, ten were from the Khazraj tribe and two from the Aws — marking the initial unification of the two previously feuding tribes.^8

The twelve men were:^9

**From the Khazraj tribe:**
1. **As'ad ibn Zurarah** (also present at the previous year's meeting)
2. **Awf ibn al-Harith ibn Rifa'ah**
3. **Rafi' ibn Malik ibn al-Ajlan**
4. **Qutbah ibn Amir ibn Hadidah**
5. **Uqbah ibn Amir ibn Nabi**
6. **Jabir ibn Abdullah ibn Ri'ab**
7. **Mu'adh ibn al-Harith** (brother of Awf)
8. **Dhakwan ibn Abd Qays**
9. **Ubadah ibn al-Samit**
10. **Yazid ibn Tha'labah**

**From the Aws tribe:**
11. **Abu al-Haytham ibn al-Tayyihan**
12. **Uwaym ibn Sa'idah**

### The Contents of the Pledge

**Ubadah ibn al-Samit** narrated the contents of the pledge they made to Prophet Muhammad SAW. This pledge is also known as *Bay'at al-Nisa'* (the Pledge of Women) because its contents were identical to the pledge later required of women — namely, a moral and spiritual pledge without military commitment:^1 ^0

The pledge comprised the following:

1. **Not to associate anything with Allah**
2. **Not to steal**
3. **Not to commit adultery**
4. **Not to kill their children** (the practice of burying infant daughters alive)
5. **Not to fabricate falsehood** between their hands and feet (false accusations)
6. **Not to disobey** the Messenger in any matter of goodness (*ma'ruf*)

Prophet Muhammad SAW then said:

"Whoever among you fulfills this pledge, his reward lies with Allah. And whoever commits any of these sins and is punished for it in this world, that punishment shall serve as expiation. And whoever commits any of these sins and Allah conceals it (in this world), then his affair is with Allah — if He wills, He forgives him, and if He wills, He punishes him."^1 ^1

### The Dispatch of Mus'ab ibn Umayr

Following the pledge, Prophet Muhammad SAW sent **Mus'ab ibn Umayr** to Yathrib as the first ambassador and teacher of Islam. Mus'ab was a young Qurayshi man formerly known for his luxurious lifestyle, who had abandoned everything for the sake of Islam. He stayed at the house of As'ad ibn Zurarah and began teaching the Qur'an and Islamic teachings.^1 ^2

Mus'ab proved himself an extraordinarily effective preacher. With his wisdom and gentle approach, he succeeded in converting key figures of Yathrib to Islam, including **Sa'd ibn Mu'adh**, the leader of the Aws tribe, and **Usayd ibn Hudayr**, whose conversion became a turning point for the entire Aws tribe.^1 ^3

The process of Islamization of Yathrib through Mus'ab lasted approximately one year, and its results were remarkable: at the following pilgrimage season (622 CE), seventy-three men and two women from Yathrib came to perform the Second Pledge of Aqabah — a pledge that this time included a military commitment to protect the Prophet — opening the way for the momentous event of the Hijrah.^1 ^4

## Dialogue and Sacred Texts

The First Pledge of Aqabah represents a strategic turning point in the history of the Islamic mission. The primary textual evidence for this event comes from hadith narrations.

Ubadah ibn al-Samit narrated concerning this pledge:

> بَايَعْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَلَى أَنْ لاَ نُشْرِكَ بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَلاَ نَسْرِقَ وَلاَ نَزْنِيَ وَلاَ نَقْتُلَ أَوْلاَدَنَا وَلاَ نَأْتِيَ بِبُهْتَانٍ نَفْتَرِيهِ بَيْنَ أَيْدِينَا وَأَرْجُلِنَا وَلاَ نَعْصِيَهُ فِي مَعْرُوفٍ
>
> *"We pledged allegiance to the Messenger of Allah SAW that we would not associate anything with Allah, would not steal, would not commit adultery, would not kill our children, would not bring forth any falsehood that we fabricate between our hands and feet, and would not disobey him in any matter of goodness. ﴾23﴿"*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 18, Kitab al-Iman; also Muslim, no. 1709

Regarding the opening of new paths for the message through new people, Allah the Almighty says:

> وَلَوْ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا لَّأَسْمَعَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ لَتَوَلَّوا وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ ﴿٢٣﴾
>
> *"Had Allah known any good in them, He would have made them hear. And if He had made them hear, they would still have turned away while they were refusing. ﴾23﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Anfal (8): 23

This verse, though its context of revelation relates to the Battle of Badr, holds relevance to the dynamics of preaching in Mecca — where many Meccans heard the call yet turned away, while the people of Yathrib who heard it for the first time embraced it wholeheartedly.^1 ^5

## Analysis and Lessons

### A Shift in Preaching Strategy

The First Pledge of Aqabah marks a fundamental shift in the strategy of Islamic preaching — from a local approach in Mecca to regional expansion. Prophet Muhammad SAW demonstrated remarkable strategic intelligence: when one door closed (Mecca), he opened another (Yathrib). This approach reflects the Qur'anic principle that Allah's help can come from unexpected directions.^1 ^6

### Pilgrimage Season Diplomacy

The utilization of the pilgrimage season as an arena for preaching demonstrates the Prophet's understanding of Arabian socio-political dynamics. The pilgrimage season was the only time when tribal norms prohibited violence and allowed for safe cross-tribal communication. The Prophet made maximum use of this "safe zone" — an early example of religious diplomacy leveraging an existing public space.^1 ^7

### The Significance of the Twelve

The number twelve in the First Pledge of Aqabah was no coincidence. They represented various clans in Yathrib and, more importantly, included representatives from both of the great rival tribes — Aws and Khazraj. The presence of two men from Aws alongside ten from Khazraj showed that Islam was beginning to be accepted as a unifying identity that transcended tribal rivalry.^1 ^8

### A Moral Pledge as Foundation

It is significant that the contents of the First Pledge of Aqabah were moral and spiritual in nature, not military. The prohibitions against polytheism, theft, adultery, infanticide, and falsehood — these are all ethical foundations that must be built before political or military commitments. Prophet Muhammad SAW built society from the ground up: character first, then strength.^1 ^9

### Mus'ab as a Model of Preaching

The dispatch of Mus'ab ibn Umayr to Yathrib was a brilliant strategic decision. The Prophet did not send a general or politician, but rather a gentle teacher of the Qur'an. Mus'ab's success proved that sustainable social transformation begins with education and the changing of hearts, not through force or coercion.^2 ^0

In less than a year, Mus'ab managed to transform Yathrib from a city torn apart by tribal warfare into a base ready to receive the Prophet's emigration. This stands as one of the most impressive preaching achievements in early Islamic history.^2 ^1

### The Seeds of Hijrah and the Medinan State

The First Pledge of Aqabah was the seed that would eventually grow into the Hijrah (622 CE) and the establishment of the Medinan state — the first Islamic political community with a written constitution (the Charter of Medina). Without this first pledge — without the twelve individuals who dared to swear allegiance at night in the pass of Aqabah — there would have been no Second Pledge of Aqabah, no Hijrah, and the course of Islamic history would have been profoundly different.^2 ^2

## Bibliography

1. Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman, *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum [The Sealed Nectar]* (Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976), pp. 128–129.
2. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, vol. 1, ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa et al. (Cairo: Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955), pp. 422–423.
3. Ibn Kathir, *Al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah*, vol. 3 (Beirut: Dar al-Fikr, 1986), p. 139.
4. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, vol. 1, p. 424.
5. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, vol. 1, pp. 428–429.

10. Al-Bukhari, *Sahih al-Bukhari*, Kitab al-Iman, no. 18.

# Bai'at Aqabah Pertama — 12 Orang Madinah Bersumpah Setia (621 M)

## Konteks

Setelah *'Am al-Huzn* (Tahun Kesedihan) dan penolakan di Tha'if, **Nabi Muhammad SAW** menghadapi kenyataan bahwa dakwah di Makkah telah mencapai titik jenuh. Perlindungan kesukuan telah hilang dengan wafatnya **Abu Thalib**, dan **Abu Lahab** yang menggantikannya sebagai kepala Bani Hasyim justru mencabut perlindungan terhadap Nabi. Situasi di Makkah semakin tidak aman bagi beliau dan para pengikutnya.^2

Dalam kondisi ini, Nabi Muhammad SAW mulai mengubah strategi dakwahnya. Beliau memanfaatkan musim haji — ketika orang-orang dari seluruh Jazirah Arab berdatangan ke Makkah — untuk menawarkan Islam kepada berbagai delegasi suku. Beliau mendatangi kemah-kemah para jamaah haji, memperkenalkan diri, membacakan Al-Qur'an, dan mengajak mereka beriman. Sebagian besar menolak, bahkan mengusir beliau. Namun, beliau tidak pernah berhenti mencoba.^1

Kota **Yatsrib** (yang kelak menjadi Madinah) memiliki kondisi sosial-politik yang unik. Kota ini dihuni oleh dua suku Arab besar — **Aus** dan **Khazraj** — yang telah bermusuhan selama beberapa generasi. Perang terakhir mereka, Perang Bu'ats (617 M), telah menguras kedua belah pihak dan menciptakan kerinduan akan perdamaian dan kepemimpinan yang bisa menyatukan mereka.^3

Selain itu, penduduk Yatsrib sudah akrab dengan konsep kenabian dan kitab suci melalui interaksi mereka dengan tiga suku Yahudi yang tinggal di kota tersebut — **Bani Qainuqa'**, **Bani Nadhir**, dan **Bani Quraizhah**. Orang-orang Yahudi sering menyebut-nyebut akan datangnya seorang nabi terakhir, dan hal ini membuat penduduk Arab Yatsrib lebih terbuka terhadap gagasan kenabian dibanding suku-suku Arab lainnya.^1

## Peristiwa

### Pertemuan Awal di Musim Haji (620 M)

Setahun sebelum Bai'at Aqabah Pertama, pada musim haji tahun kesebelas kenabian (620 M), Nabi Muhammad SAW bertemu dengan sekelompok kecil dari suku Khazraj di dekat Aqabah. Mereka berjumlah enam orang: **As'ad bin Zurarah**, **Auf bin al-Harits**, **Rafi' bin Malik**, **Quthbah bin Amir**, **Uqbah bin Amir**, dan **Jabir bin Abdullah bin Ri'ab**.^1

Ketika Nabi membacakan Al-Qur'an dan menjelaskan Islam kepada mereka, mereka saling berpandangan dan berkata: "Demi Allah, ini adalah nabi yang dijanjikan oleh orang-orang Yahudi. Jangan sampai mereka mendahului kita untuk beriman kepadanya!"^1

Keenam orang ini memeluk Islam dan kembali ke Yatsrib dengan semangat yang membara. Mereka mulai menyebarkan Islam di kota mereka, sehingga nyaris tidak ada rumah di Yatsrib yang tidak membicarakan tentang Muhammad.^2

### Bai'at Aqabah Pertama (621 M)

Pada musim haji berikutnya, tahun kedua belas kenabian (621 M), datanglah dua belas orang dari Yatsrib menemui Nabi Muhammad SAW di Aqabah — sebuah lembah sempit antara Mina dan Makkah yang menjadi lokasi pelemparan jumrah. Dari dua belas orang ini, sepuluh berasal dari suku Khazraj dan dua dari suku Aus — menandai persatuan awal kedua suku yang selama ini bermusuhan.^1

Kedua belas orang tersebut adalah:^4

**Dari suku Khazraj:**
1. **As'ad bin Zurarah** (juga hadir pada pertemuan tahun sebelumnya)
2. **Auf bin al-Harits bin Rifa'ah**
3. **Rafi' bin Malik bin al-Ajlan**
4. **Quthbah bin Amir bin Hadidah**
5. **Uqbah bin Amir bin Nabi**
6. **Jabir bin Abdullah bin Ri'ab**
7. **Mu'adz bin al-Harits** (saudara Auf)
8. **Dzakwan bin Abd Qais**
9. **Ubadah bin al-Shamit**
10. **Yazid bin Tsa'labah**

**Dari suku Aus:**
11. **Abu al-Haitsam bin al-Tayyihan**
12. **Uwaim bin Sa'idah**

### Isi Bai'at

**Ubadah bin al-Shamit** meriwayatkan isi bai'at yang mereka ucapkan kepada Nabi Muhammad SAW. Bai'at ini dikenal juga sebagai *Bai'at al-Nisa'* (Bai'at Perempuan) karena isinya sama dengan bai'at yang kelak diminta dari kaum perempuan — yakni bai'at moral dan spiritual tanpa komitmen militer:^5

Isi bai'at tersebut mencakup:

1. **Tidak menyekutukan Allah** dengan sesuatu apapun
2. **Tidak mencuri**
3. **Tidak berzina**
4. **Tidak membunuh anak-anak** mereka (tradisi mengubur bayi perempuan hidup-hidup)
5. **Tidak membuat kebohongan** yang mereka ada-adakan antara tangan dan kaki mereka (tuduhan palsu)
6. **Tidak mendurhakai** Rasulullah dalam perkara yang baik (*ma'ruf*)

Nabi Muhammad SAW kemudian bersabda:

"Barang siapa di antara kalian memenuhi janjinya, maka pahalanya di sisi Allah. Dan barang siapa melanggar sesuatu dari itu lalu dihukum di dunia, maka hukuman itu menjadi penebus dosanya. Dan barang siapa melanggar sesuatu dari itu lalu Allah menutupinya (di dunia), maka urusannya terserah Allah — jika Dia berkehendak, Dia mengampuninya, dan jika Dia berkehendak, Dia menghukumnya."^5

### Pengutusan Mush'ab bin Umair

Setelah bai'at, Nabi Muhammad SAW mengutus **Mush'ab bin Umair** ke Yatsrib sebagai duta dan pengajar Islam yang pertama. Mush'ab adalah seorang pemuda Quraisy yang dulunya dikenal karena kemewahan hidupnya, namun meninggalkan segalanya demi Islam. Beliau tinggal di rumah As'ad bin Zurarah dan mulai mengajarkan Al-Qur'an serta ajaran Islam.^1

Mush'ab membuktikan diri sebagai dai yang sangat efektif. Dengan kebijaksanaan dan kelembutan pendekatannya, ia berhasil mengislamkan tokoh-tokoh penting Yatsrib, termasuk **Sa'd bin Mu'adz**, pemimpin suku Aus, dan **Usaid bin Hudhair**, yang keislamannya menjadi titik balik bagi seluruh suku Aus.^1

Proses Islamisasi Yatsrib melalui Mush'ab ini berlangsung selama setahun, dan hasilnya sangat signifikan: pada musim haji berikutnya (622 M), datanglah tujuh puluh tiga pria dan dua perempuan dari Yatsrib untuk melaksanakan Bai'at Aqabah Kedua — bai'at yang kali ini mencakup komitmen militer untuk melindungi Nabi — yang membuka jalan bagi peristiwa Hijrah.^2

## Dialog dan Dalil

Peristiwa Bai'at Aqabah Pertama merupakan titik balik strategis dalam sejarah dakwah Islam. Dalil yang terkait dengan peristiwa ini terutama berasal dari riwayat hadits.

Ubadah bin al-Shamit meriwayatkan tentang bai'at ini:

> بَايَعْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَلَى أَنْ لاَ نُشْرِكَ بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَلاَ نَسْرِقَ وَلاَ نَزْنِيَ وَلاَ نَقْتُلَ أَوْلاَدَنَا وَلاَ نَأْتِيَ بِبُهْتَانٍ نَفْتَرِيهِ بَيْنَ أَيْدِينَا وَأَرْجُلِنَا وَلاَ نَعْصِيَهُ فِي مَعْرُوفٍ
>
> *"Kami membai'at Rasulullah SAW untuk tidak menyekutukan Allah dengan sesuatu apapun, tidak mencuri, tidak berzina, tidak membunuh anak-anak kami, tidak mendatangkan kebohongan yang kami ada-adakan antara tangan dan kaki kami, dan tidak mendurhakai beliau dalam perkara yang baik. ﴾23﴿"*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 18, Kitab al-Iman; juga HR Muslim, no. 1709

Terkait dengan pembukaan jalan dakwah melalui orang-orang baru, Allah SWT berfirman:

> وَلَوْ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا لَّأَسْمَعَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ لَتَوَلَّوا وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ ﴿٢٣﴾
>
> *"Kalau sekiranya Allah mengetahui kebaikan ada pada mereka, tentu Allah menjadikan mereka dapat mendengar. Dan kalau Allah menjadikan mereka dapat mendengar, niscaya mereka pasti berpaling, dan mereka memalingkan diri. ﴾23﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Anfal (8): 23

Ayat ini, meskipun konteks turunnya berkaitan dengan peristiwa Badar, memiliki relevansi dengan dinamika dakwah di Makkah — di mana banyak penduduk Makkah yang mendengar dakwah namun berpaling, sementara orang-orang Yatsrib yang baru mendengar justru menerimanya dengan sepenuh hati.^6

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Pergeseran Strategi Dakwah

Bai'at Aqabah Pertama menandai pergeseran fundamental dalam strategi dakwah Islam — dari pendekatan lokal di Makkah menuju ekspansi regional. Nabi Muhammad SAW menunjukkan kecerdasan strategis yang luar biasa: ketika satu pintu tertutup (Makkah), beliau membuka pintu lain (Yatsrib). Pendekatan ini mencerminkan prinsip Qur'ani bahwa pertolongan Allah bisa datang dari arah yang tidak terduga.^2

### Diplomasi Musim Haji

Pemanfaatan musim haji sebagai arena dakwah menunjukkan pemahaman Nabi terhadap dinamika sosial-politik Arabia. Musim haji adalah satu-satunya waktu ketika norma kesukuan melarang kekerasan dan memungkinkan komunikasi lintas suku secara aman. Nabi memanfaatkan "zona aman" ini secara maksimal — sebuah contoh awal diplomasi religius yang memanfaatkan ruang publik yang sudah ada.^3

### Signifikansi Dua Belas Orang

Jumlah dua belas orang dalam Bai'at Aqabah Pertama bukan angka yang kebetulan. Mereka mewakili berbagai kabilah di Yatsrib dan, yang lebih penting, mencakup perwakilan dari kedua suku besar yang bermusuhan — Aus dan Khazraj. Kehadiran dua orang dari suku Aus di samping sepuluh orang dari suku Khazraj menunjukkan bahwa Islam mulai diterima sebagai identitas pemersatu yang melampaui rivalitas kesukuan.^1

### Bai'at Moral sebagai Fondasi

Penting untuk dicatat bahwa isi Bai'at Aqabah Pertama bersifat moral dan spiritual, bukan militer. Larangan syirik, pencurian, zina, pembunuhan anak, dan kebohongan — ini semua adalah fondasi etika yang harus dibangun sebelum komitmen politik atau militer. Nabi Muhammad SAW membangun masyarakat dari bawah ke atas: pertama karakter, baru kemudian kekuatan.^2

### Mush'ab sebagai Model Dakwah

Pengutusan Mush'ab bin Umair ke Yatsrib adalah keputusan strategis yang brilian. Nabi tidak mengirim seorang jenderal atau politisi, melainkan seorang pengajar Al-Qur'an yang lembut pendekatannya. Keberhasilan Mush'ab membuktikan bahwa transformasi sosial yang berkelanjutan dimulai dari pendidikan dan perubahan hati, bukan dari kekuatan atau paksaan.^1

Dalam waktu kurang dari setahun, Mush'ab berhasil mengubah Yatsrib dari sebuah kota yang terpecah oleh perang suku menjadi basis yang siap menerima hijrah Nabi. Ini merupakan salah satu keberhasilan dakwah paling mengesankan dalam sejarah Islam awal.^3

### Benih Hijrah dan Negara Madinah

Bai'at Aqabah Pertama adalah benih yang kelak tumbuh menjadi peristiwa Hijrah (622 M) dan berdirinya negara Madinah — komunitas politik Islam pertama yang memiliki konstitusi tertulis (Piagam Madinah). Tanpa bai'at pertama ini — tanpa kedua belas orang yang berani mengucapkan sumpah setia di malam hari di lembah Aqabah — tidak akan ada Bai'at Aqabah Kedua, tidak akan ada hijrah, dan sejarah Islam akan berjalan sangat berbeda.^2

## Daftar Pustaka

1. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, jilid 1, tahqiq Musthafa al-Saqqa dkk. (Kairo: Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
2. Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman, *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976).
3. Ibn Kathir, *Al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah*, jilid 3 (Beirut: Dar al-Fikr, 1986).
4. Ibn Sa'd, *Al-Thabaqat al-Kubra*, jilid 3 (Beirut: Dar Shadir, 1968).
5. Al-Bukhari, *Shahih al-Bukhari*.
6. Al-Thabari, *Tafsir al-Thabari* (Kairo: Dar al-Ma'arif, 1954).

# The Second Pledge of Aqabah 🤝

---

## The Delegates from Madinah

A year earlier, twelve people from the city of Madinah had met Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and accepted Islam. They went home with a teacher named **Mus'ab ibn Umair** to teach Islam in Madinah.

Mus'ab was so good at sharing the message that Islam spread everywhere in Madinah. One by one, tribal leaders began to accept Islam. When the hajj season came, **75 Muslims from Madinah** — 73 men and 2 women — set out for Makkah to meet the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in person.

🎨 *Illustration: A large caravan from Madinah walking through the desert toward Makkah. Setting: a desert trail under a sunset sky. Characters: men and women in simple clothes with determined faces. Key objects: walking sticks, travel provisions. Dominant colours: sunset orange, earthy brown. Mood: hopeful and brave. Composition: the caravan stretching from the foreground to the horizon.*

---

## A Secret Meeting on the Hill of Aqabah

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ arranged a very secret meeting. The place was the pass of the **Hill of Aqabah** near Mina, the time was midnight — so that the Quraysh would not find out.

The Muslims from Madinah crept out of their tents very carefully, "like birds creeping slowly," said Ka'b ibn Malik who was there. One by one they gathered at the dark mountain pass.

The Prophet came with his uncle **al-Abbas**, who had not yet accepted Islam but wanted to make sure his nephew was safe. Al-Abbas told the people of Madinah: "If you are sure you can protect Muhammad, go ahead. But if not, leave him now."

🎨 *Illustration: A group of people gathered in a dark mountain pass under starlight. Setting: a rocky pass in the Hill of Aqabah, nighttime. Characters: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (without face) in the centre, al-Abbas beside him, surrounded by dozens of people. Key objects: large boulders, starry sky. Dominant colours: deep night blue, silver starlight. Mood: solemn and secretive. Composition: a circle of people surrounding the Prophet between rock walls.*

---

## A Brave Promise

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ said: "I ask you to protect me as you protect your wives and children."

**Al-Bara' ibn Ma'rur**, the eldest leader, immediately reached out his hand: "We will protect you as we protect ourselves!"

Then someone asked: "O Messenger of Allah, if Allah gives you victory later, will you leave us?" The Prophet smiled and answered: "No. My blood is your blood. I am part of you."

Another man stood up and reminded his : "this means? It means we are ready to fight everyone who opposes Muhammad. If you cannot handle that, step back now!"

They answered together: "We are ready! What do we get, O Messenger of Allah?"

The Prophet answered: **"Paradise."**

One by one, all 75 people came forward and shook hands with the Prophet as a sign of their loyal pledge.

🎨 *Illustration: Someone shaking hands with the Prophet (shown from behind/without face). Setting: rocky pass at night with moonlight. Characters: hands clasping, faces full of conviction. Key objects: a firm handshake. Dominant colours: soft gold, night blue. Mood: solemn and deeply committed. Composition: focus on the handshake in the centre, a queue of people behind.*

---

## Twelve Leaders

After the pledge, the Prophet chose **12 leaders** called *naqib* — 9 from the Khazraj tribe and 3 from the Aws tribe. Their job was to lead and look after the Muslims in Madinah.

The Prophet told them: "You are responsible for your people, just like the loyal followers of Prophet Isa (Jesus)."

The Second Pledge of Aqabah was incredibly important because it was the **beginning of the hijrah** — the migration of Muslims from Makkah to Madinah. Without the loyal promise of these 75 brave people, the hijrah would never have happened!

**An important lesson:** The courage and loyalty of the people of Madinah teach us that any great cause needs **bravery, honesty, and commitment**. They were not promised wealth or power — only Paradise. And that was enough for them. 🌟

🎨 *Illustration: Twelve people standing tall before the Prophet as dawn begins to break. Setting: the pass of Aqabah with a brightening sky. Characters: 12 naqib standing upright with a sense of duty, the Prophet (from behind) facing them. Key objects: the first rays of dawn. Dominant colours: dawn purple, sunrise gold. Mood: hopeful and responsible. Composition: the naqib lined up facing the Prophet, dawn sky in the background.*

# Bai'at Aqabah Kedua 🤝

---

## Utusan dari Madinah

Setahun sebelumnya, dua belas orang dari kota Madinah datang menemui Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan masuk Islam. Mereka pulang membawa seorang guru bernama **Mush'ab bin Umair** untuk mengajarkan Islam di Madinah.

Mush'ab begitu pandai menyampaikan dakwah sehingga Islam tersebar ke mana-mana di Madinah. Satu per satu, para pemimpin suku mulai memeluk Islam. Saat musim haji tiba, **75 orang muslim Madinah** — 73 laki-laki dan 2 perempuan — berangkat ke Makkah untuk menemui Rasulullah ﷺ secara langsung.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Rombongan besar dari Madinah berjalan melewati padang pasir menuju Makkah. Setting: jalan setapak padang pasir di bawah langit senja. Karakter: rombongan laki-laki dan perempuan berpakaian sederhana dengan wajah penuh tekad. Objek kunci: tongkat jalan, bekal perjalanan. Warna dominan: jingga senja, cokelat tanah. Mood: penuh harapan dan keberanian. Komposisi: rombongan membentang dari latar depan ke cakrawala.*

---

## Pertemuan Rahasia di Bukit Aqabah

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengatur pertemuan yang sangat rahasia. Tempatnya di celah **Bukit Aqabah** dekat Mina, waktunya tengah malam — supaya orang-orang Quraisy tidak mengetahui.

Para muslim Madinah menyelinap keluar dari tenda-tenda mereka dengan sangat hati-hati, "seperti burung yang merayap pelan," kata Ka'b bin Malik yang ikut hadir. Satu per satu mereka berkumpul di celah bukit yang gelap.

Nabi datang bersama pamannya **al-Abbas**, yang belum masuk Islam tapi ingin memastikan keponakannya aman. Al-Abbas berkata kepada orang-orang Madinah: "Jika kalian yakin bisa melindungi Muhammad, silakan. Tapi jika tidak, tinggalkanlah sekarang."

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Sekelompok orang berkumpul di celah bukit gelap di bawah cahaya bintang. Setting: celah batu di bukit Aqabah, malam hari. Karakter: Nabi Muhammad ﷺ (tanpa wajah) di tengah, al-Abbas di sampingnya, dikelilingi puluhan orang. Objek kunci: batu-batu besar, langit berbintang. Warna dominan: biru tua malam, perak bintang. Mood: khidmat dan rahasia. Komposisi: lingkaran orang mengelilingi Nabi di antara dinding batu.*

---

## Janji yang Berani

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ berkata: "Aku meminta kalian melindungiku sebagaimana kalian melindungi istri dan anak-anak kalian."

**Al-Bara' bin Ma'rur**, pemimpin tertua, langsung mengulurkan tangan: "Kami akan melindungimu seperti kami melindungi diri kami sendiri!"

Lalu ada yang bertanya: "Wahai Rasulullah, kalau Allah memenangkanmu nanti, apakah engkau akan meninggalkan kami?" Nabi tersenyum dan menjawab: "Tidak. Darahku adalah darah kalian. Aku bagian dari kalian."

Seseorang berdiri dan mengingatkan nya: "Tahukah kalian, ini artinya kita siap berperang melawan seluruh orang yang memusuhi Muhammad. Kalau tidak sanggup, mundur sekarang!"

Mereka menjawab serentak: "Kami siap! Apa yang kami dapat, wahai Rasulullah?"

Nabi menjawab: **"Surga."**

Satu per satu, 75 orang itu maju dan berjabat tangan dengan Nabi sebagai tanda janji setia.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Seseorang berjabat tangan dengan Nabi (ditampilkan dari belakang/tanpa wajah). Setting: celah batu malam hari dengan cahaya bulan. Karakter: tangan-tangan berjabat, wajah-wajah penuh keyakinan. Objek kunci: jabat tangan erat. Warna dominan: keemasan lembut, biru malam. Mood: khidmat dan penuh komitmen. Komposisi: fokus pada jabat tangan di tengah, antrian orang di belakang.*

---

## Dua Belas Pemimpin

Setelah bai'at, Nabi memilih **12 orang pemimpin** yang disebut *naqib* — 9 dari suku Khazraj dan 3 dari suku Aus. Mereka bertugas memimpin dan menjaga kaum muslimin di Madinah.

Nabi berkata kepada mereka: "Kalian penanggung jawab kaum kalian, seperti para pengikut setia Nabi Isa."

Bai'at Aqabah Kedua ini sangat penting karena menjadi **awal mula hijrah** — perpindahan kaum muslimin dari Makkah ke Madinah. Tanpa janji setia 75 orang pemberani ini, hijrah tidak akan pernah terjadi!

**Pelajaran penting:** Keberanian dan kesetiaan orang-orang Madinah mengajarkan kita bahwa perjuangan membutuhkan **keberanian, kejujuran, dan komitmen**. Mereka tidak dijanjikan harta atau kekuasaan — hanya surga. Dan itu sudah cukup bagi mereka. 🌟

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Dua belas orang berdiri tegap di hadapan Nabi saat fajar mulai menyingsing. Setting: celah Aqabah dengan langit mulai terang. Karakter: 12 naqib berdiri tegak dengan penuh tanggung jawab, Nabi (dari belakang) di hadapan mereka. Objek kunci: jari-jari fajar di langit. Warna dominan: ungu fajar, emas matahari terbit. Mood: penuh harapan dan tanggung jawab. Komposisi: naqib berjajar menghadap Nabi, langit fajar di latar belakang.*

# The Second Pledge of Aqabah: 75 Delegates from Madinah and the Birth of an Islamic State

## Context: From the First to the Second Aqabah

A year before the Second Pledge of Aqabah, twelve men from Yathrib (Madinah) had met Prophet Muhammad ﷺ at the hill of Aqabah and declared their Islam in what became known as the First Pledge of Aqabah.^1 They returned to Madinah accompanied by **Mus'ab ibn Umair**, the Prophet's envoy tasked with teaching Islam and the Quran to the city's inhabitants.^1 ^2

Mus'ab fulfilled his mission brilliantly. In less than a year, Islam spread rapidly among the Aws and Khazraj — two major Arab tribes in Madinah that had been warring against each other for generations. Ibn Hisham records that hardly a household among the Ansar remained untouched by the Islamic call.^1 Key figures such as **Sa'd ibn Mu'adh**, chief of the Banu Abd al-Ashhal of the Aws, and **Usayd ibn Hudayr** embraced Islam through Mus'ab's efforts — bringing their entire clans into the faith.^1 ^2

This success set the stage for a momentous event at the next hajj season. That year, the pilgrimage caravan from Madinah was far larger than usual — among them were 73 men and 2 women who had embraced Islam and were determined to meet Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in person.^1 ^2 ^3

## The Event: A Secret Meeting at Midnight

The meeting was planned with meticulous care. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ specified the time and place: **the middle of the nights of Tashriq** (the days following the standing at Arafah), at the pass of Aqabah in Mina.^1 Absolute secrecy was essential, for the Quraysh would have reacted violently had they learned that the people of another city intended to offer Muhammad protection.

Ibn Hisham narrates in great detail from Ka'b ibn Malik, one of the pledge participants.^1 Ka'b recounts that they slept among their polytheist fellow-pilgrims that night. When the first third of the night had passed, they slipped out one by one, "creeping like *qata* birds," toward the pass of Aqabah. They gathered there and waited for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ arrived accompanied by his uncle, **al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib**, who had not yet embraced Islam but wished to ensure his nephew's safety.^1 ^2 Al-Abbas spoke first. He said:

*"O people of Khazraj" — the Quraysh referred to all Arab inhabitants of Madinah as 'Khazraj' — "Muhammad holds a position among his people as you well know. We have protected him from those who oppose him. He enjoys honour among his people and security in his land. Yet he has chosen to join you. If you are confident that you can fulfil what you have promised him and protect him from his enemies, then that responsibility is yours. But if you think you will surrender him and betray him after he comes to you, then leave him now."*^1

## Dialogue and Covenant: A Binding Pledge

After al-Abbas finished, the delegates from Madinah asked Prophet Muhammad ﷺ himself to speak. He recited verses from the Quran, called upon Allah, and encouraged them toward Islam.^2 Then he outlined the terms of the pledge:

*"I take your pledge on the basis that you will protect me as you protect your wives and children."*^1

**Al-Bara' ibn Ma'rur** — the eldest leader among the delegation — immediately extended his hand and declared: *"Yes! By the One who sent you with the truth, we shall protect you as we protect ourselves. Accept our pledge, O Messenger of Allah. We are people of war and people of arms — we have inherited this from generation to generation."*^1

While al-Bara' was still speaking, **Abu al-Haytham ibn al-Tayyihan** interjected with a question that revealed his political acumen: *"O Messenger of Allah, there are ties between us and those people — meaning the Jews of Madinah — and we shall sever them. If we do so, and then Allah grants you victory, will you return to your people and leave us?"*^1

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ smiled and answered:

*"No. Your blood is my blood, your honour is my honour. I am part of you and you are part of me. I fight those you fight and make peace with those you make peace with."*^1 ^2

Then **al-Abbas ibn Ubadah ibn Nadlah** stood and addressed his own people — ensuring they fully understood the weight of their decision: *"O people of Khazraj, do you know upon what basis you are pledging allegiance to this man? You are pledging to fight all of mankind — red and black alike. If you think that when your wealth is destroyed by calamity and your leaders are killed, you will hand him over, then leave him now. By Allah, if you do that, it will be disgrace in this world and the next. But if you are certain you will fulfil what you have promised even though your wealth is destroyed and your leaders are killed, then take him. By Allah, that is the good of this world and the hereafter."*^1

They answered in unison: *"We accept him even though our wealth is destroyed and our leaders are killed. What do we receive if we fulfil this pledge, O Messenger of Allah?"*

He replied: *"Paradise."*^1

One by one, all seventy-five came forward and clasped the Prophet's hand, declaring their pledge.

## The Appointment of Twelve *Naqib*

After the pledge was complete, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ asked them to choose twelve *naqib* (leaders/guarantors) — nine from the Khazraj and three from the Aws — to serve as his representatives in Madinah.^1 ^2 ^3 These *naqib* were responsible for their respective communities, ensuring the commitments of the pledge were upheld.

Among the *naqib* were:
- **As'ad ibn Zurarah** (Banu al-Najjar, Khazraj)
- **Sa'd ibn al-Rabi'** (Banu al-Harith, Khazraj)
- **Abdullah ibn Rawahah** (Banu al-Harith, Khazraj)
- **Sa'd ibn Ubadah** (Banu Sa'idah, Khazraj)
- **Usayd ibn Hudayr** (Banu Abd al-Ashhal, Aws)
- **Sa'd ibn Khaythamah** (Banu Abd al-Ashhal, Aws)^1 ^3

The Prophet told them: *"You are the guarantors for your people, just as the disciples (hawariyyun) were for Isa ibn Maryam. And I am the guarantor for my people."*^1

## The Quraysh Reaction: Suspicion Too Late

The secrecy of the meeting was not entirely maintained. Ibn Hisham narrates that a devil cried out from the summit of Aqabah, warning the people of Mina about "Muhammad and the *shuba'* (those who have abandoned the religion of their forefathers) who have gathered to wage war against you!"^1

Al-Abbas ibn Ubadah immediately offered: *"O Messenger of Allah, by the One who sent you with the truth, if you wish, tomorrow we will fall upon the people of Mina with our swords!"* The Prophet replied firmly: *"We have not been commanded to do that. Return to your places."*^1

The next morning, a Quraysh delegation visited the Madinah pilgrimage camp and asked directly whether they had pledged allegiance to Muhammad. The polytheists from Madinah — who genuinely knew nothing of the night's events — swore that no such thing had happened. The Muslims from Madinah remained silent, and the honest polytheists unwittingly shielded them.^1 ^2

## Analysis: Political and Strategic Significance

The Second Pledge of Aqabah was not merely a religious oath of loyalty — it was a **political and military pact** that fundamentally altered the balance of power in the Arabian Peninsula.

**First, a new political legitimacy.** For the first time, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ received a guarantee of political protection from an entity outside Makkah. During thirteen years of preaching in Makkah, he had depended on clan-based protection — first Abu Talib, then the Banu Hashim. This pledge provided protection based on ideology, not tribal kinship. It was a paradigmatic leap in the concept of Arab loyalty.

**Second, a constitutional framework.** The appointment of twelve *naqib* demonstrated that the Prophet was building not only a spiritual community but also a governance structure. The *naqib* functioned as regional and tribal representatives — an early form of political representation in Islam.

**Third, extraordinary courage from Madinah.** By pledging allegiance to Muhammad, the people of Madinah consciously placed themselves in opposition to the entire Quraysh — the most powerful tribe in the Arabian Peninsula, custodians of the Ka'bah, and controllers of the north-south trade route. This was no impulsive decision; the dialogues show they fully understood the consequences.

**Fourth, the opening of the path to hijrah.** Without the Second Pledge of Aqabah, the emigration to Madinah would have been impossible. This pledge was the political prerequisite enabling the mass migration of Muslims from Makkah to Madinah with a guarantee of security.

## Lessons: Enduring Wisdom

The Second Pledge of Aqabah teaches several fundamental principles:

1. **Planning and secrecy.** Prophet Muhammad ﷺ did not act haphazardly. The time, place, and procedure of the meeting were meticulously planned. Faith does not negate strategic planning.

2. **Transparency in covenants.** Both al-Abbas and al-Abbas ibn Ubadah ibn Nadlah ensured that every participant understood the full consequences of the pledge. No one was deceived or caught off-guard.

3. **Courage grounded in principle.** When asked what their reward would be, the Prophet did not promise wealth, power, or land. He answered: *"Paradise."* And they accepted.

4. **Collective leadership.** The appointment of *naqib* shows that Islam does not mandate absolute one-person rule but rather distributed responsibility.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (The Prophetic Biography). Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safi al-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Vol. 1. Beirut: Dar Sadir.
e14-baiat-aqabah-kedua.general-id:
# Bai'at Aqabah Kedua: 75 Utusan Madinah dan Lahirnya Negara Islam

## Konteks: Dari Aqabah Pertama ke Kedua

Setahun sebelum Bai'at Aqabah Kedua, dua belas orang dari Yatsrib (Madinah) telah menemui Nabi Muhammad ﷺ di bukit Aqabah dan menyatakan keislaman mereka dalam peristiwa yang dikenal sebagai Bai'at Aqabah Pertama.^1 Mereka pulang ke Madinah membawa serta **Mush'ab bin Umair**, utusan Nabi yang ditugaskan mengajarkan Islam dan al-Quran kepada penduduk kota itu.^1 ^2

Mush'ab menunaikan tugasnya dengan cemerlang. Dalam waktu kurang dari setahun, Islam menyebar dengan pesat di kalangan suku Aus dan Khazraj — dua suku besar Arab di Madinah yang selama beberapa generasi saling berperang. Ibn Hisham mencatat bahwa hampir tidak ada rumah di kalangan Anshar yang tidak tersentuh dakwah Islam.^1 Figur-figur kunci seperti **Sa'd bin Mu'adz**, pemimpin Bani Abdul Asyhal dari suku Aus, dan **Usaid bin Hudhair** memeluk Islam melalui dakwah Mush'ab — membawa seluruh klan mereka masuk Islam.^1 ^2

Keberhasilan ini menjadi latar belakang peristiwa besar yang akan terjadi pada musim haji berikutnya. Tahun itu, rombongan haji dari Madinah berjumlah jauh lebih besar dari biasanya — di antara mereka terdapat 73 orang laki-laki dan 2 orang perempuan yang memeluk Islam dan bertekad menemui Nabi Muhammad ﷺ secara langsung.^1 ^2 ^3

## Peristiwa: Pertemuan Rahasia di Tengah Malam

Pertemuan ini direncanakan dengan sangat hati-hati. Nabi Muhammad ﷺ menentukan waktu dan tempat: **pertengahan malam-malam Tasyriq** (hari-hari setelah wuquf di Arafah), di celah bukit Aqabah, Mina.^1 Kerahasiaan mutlak diperlukan karena kaum Quraisy akan bereaksi keras jika mengetahui bahwa penduduk kota lain hendak memberikan perlindungan kepada Muhammad dan pengikutnya.

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan dari Ka'b bin Malik, salah satu peserta bai'at, dengan sangat rinci.^1 Ka'b menceritakan bahwa malam itu mereka tidur bersama rombongan haji mereka yang masih musyrik. Ketika sepertiga malam pertama berlalu, mereka menyelinap keluar satu per satu, "bagaikan burung-burung *qatha* yang merayap perlahan," menuju celah Aqabah. Mereka berkumpul di sana dan menunggu Rasulullah ﷺ.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ datang ditemani pamannya, **al-Abbas bin Abdul Muthalib**, yang saat itu belum memeluk Islam tetapi ingin memastikan keselamatan keponakannya.^1 ^2 Al-Abbas berbicara lebih dulu. Ia berkata:

*"Wahai kaum Khazraj" — orang-orang Quraisy menyebut seluruh penduduk Arab Madinah sebagai 'Khazraj' — "Muhammad memiliki kedudukan di tengah kaumnya sebagaimana kalian ketahui. Kami telah melindunginya dari mereka yang menentangnya. Ia berada dalam kemuliaan di tengah kaumnya dan perlindungan di negerinya. Namun ia memilih untuk bergabung dengan kalian. Maka jika kalian yakin mampu memenuhi apa yang kalian janjikan kepadanya dan melindunginya dari musuh-musuhnya, maka tanggung jawab itu ada pada kalian. Tetapi jika kalian merasa akan menyerahkannya dan mengkhianatinya setelah ia datang kepada kalian, maka tinggalkanlah ia sekarang."*^1

## Dialog dan Dalil: Perjanjian yang Mengikat

Setelah al-Abbas selesai, para utusan Madinah meminta Nabi Muhammad ﷺ sendiri yang berbicara. Beliau membacakan beberapa ayat al-Quran, menyerukan kepada Allah, dan mendorong mereka kepada Islam.^2 Kemudian beliau menguraikan syarat-syarat bai'at:

*"Aku membai'at kalian atas dasar: kalian melindungiku sebagaimana kalian melindungi istri-istri dan anak-anak kalian."*^1

**Al-Bara' bin Ma'rur** — pemimpin tertua di antara rombongan — segera mengulurkan tangannya dan berkata: *"Ya! Demi Dzat yang mengutusmu dengan kebenaran, kami akan melindungimu sebagaimana kami melindungi diri kami sendiri. Maka terimalah bai'at kami, wahai Rasulullah. Kami adalah ahli perang dan ahli senjata, kami mewarisinya turun-temurun."*^1

Ketika al-Bara' masih berbicara, **Abu al-Haitsam bin al-Tayyihan** menyela dengan pertanyaan yang menunjukkan ketajaman politiknya: *"Wahai Rasulullah, sesungguhnya antara kami dan kaum itu — yaitu Yahudi Madinah — terdapat tali-tali perjanjian, dan kami akan memutuskannya. Apakah jika kami melakukan itu, lalu Allah memenangkanmu, engkau akan kembali kepada kaummu dan meninggalkan kami?"*^1

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ tersenyum dan menjawab:

*"Tidak. Darah kalian adalah darahku, kehormatan kalian adalah kehormatanku. Aku bagian dari kalian dan kalian bagian dariku. Aku berperang melawan siapa yang kalian perangi, dan aku berdamai dengan siapa yang kalian ajak damai."*^1 ^2

Lalu **al-Abbas bin Ubadah bin Nadhlah** berdiri dan berkata kepada kaumnya sendiri — memastikan mereka memahami bobot keputusan ini: *"Wahai kaum Khazraj, tahukah kalian atas dasar apa kalian membai'at orang ini? Kalian membai'atnya atas dasar berperang melawan seluruh manusia — merah dan hitam kulit mereka. Jika kalian berpikir bahwa ketika harta kalian habis oleh musibah dan pemuka-pemuka kalian terbunuh, kalian akan menyerahkannya, maka tinggalkanlah ia sekarang. Demi Allah, jika kalian melakukan itu, maka itu adalah kehinaan di dunia dan akhirat. Tetapi jika kalian yakin akan memenuhi apa yang kalian janjikan meskipun harta kalian habis dan pemuka-pemuka kalian terbunuh, maka ambillah ia. Demi Allah, itulah kebaikan dunia dan akhirat."*^1

Mereka menjawab serentak: *"Kami mengambilnya meskipun harta kami habis dan pemuka-pemuka kami terbunuh. Apa yang kami dapatkan jika kami memenuhi janji ini, wahai Rasulullah?"*

Beliau menjawab: *"Surga."*^1

Satu per satu, ketujuh puluh lima orang itu maju dan berjabat tangan dengan Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, menyatakan bai'at mereka.

## Pengangkatan Dua Belas *Naqib*

Setelah bai'at selesai, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ meminta mereka memilih dua belas orang *naqib* (pemimpin/penanggung jawab) — sembilan dari Khazraj dan tiga dari Aus — sebagai wakil beliau di Madinah.^1 ^2 ^3 Para *naqib* ini bertanggung jawab atas kaum masing-masing, memastikan komitmen bai'at dijalankan.

Di antara para *naqib* tersebut adalah:
- **As'ad bin Zurarah** (Bani Najjar, Khazraj)
- **Sa'd bin al-Rabi'** (Bani al-Harits, Khazraj)
- **Abdullah bin Rawahah** (Bani al-Harits, Khazraj)
- **Sa'd bin Ubadah** (Bani Sa'idah, Khazraj)
- **Usaid bin Hudhair** (Bani Abdul Asyhal, Aus)
- **Sa'd bin Khaitshamah** (Bani Abdul Asyhal, Aus)^1 ^3

Nabi berkata kepada mereka: *"Kalian adalah penanggung jawab atas kaum kalian, sebagaimana para hawariyyun (pengikut setia) bagi Isa bin Maryam. Dan aku adalah penanggung jawab atas kaumku."*^1

## Reaksi Quraisy: Kecurigaan yang Terlambat

Kerahasiaan pertemuan itu tidak sepenuhnya berhasil. Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa seekor setan berteriak dari puncak Aqabah, memperingatkan penduduk Mina tentang "Muhammad dan orang-orang *shuba'* (yang telah meninggalkan agama nenek moyang) yang berkumpul untuk memerangi kalian!"^1

Al-Abbas bin Ubadah spontan menawarkan diri: *"Wahai Rasulullah, demi Dzat yang mengutusmu dengan kebenaran, jika engkau kehendaki, besok kami akan menyerang penduduk Mina dengan pedang-pedang kami!"* Nabi menjawab dengan tegas: *"Kami belum diperintahkan untuk itu. Kembalilah ke tempat-tempat kalian."*^1

Keesokan harinya, delegasi Quraisy mendatangi rombongan haji Madinah dan menanyakan langsung apakah mereka telah membai'at Muhammad. Para musyrikin dari Madinah — yang memang tidak mengetahui peristiwa malam itu — bersumpah bahwa tidak ada hal semacam itu terjadi. Kaum muslimin dari Madinah diam saja, dan para musyrikin dari Madinah yang jujur itu melindungi mereka tanpa sadar.^1 ^2

## Analisis: Signifikansi Politik dan Strategis

Bai'at Aqabah Kedua bukan sekadar sumpah setia keagamaan — ia adalah **perjanjian politik dan militer** yang secara fundamental mengubah peta kekuatan di Jazirah Arab.

**Pertama, legitimasi politik baru.** Untuk pertama kalinya, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mendapat jaminan perlindungan politik dari entitas di luar Makkah. Selama 13 tahun dakwah di Makkah, beliau bergantung pada perlindungan klan — Abu Thalib, lalu Bani Hasyim. Bai'at ini memberikan perlindungan berbasis ideologis, bukan kesukuan. Ini adalah lompatan paradigmatik dalam konsep loyalitas Arab.

**Kedua, kerangka konstitusional.** Pengangkatan dua belas *naqib* menunjukkan bahwa Nabi Muhammad ﷺ tidak hanya membangun komunitas spiritual, tetapi juga struktur pemerintahan. Para *naqib* berfungsi sebagai representasi wilayah dan suku — sebuah bentuk awal dari sistem perwakilan politik dalam Islam.

**Ketiga, keberanian luar biasa pihak Madinah.** Dengan membai'at Muhammad, penduduk Madinah secara sadar menempatkan diri mereka dalam posisi bermusuhan dengan seluruh Quraisy — suku paling berkuasa di Jazirah Arab, penjaga Ka'bah, dan pengendali jalur perdagangan utara-selatan. Keputusan ini bukan impulsif; dialog-dialog yang terjadi menunjukkan bahwa mereka sepenuhnya memahami konsekuensinya.

**Keempat, pembukaan jalan hijrah.** Tanpa Bai'at Aqabah Kedua, hijrah ke Madinah tidak mungkin terjadi. Bai'at ini adalah prasyarat politik yang memungkinkan perpindahan massal kaum muslimin dari Makkah ke Madinah dengan jaminan keamanan.

## Hikmah: Pelajaran Abadi

Bai'at Aqabah Kedua mengajarkan beberapa prinsip mendasar:

1. **Perencanaan dan kerahasiaan.** Nabi Muhammad ﷺ tidak bertindak sembarangan. Waktu, tempat, dan prosedur pertemuan direncanakan dengan cermat. Keimanan tidak meniadakan perencanaan strategis.

2. **Transparansi dalam perjanjian.** Baik al-Abbas maupun al-Abbas bin Ubadah bin Nadhlah memastikan bahwa seluruh peserta memahami konsekuensi penuh dari bai'at ini. Tidak ada yang tertipu atau terkejut.

3. **Keberanian berbasis prinsip.** Ketika ditanya apa imbalan bagi mereka, Nabi tidak menjanjikan kekayaan, kekuasaan, atau tanah. Beliau menjawab: *"Surga."* Dan mereka menerimanya.

4. **Sistem kepemimpinan kolektif.** Pengangkatan *naqib* menunjukkan bahwa Islam tidak menghendaki kepemimpinan satu orang yang absolut, melainkan tanggung jawab yang terdistribusi.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Sirah Nabawiyah). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Jilid 1. Beirut: Dar Shadir.

e15-hijrah-madinah.children-en:
# The Hijrah to Madinah 🌙

---

## The Evil Plan of the Quraysh

The Quraysh were furious that so many Muslims had moved to Madinah. They gathered and made an evil plan: to kill Prophet Muhammad ﷺ! Young men from every clan would attack the Prophet all at once.

But Allah told the Prophet about their plan. That night, the Prophet asked his cousin **Ali ibn Abi Talib** to sleep in his bed wearing his green cloak. The Quraysh youth surrounding the house thought the Prophet was still sleeping inside!

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ walked right past them **without being seen**, reciting verses of the Quran. Allah covered their eyes so that no one noticed the Prophet had left. Subhanallah!

🎨 *Illustration: A luminous figure of the Prophet (without face, glowing) walking past sleeping men outside a house. Setting: a simple Makkan house at night. Characters: Ali sleeping inside under a green cloak, Quraysh youth asleep outside. Key objects: an open door, moonlight. Dominant colours: night blue, soft golden glow. Mood: miraculous and divinely protected. Composition: the Prophet passing through the centre, sleeping youth on either side.*

---

## Hiding in the Cave of Thawr

The Prophet went to the home of his best friend, **Abu Bakr al-Siddiq**. They set off together — but not northward toward Madinah! Instead they went south, to the **Cave of Thawr**, so the Quraysh would search in the wrong direction. Very clever!

Inside the cave, Abu Bakr cleaned out every hole that might hide snakes. One hole was left, and Abu Bakr sealed it with **his own heel**. A snake bit his heel, but Abu Bakr didn't move because he didn't want to wake the Prophet sleeping in his lap. That's a true friend! 💪

The most thrilling moment came when the Quraysh trackers arrived at the cave entrance. Abu Bakr whispered in fear, but the Prophet said calmly: **"Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us."**

Allah protected them — the trackers never entered the cave!

🎨 *Illustration: Two figures inside a dark cave, with shadows of trackers' feet visible at the cave mouth. Setting: a small rocky cave with faint light from outside. Characters: the Prophet (without face) sitting calmly, Abu Bakr beside him looking worried but faithful. Key objects: a spider's web at the cave mouth, faint light from outside. Dominant colours: stone brown, faint gold. Mood: tense yet deeply calm. Composition: two figures inside the dark cave, silhouettes of people outside.*

---

## The Chaser Who Changed His Mind

After three days, the Prophet and Abu Bakr set off for Madinah along the coastal route. The Quraysh offered a reward of **100 camels** to anyone who captured the Prophet — that's a huge amount!

A skilled tracker named **Suraqah ibn Malik** chased after them. But every time his horse got close, it **sank into the sand**! Once, twice, three times — his horse kept sinking. Suraqah realised that a power was protecting the Prophet.

Suraqah stopped chasing and asked for forgiveness. The Prophet even promised him: "One day, you will wear the bracelets of the King of Persia!" Suraqah must have thought, "That's impossible!" But years later, that promise **actually came true**! 😲

🎨 *Illustration: A rider on a horse sinking into desert sand. Setting: a vast desert with a coastal trail visible. Characters: Suraqah on a horse stuck in sand, looking shocked. Key objects: the sinking horse, dust rising. Dominant colours: sand yellow, sky blue. Mood: dramatic and astonishing. Composition: Suraqah in the centre, two distant riders (the Prophet and Abu Bakr) in the background.*

---

## Welcome to Madinah!

The people of Madinah had been waiting for the Prophet every single day! They went out each morning to the edge of the city, waited until the sun got too hot, then went home and waited again the next day.

Finally, the day they were waiting for arrived! When Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was spotted in the distance, the whole of Madinah **erupted with joy**. The young girls of the Ansar sang: *"The full moon has risen upon us..."* 🎶

The Prophet built **Masjid Quba** — the very first mosque in Islam — in the first village he stopped at.

The hijrah was so important that it became the **start of the Islamic calendar** (the Hijri calendar). Every time we see a Hijri year, we remember this incredible journey!

**An important lesson:** The hijrah teaches us that **Allah always protects those who believe**, as long as we also try our very best. The Prophet planned everything carefully, then placed his trust in Allah. That is true *tawakkul*! 🌟

🎨 *Illustration: The people of Madinah joyfully welcoming the Prophet's arrival. Setting: the outskirts of Madinah with date palm trees. Characters: people cheering happily, children running, the Prophet (without face) on a camel. Key objects: date palms, Madinah houses. Dominant colours: fresh green, warm gold. Mood: joyful and grateful. Composition: the Prophet entering from the right, welcoming crowds on the left.*
e15-hijrah-madinah.children-id:
# Hijrah ke Madinah 🌙

---

## Rencana Jahat Orang Quraisy

Orang-orang Quraisy sangat marah karena banyak muslim yang pindah ke Madinah. Mereka berkumpul dan membuat rencana jahat: membunuh Nabi Muhammad ﷺ! Rencananya, pemuda dari setiap klan akan menyerang Nabi secara bersamaan.

Tapi Allah memberitahu Nabi tentang rencana ini. Malam itu, Nabi meminta sepupunya **Ali bin Abi Thalib** untuk tidur di tempat tidurnya dan memakai selimut hijau beliau. Para pemuda Quraisy yang mengepung rumah mengira Nabi masih tidur di dalam!

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ keluar melewati mereka **tanpa terlihat**, sambil membaca ayat al-Quran. Allah menutup mata mereka sehingga tidak seorang pun menyadari Nabi sudah pergi. Subhanallah!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Sosok Nabi (tanpa wajah, bercahaya) berjalan melewati orang-orang yang tertidur di depan rumah. Setting: rumah sederhana Makkah di malam hari. Karakter: Ali tertidur di dalam rumah berselimut hijau, pemuda Quraisy tertidur di luar. Objek kunci: pintu rumah terbuka, cahaya bulan. Warna dominan: biru malam, cahaya keemasan lembut. Mood: ajaib dan penuh perlindungan ilahi. Komposisi: Nabi melintas di tengah, pemuda tertidur di kiri-kanan.*

---

## Bersembunyi di Gua Tsur

Nabi pergi ke rumah sahabat terbaiknya, **Abu Bakr al-Shiddiq**. Mereka berdua berangkat bersama — tapi bukan ke arah Madinah di utara! Mereka justru pergi ke selatan, ke **Gua Tsur**, supaya orang Quraisy mencari ke arah yang salah. Cerdas sekali!

Di dalam gua, Abu Bakr membersihkan semua lubang yang mungkin ada ularnya. Satu lubang tersisa, dan Abu Bakr menutupnya dengan **tumitnya sendiri**. Seekor ular menggigit tumitnya, tapi Abu Bakr tidak bergerak karena tidak mau membangunkan Nabi yang tidur di pangkuannya. Itulah sahabat sejati! 💪

Saat paling menegangkan terjadi ketika para pelacak Quraisy sampai di depan gua. Abu Bakr berbisik ketakutan, tapi Nabi berkata dengan tenang: **"Jangan bersedih, sesungguhnya Allah bersama kita."**

Allah melindungi mereka — para pelacak tidak masuk ke dalam gua!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Dua orang di dalam gua gelap, dengan bayangan kaki para pelacak terlihat di mulut gua. Setting: gua batu kecil dengan sedikit cahaya dari luar. Karakter: Nabi (tanpa wajah) duduk tenang, Abu Bakr di sampingnya tampak khawatir tapi setia. Objek kunci: sarang laba-laba di mulut gua, cahaya tipis dari luar. Warna dominan: cokelat batu, sedikit emas cahaya. Mood: tegang tapi penuh ketenangan. Komposisi: dua sosok di dalam gua gelap, siluet orang di luar.*

---

## Pengejar yang Berubah Pikiran

Setelah tiga hari, Nabi dan Abu Bakr berangkat ke Madinah lewat jalur pantai. Orang Quraisy menjanjikan hadiah **100 unta** bagi siapa yang menangkap Nabi — itu banyak sekali!

Seorang pelacak hebat bernama **Suraqah bin Malik** mengejar mereka. Tapi setiap kali kudanya hampir menyusul, kudanya **terperosok ke dalam pasir**! Satu kali, dua kali, tiga kali — kudanya selalu tenggelam. Suraqah sadar bahwa ada kekuatan yang melindungi Nabi.

Suraqah berhenti mengejar dan meminta maaf. Nabi bahkan berjanji kepadanya: "Suatu hari nanti, kamu akan memakai gelang raja Persia!" Suraqah pasti berpikir, "Itu tidak mungkin!" Tapi belasan tahun kemudian, janji itu **benar-benar terjadi**! 😲

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Seorang penunggang kuda dengan kuda yang terperosok ke dalam pasir gurun. Setting: padang pasir luas dengan jalur pantai terlihat. Karakter: Suraqah di atas kuda yang tenggelam di pasir, ekspresi terkejut. Objek kunci: kuda yang terperosok, debu membumbung. Warna dominan: kuning pasir, biru langit. Mood: dramatis dan menakjubkan. Komposisi: Suraqah di tengah, dua penunggang (Nabi dan Abu Bakr) di kejauhan.*

---

## Selamat Datang di Madinah!

Penduduk Madinah sudah menunggu Nabi setiap hari! Mereka keluar pagi-pagi ke pinggiran kota, menunggu sampai matahari terlalu terik, lalu pulang dan menunggu lagi besoknya.

Akhirnya, hari yang ditunggu tiba! Saat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ terlihat dari kejauhan, seluruh Madinah **meledak dalam kegembiraan**. Anak-anak perempuan Anshar bernyanyi: *"Telah terbit bulan purnama atas kami..."* 🎶

Nabi membangun **Masjid Quba** — masjid pertama dalam Islam — di desa pertama yang beliau singgahi.

Hijrah ini begitu penting sehingga dijadikan **awal kalender Islam** (penanggalan Hijriyah). Setiap kali kita melihat tahun Hijriyah, kita mengingat perjalanan luar biasa ini!

**Pelajaran penting:** Hijrah mengajarkan kita bahwa **Allah selalu melindungi orang yang beriman**, asalkan kita juga berusaha sekuat tenaga. Nabi merencanakan segalanya dengan cermat, lalu berserah kepada Allah. Itulah tawakkal sejati! 🌟

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Penduduk Madinah menyambut kedatangan Nabi dengan penuh suka cita. Setting: pinggiran kota Madinah dengan pohon kurma. Karakter: orang-orang bersorak gembira, anak-anak berlarian, Nabi (tanpa wajah) di atas unta. Objek kunci: pohon kurma, rumah-rumah Madinah. Warna dominan: hijau segar, emas hangat. Mood: gembira dan penuh syukur. Komposisi: Nabi memasuki kota dari kanan, kerumunan penyambut di kiri.*
e15-hijrah-madinah.general-en:
# The Hijrah to Madinah: A Turning Point in History and the Dawn of the Islamic Calendar

## Context: Why the Hijrah Had to Happen

After the Second Pledge of Aqabah had secured protection from the people of Madinah, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ instructed his companions to begin emigrating — quietly slipping out of Makkah to Madinah.^1 ^2 One by one, Muslim families departed. Houses in the Muslim quarters of Makkah began to empty.

The situation in Makkah had become untenable. During thirteen years of preaching, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and his followers endured a three-year economic and social boycott in Shi'b Abi Talib, physical torture of the weaker companions, and even assassination attempts against the Prophet himself.^1 ^2

Ibn Hisham and al-Mubarakfuri record that the Quraysh recognised the grave danger of this migration.^2 If Muhammad established a power base in Madinah — a city situated on the Quraysh's main trade route to al-Sham — their economic and political dominance would be threatened. They convened an emergency council at **Dar al-Nadwah** (the Quraysh assembly hall).

## The Event: The Dar al-Nadwah Conspiracy

Ibn Hisham narrates that the Quraysh leaders assembled and debated three options: imprison Muhammad, exile him, or kill him.^1 **Abu Jahl** proposed what was deemed the most cunning plan: select one strong young man from each Quraysh clan, then have them all strike Muhammad simultaneously. This way, his blood would be distributed among all clans, and the Banu Hashim would be unable to fight the entire Quraysh for blood-vengeance.^1 ^2

Allah informed Prophet Muhammad ﷺ of this conspiracy through revelation:

> وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِيُثْبِتُوكَ أَوْ يَقْتُلُوكَ أَوْ يُخْرِجُوكَ ۚ وَيَمْكُرُونَ وَيَمْكُرُ اللَّهُ ۖ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ ﴿٣٠﴾
>
> *"And [remember] when those who disbelieved plotted against you to restrain you or kill you or evict you. They plan, and Allah plans. And Allah is the best of planners. ﴾30﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Anfal (8): 30

## The Night of Departure: Ali in the Prophet's Bed

On the appointed night, the Quraysh youth had already surrounded the Prophet's house with drawn swords, waiting for him to emerge.^1 ^3 The Prophet asked **Ali ibn Abi Talib** — his young cousin — to sleep in his bed, wrapped in his green cloak, so the besiegers would think he was still inside.

Imam al-Bukhari narrates that Prophet Muhammad ﷺ walked out past them unseen, casting dust toward them while reciting the opening of Surah Yasin:^3

> وَجَعَلْنَا مِن بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ سَدًّا وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ سَدًّا فَأَغْشَيْنَاهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ ﴿٩﴾
>
> *"And We have placed before them a barrier and behind them a barrier and covered them, so they do not see. ﴾9﴿"*
> — QS. Yasin (36): 9

He went straight to the home of **Abu Bakr al-Siddiq**, his closest companion, who had prepared two camels for this journey months in advance.^3

## Three Days in the Cave of Thawr

Rather than heading directly north toward Madinah — the obvious route the Quraysh would track — Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and Abu Bakr went south instead, toward the **Cave of Thawr** on Jabal Thawr, approximately five kilometres south of Makkah.^1 ^2 ^3 This was a brilliant strategic decision: the enemy would certainly search northward toward Madinah, not south.

Al-Bukhari narrates the events inside the cave in vivid detail.^3 Abu Bakr entered first and cleaned the cave of holes that might harbour snakes or scorpions, plugging them with torn pieces of his own garment. When one hole remained and he had no cloth left, he sealed it with his heel. A snake bit his heel, but Abu Bakr did not move so as not to wake the Prophet, who slept with his head in Abu Bakr's lap.^3

The most dramatic moment came when the Quraysh trackers reached the mouth of the cave. Abu Bakr whispered in anguish: *"O Messenger of Allah, if any of them were to look beneath his feet, he would see us."* Prophet Muhammad ﷺ replied with extraordinary calm:

*"O Abu Bakr, what do you think of two whose third is Allah? "*^3

The Quran immortalised this moment:

> إِلَّا تَنصُرُوهُ فَقَدْ نَصَرَهُ اللَّهُ إِذْ أَخْرَجَهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ثَانِيَ اثْنَيْنِ إِذْ هُمَا فِي الْغَارِ إِذْ يَقُولُ لِصَاحِبِهِ لَا تَحْزَنْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَنَا ﴿٤٠﴾
>
> *"If you do not aid him — Allah has already aided him when those who disbelieved drove him out [of Makkah] as one of two, when they were in the cave and he said to his companion, 'Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us.' ﴾40﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Tawbah (9): 40

During the three days in the cave, **Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr** — Abu Bakr's young son — served as their spy, spending the night in the cave then returning to Makkah each morning to gather intelligence.^3 **Asma' bint Abi Bakr**, Abu Bakr's daughter, brought food each night, tearing her waist-belt in two to tie the provisions — earning her the title *Dhat al-Nitaqayn* (She of the Two Belts).^2 ^3 **Amir ibn Fuhayrah**, Abu Bakr's freed slave, drove his flock of sheep over their tracks to erase their footprints.^3

## The Journey to Madinah: The Story of Suraqah ibn Malik

After three nights, the Prophet and Abu Bakr set out for Madinah via an unconventional coastal route, guided by **Abdullah ibn Urayqit** — a professional guide from the Banu al-Dil who was still a polytheist but was trusted for his expertise.^1 ^2

The Quraysh had placed a bounty of **one hundred camels** on Muhammad's head — dead or alive.^1 ^2 This was an enormous fortune. **Suraqah ibn Malik**, an expert tracker from the Banu Mudlij, heard about two riders spotted on the coastal route and immediately gave chase, lured by the massive reward.

Al-Bukhari narrates Suraqah's account in his own words.^3 As Suraqah closed in, his horse suddenly sank into the sand up to its knees — something that had never happened before. He spurred his horse again, and a second time it sank. On the third attempt, the horse plunged in up to its chest, and from its hoofprints rose a column of dust like smoke.

Suraqah realised that a force was protecting Muhammad. He called out for a guarantee of safety. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ granted it and even made a promise that seemed impossible at the time: *"How will you feel, Suraqah, when you wear the bracelets of Kisra (the king of Persia)?"*^2 ^3

This promise was fulfilled over a decade later, during the caliphate of Umar ibn al-Khattab, when Persia was conquered and Kisra's bracelets were indeed placed upon Suraqah's wrists.^2

## Arrival at Quba and Madinah

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ arrived first at **Quba**, a village on the southern outskirts of Madinah, on Monday 12 Rabi' al-Awwal (approximately 24 September 622 CE).^1 ^2 There he established **Masjid Quba** — the first mosque built in Islam.

The people of Madinah had been expecting his arrival daily. Al-Bukhari narrates that they went out each morning to the high ground on the city's edge, waiting until the midday heat forced them back.^3 On the day of his arrival, a Jewish man in Madinah who spotted them from atop a fortress cried out: *"O Arabs! Here is the one you have been waiting for!"* The whole of Madinah erupted in joy. The young girls of the Ansar sang the famous verses:

*"Tala'a al-badru 'alayna / min Thaniyyat al-Wada'... / The full moon has risen upon us / from the pass of Wada'..."*^2

## Analysis: Why the Hijrah Became Year Zero of the Islamic Calendar

The hijrah was not merely a geographical relocation — it was a fundamental transformation from an oppressed community into a sovereign state. **Umar ibn al-Khattab** later designated the hijrah as the starting point of the Islamic calendar (*tarikh Hijri*), not the Prophet's birth, not the first revelation, not the victory at Badr — but the hijrah.^1 ^2

The reasoning was profound: the hijrah marked the moment when Islam shifted from a phase of individual preaching to civilisation-building. Before the hijrah, Islam was a faith held by scattered, persecuted individuals. After the hijrah, Islam became the foundation of a society, a legal system, an economy, and a polity.

**First, planned trust in God.** The hijrah story is the perfect blend of human effort and divine aid. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ planned every detail — the direction of travel, the hiding location, the logistics network, the professional guide — while placing complete trust in Allah. In the Cave of Thawr, with the enemy at the doorstep, he said: *"Allah is with us."*

**Second, collective sacrifice.** The hijrah was not one person's act. Ali risked his life sleeping in the Prophet's bed. Abu Bakr sacrificed his wealth. Abdullah and Asma' risked their safety as intelligence agents and couriers. Amir ibn Fuhayrah devoted his time to covering tracks. Every small role contributed to the greater success.

**Third, leaving home for principle.** The *muhajirin* left their homes, their wealth, even their families in Makkah for the sake of their faith. **Suhayb al-Rumi** had to surrender his entire fortune, seized by the Quraysh as a "ransom" to be allowed to leave. When the Prophet heard this, he said: *"Suhayb has profited! Suhayb has profited!"*^2

## Lessons: The Enduring Wisdom of the Hijrah

1. **Planning does not contradict trust in God.** The Prophet did not wait for a miracle to save him. He planned meticulously, then placed his trust in Allah. This is the model balance between effort and reliance on God.

2. **True friendship is tested in crisis.** Abu Bakr was the model of an ideal companion — he sacrificed everything without being asked, shielded the Prophet with his own body, and never complained.

3. **Everyone has a role.** From a shepherd (Amir) to a teenage girl (Asma'), the hijrah was made possible by the contributions of many people from diverse backgrounds.

4. **Leaving comfort zones for truth.** The hijrah teaches that homeland is not merely about geography — it is the place where one can practise one's faith freely.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (The Prophetic Biography). Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safi al-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih al-Bukhari). Hadiths on the hijrah, the Cave of Thawr, Suraqah ibn Malik, and the arrival in Madinah.
e15-hijrah-madinah.general-id:
# Hijrah ke Madinah: Titik Balik Sejarah dan Awal Penanggalan Islam

## Konteks: Mengapa Hijrah Harus Terjadi

Setelah Bai'at Aqabah Kedua memberikan jaminan perlindungan dari penduduk Madinah, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ memerintahkan para sahabatnya untuk mulai berhijrah — berpindah secara diam-diam dari Makkah ke Madinah.^1 ^2 Satu per satu, keluarga-keluarga muslim meninggalkan Makkah. Rumah-rumah di perkampungan Bani Muhajir mulai kosong.

Situasi di Makkah memang sudah tidak bisa ditoleransi lagi. Selama tiga belas tahun berdakwah, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan pengikutnya menghadapi boikot ekonomi dan sosial selama tiga tahun di Syi'b Abu Thalib, penyiksaan fisik terhadap para sahabat yang lemah, dan bahkan percobaan pembunuhan terhadap beliau sendiri.^1 ^2

Ibn Hisham dan al-Mubarakfuri mencatat bahwa kaum Quraisy menyadari bahaya besar dari hijrah ini.^2 Jika Muhammad berhasil membangun basis kekuatan di Madinah — sebuah kota yang terletak di jalur perdagangan utama Quraisy ke Syam — maka dominasi ekonomi dan politik mereka terancam. Maka mereka mengadakan pertemuan darurat di **Dar al-Nadwah** (balai pertemuan Quraisy).

## Peristiwa: Konspirasi Dar al-Nadwah

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa para pemimpin Quraisy berkumpul dan membahas tiga opsi: memenjarakan Muhammad, mengusirnya, atau membunuhnya.^1 **Abu Jahal** mengusulkan rencana yang dianggap paling cerdik: memilih satu pemuda tangguh dari setiap klan Quraisy, lalu mereka semua menyerang Muhammad secara bersamaan. Dengan demikian, darah Muhammad tersebar di antara semua klan, dan Bani Hasyim tidak akan mampu memerangi seluruh Quraisy untuk menuntut balas.^1 ^2

Allah memberitahu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ tentang konspirasi ini melalui wahyu:

> وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِيُثْبِتُوكَ أَوْ يَقْتُلُوكَ أَوْ يُخْرِجُوكَ ۚ وَيَمْكُرُونَ وَيَمْكُرُ اللَّهُ ۖ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ ﴿٣٠﴾
>
> *"Dan (ingatlah) ketika orang-orang kafir memikirkan tipu daya terhadapmu untuk menangkapmu, membunuhmu, atau mengusirmu. Mereka membuat tipu daya dan Allah membalas tipu daya mereka. Dan Allah sebaik-baik pembalas tipu daya. ﴾30﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Anfal (8): 30

## Malam Keberangkatan: Ali di Tempat Tidur Nabi

Pada malam yang telah ditentukan, para pemuda Quraisy sudah mengepung rumah Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dengan pedang terhunus, menunggu beliau keluar.^1 ^3 Nabi meminta **Ali bin Abi Thalib** — sepupunya yang masih remaja — untuk tidur di tempat tidurnya, berselimutkan kain hijau beliau, agar para pengepung mengira beliau masih di dalam rumah.

Imam al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi Muhammad ﷺ keluar melewati mereka tanpa terlihat, sambil menaburkan debu ke arah mereka dan membaca awal Surah Yasin:^3
