# Perang Khandaq (Parit): Strategi Pertahanan Salman al-Farisi

## Konteks: Koalisi Terbesar Melawan Madinah

Setelah Badr dan Uhud, kaum Quraisy menyadari bahwa mereka tidak bisa menghancurkan Madinah sendirian. Pada tahun 5 Hijriyah (627 M), mereka membentuk koalisi militer terbesar yang pernah dikumpulkan di Jazirah Arab untuk menyerang satu kota.^1

Aliansi ini — yang dalam al-Quran disebut *al-Ahzab* (golongan-golongan yang bersekutu) — terdiri dari beberapa kekuatan utama: Quraisy Makkah yang dipimpin Abu Sufyan dengan 4.000 prajurit; suku Ghathafan dan Bani Fazarah dari Najd dengan 6.000 prajurit; serta beberapa suku Arab lainnya.^1 ^2 Total pasukan koalisi berjumlah sekitar **10.000 prajurit** — kekuatan militer terbesar yang pernah diarahkan terhadap komunitas Muslim hingga saat itu.

Pembentukan koalisi ini dimotori oleh tokoh-tokoh Yahudi dari Bani Nadhir yang telah diusir dari Madinah. Pemimpin mereka, Huyay bin Akhtab, berkeliling dari satu suku ke suku lain, menghasut mereka dengan janji pembagian kekayaan Madinah dan pembasmi ancaman Islam.^2 ^3

Di sisi lain, Madinah hanya memiliki sekitar **3.000 prajurit** untuk mempertahankan diri. Rasio satu lawan tiga — lebih buruk dari Badr — dan kali ini musuh datang dengan persiapan yang jauh lebih matang.

## Peristiwa: Strategi Parit yang Revolusioner

### Ide Salman al-Farisi

Ketika ancaman koalisi semakin nyata, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ kembali bermusyawarah dengan para sahabat. Di sinilah **Salman al-Farisi** — sahabat yang berasal dari Persia — mengajukan gagasan yang mengubah segalanya.^2

Salman berkata: *"Wahai Rasulullah, di Persia, apabila kami dikepung musuh, kami menggali parit di sekeliling kota."*

Ide ini sama sekali asing bagi tradisi perang Arab. Bangsa Arab terbiasa dengan pertempuran terbuka di padang pasir — duel kehormatan, serangan kavaleri, dan benturan langsung. Menggali parit dianggap tidak jantan. Namun Nabi Muhammad ﷺ langsung menerima gagasan ini dengan antusias — menunjukkan keterbukaan beliau terhadap kearifan dari berbagai peradaban.^1 ^2

### Penggalian Parit

Seluruh penduduk Muslim Madinah — laki-laki, perempuan, tua, muda — bekerja selama sekitar **enam hari** menggali parit di sisi utara Madinah.^1 Sisi ini adalah satu-satunya arah yang terbuka untuk serangan; sisi-sisi lainnya dilindungi oleh formasi bebatuan lava*harrah*, perkebunan kurma yang lebat, dan bangunan-bangunan kota.

Parit tersebut membentang sepanjang kira-kira **5,5 kilometer** dengan lebar sekitar 9 meter dan kedalaman sekitar 4,5 meter — cukup untuk mencegah kuda dan unta melintas.^2 ^3

Imam al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi Muhammad ﷺ sendiri ikut menggali. Para sahabat melihat beliau mengangkut tanah hingga debu menutupi perutnya. Di tengah kerja keras itu, para sahabat bersyair:^1

*"Kami adalah orang-orang yang telah berbaiat kepada Muhammad, untuk berjihad selama kami masih hidup."*

Dan Nabi menjawab: *"Ya Allah, tiada kehidupan kecuali kehidupan akhirat. Maka muliakanlah kaum Anshar dan Muhajirin."*^1

Selama penggalian, kondisi kaum Muslim sangat sulit. Al-Waqidi dalam *al-Maghazi* mencatat bahwa persediaan makanan nyaris habis. Para sahabat mengikatkan batu di perut mereka untuk menahan lapar. Ketika mereka menunjukkan hal ini kepada Nabi, beliau menyingkap perutnya — dan ternyata beliau mengikatkan **dua** batu di perutnya sendiri.^2 ^3

### Pengepungan

Ketika koalisi tiba di Madinah, mereka terkejut menemukan parit yang menghalangi serangan mereka. Tidak ada preseden dalam sejarah perang Arab untuk taktik semacam ini. Amr bin al-Ash — salah satu jenderal Quraisy — dilaporkan berkata: *"Ini adalah tipu muslihat yang tidak pernah dilakukan oleh bangsa Arab!"*^2

Pengepungan berlangsung selama kurang lebih **dua puluh tujuh hari** — ada riwayat yang menyebutkan hampir sebulan.^1 ^2 Beberapa prajurit Quraisy mencoba menyeberangi parit di titik-titik sempit, namun sebagian besar berhasil dicegah.

Salah satu momen paling dramatis terjadi ketika **Amr bin Abd Wudd**, pejuang legendaris Quraisy yang dikenal setara dengan seribu prajurit, berhasil melompati parit dengan kudanya. Ia menantang duel. Para sahabat terdiam karena gentar. **Ali bin Abi Thalib** maju menerima tantangan itu dan berhasil mengalahkannya — sebuah aksi keberanian yang masyhur dalam sejarah Islam.^2

### Pengkhianatan dan Diplomasi

Di tengah pengepungan, situasi bertambah genting ketika **Bani Quraizhah** — suku Yahudi yang terikat perjanjian dengan Muslim di Madinah — mengkhianati perjanjian mereka dan bergabung dengan koalisi, atas hasutan Huyay bin Akhtab.^1 ^2 Ini membuka ancaman dari dalam kota sendiri.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ merespons dengan diplomasi cerdas. Beliau mengutus **Nu'aim bin Mas'ud**, seorang prajurit Ghathafan yang baru saja masuk Islam secara diam-diam, untuk menebar ketidakpercayaan antara Quraisy, Ghathafan, dan Bani Quraizhah. Nu'aim berhasil — ia membuat masing-masing pihak saling mencurigai, memecah kesolidan koalisi dari dalam.^2 ^3

### Angin dan Pertolongan Ilahi

Puncak pertolongan datang secara dramatis. Allah mengirimkan angin kencang dan badai pasir yang sangat dingin — diriwayatkan terjadi pada malam yang sangat gelap. Angin ini menerbangkan kemah-kemah pasukan koalisi, memadamkan api mereka, membalikkan periuk-periuk masak mereka, dan menciptakan kekacauan di perkemahan musuh.^1 ^2

Abu Sufyan, yang sudah lelah dengan pengepungan yang berlarut-larut, akhirnya memutuskan untuk mundur. Ia berteriak di tengah malam: *"Wahai orang-orang Quraisy, kembalilah! Sesungguhnya aku akan pulang!"* Dan dengan itu, koalisi terbesar yang pernah dibentuk untuk melawan Islam, bubar tanpa berhasil memasuki Madinah.

## Dialog dan Dalil

Al-Quran merekam peristiwa Khandaq dalam Surah Al-Ahzab:

> يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جَاءَتْكُمْ جُنُودٌ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا وَجُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا ﴿٩﴾
>
> *"Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, ingatlah nikmat Allah kepadamu ketika datang kepadamu pasukan-pasukan (musuh), lalu Kami kirimkan kepada mereka angin dan pasukan (malaikat) yang tidak kamu lihat. ﴾9﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Ahzab (33): 9

Tentang ujian berat yang dihadapi kaum Muslim selama pengepungan:

> إِذْ جَاءُوكُم مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ وَإِذْ زَاغَتِ الْأَبْصَارُ وَبَلَغَتِ الْقُلُوبُ الْحَنَاجِرَ ﴿١٠﴾
>
> *"(Yaitu) ketika mereka datang kepadamu dari atas dan dari bawahmu, dan ketika penglihatan menjadi kabur dan hati menyesak sampai ke tenggorokan. ﴾10﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Ahzab (33): 10

Dan setelah kemenangan, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ bersabda:^1

> الْآنَ نَغْزُوهُمْ وَلَا يَغْزُونَنَا، نَحْنُ نَسِيرُ إِلَيْهِمْ
>
> *"Sekarang kitalah yang akan menyerang mereka, dan mereka tidak akan menyerang kita lagi. Kitalah yang akan mendatangi mereka. ﴾10﴿"*
> — HR Bukhari, no. 4109, Kitab al-Maghazi

Pernyataan ini terbukti benar — setelah Khandaq, Quraisy tidak pernah lagi menyerang Madinah.

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Inovasi Lintas Peradaban

Strategi parit Salman al-Farisi adalah contoh cemerlang bagaimana Islam menyambut kearifan dari berbagai peradaban. Salman adalah orang Persia yang membawa pengetahuan teknik militer dari peradabannya; Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, seorang Arab, menerimanya tanpa ego nasionalis. Pesan ini sangat relevan: kebenaran dan kebaikan tidak mengenal batas etnis — dari mana pun datangnya, ia layak diadopsi.

### Pertahanan Lebih Kuat dari Serangan

Khandaq membuktikan bahwa dalam situasi tertentu, pertahanan yang cerdas lebih efektif daripada serangan langsung. 3.000 prajurit yang bertahan di balik parit berhasil menahan 10.000 penyerang tanpa pertempuran terbuka yang besar. Kecerdasan strategis menggantikan kekuatan fisik — prinsip yang relevan dalam segala aspek kehidupan.

### Kesabaran di Bawah Tekanan

Pengepungan selama hampir sebulan adalah ujian mental yang luar biasa. Kelaparan, dingin, ancaman dari luar dan pengkhianatan dari dalam — semua terjadi bersamaan. Namun kaum Muslim bertahan. Al-Quran menggambarkan kondisi ini sebagai saat "hati menyesak sampai ke tenggorokan" — tapi justru di titik terendah itulah pertolongan Allah datang.

### Titik Balik Strategis

Khandaq menandai perubahan fundamental dalam dinamika kekuatan di Jazirah Arab. Setelah Khandaq, Quraisy tidak pernah lagi mampu melakukan serangan ofensif terhadap Madinah. Inisiatif strategis berpindah sepenuhnya ke tangan kaum Muslim — sebagaimana dinyatakan oleh Nabi sendiri. Dua tahun kemudian, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ memimpin 10.000 sahabat dalam pembebasan Makkah tanpa pertumpahan darah.

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Bukhari). Hadits no. 4109 dan lainnya, Kitab al-Maghazi, Bab Ghazwah al-Khandaq.

2. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Jilid 3, Bab Ghazwah al-Khandaq. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

3. **Al-Waqidi, Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Umar.** *Kitab al-Maghazi*. Jilid 2, Bab Ghazwah al-Khandaq. Beirut: Dar al-A'lami, 1989.

e22-hudaibiyah.children-en:
# The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah 🕊️

---

## Missing Makkah

For six years, the Muslims had been living in Madinah, far from Makkah — the city where they were born and where the Ka'bah stood. They missed it so much.

One night, **Prophet Muhammad ﷺ** had a dream of performing umrah at the Ka'bah. A prophet's dream is always true! So he invited about **1,400 companions** to travel to Makkah. They wore white *ihram* clothes and brought sacrificial animals — a sign that they came to worship, not to fight.

But the Quraysh were not happy. They sent horsemen to block the Muslims' path!

> 🎨 **Illustration**: A vast desert with a large caravan dressed in white walking toward Makkah, camels carrying supplies, a golden-bright sky. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ at the front. Dominant colors: white, sandy gold, sky blue. Mood: hopeful. Composition: wide panorama, caravan stretching from left to right.

---

## Stopping at Hudaybiyyah

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ chose a winding path through rocky cliffs to avoid the Quraysh soldiers. At a place called **Hudaybiyyah**, the Prophet's camel named **al-Qaswa'** suddenly knelt down and refused to move!

Some companions said: "The camel is being stubborn!" But the Prophet smiled and said: *"Al-Qaswa' is not being stubborn. She has been stopped by the One who stopped the elephant army."*

It turned out that Allah wanted them to stop there! At Hudaybiyyah, the well was almost dry. The Prophet placed an arrow in the well — and amazingly, water gushed out! Everyone could drink to their fill.

> 🎨 **Illustration**: Camel al-Qaswa' kneeling in a rocky valley, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ standing beside her with a calm expression. Behind, the caravan waits. Nearby, a small well with water beginning to flow. Dominant colors: rocky brown, white garments. Mood: wondrous. Composition: focus on the camel and Prophet at center, valley stretching in the background.

---

## The Pledge Under the Tree

The Prophet sent **Uthman ibn Affan** to Makkah to talk with the Quraysh. But Uthman didn't come back for a long time, and a rumor spread that he had been killed!

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ sat under a big tree and asked all his companions to make a pledge — a promise that they would not retreat no matter what happened. One by one, **1,400 companions** extended their hands and pledged. This was called the **Pledge of Ridwan**.

Allah was very pleased with their bravery. In the Quran, Allah says: **"Certainly was Allah pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree."** (QS Al-Fath: 18)

Thankfully, the rumor was not true — Uthman was safe!

> 🎨 **Illustration**: A large leafy tree in the desert, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ sitting beneath it, companions lined up extending their hands to pledge. Faces full of determination. Dominant colors: green foliage, white garments, brown earth. Mood: solemn and brave. Composition: the large tree as the focal point, companions gathered around it.

---

## A Difficult Agreement

Finally, the Quraysh sent **Suhayl ibn Amr** to negotiate. The result was an agreement that made many companions sad:

- The Muslims had to **go home without performing umrah** that year
- They could come back **next year** and stay for three days
- A **ceasefire** for ten years

**Umar ibn al-Khattab** was very upset. He asked: "Why should we accept this?" The Prophet calmly answered: *"I am the Messenger of Allah, and He will surely help me."*

The saddest moment was when **Abu Jandal** — a Muslim being tortured in Makkah — came begging for help, but had to be returned according to the agreement. Many companions wept.

But it turned out... this treaty was a **great victory**! Allah declared: **"Indeed, We have granted you a clear victory."** (QS Al-Fath: 1)

> 🎨 **Illustration**: Two groups facing each other — Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and companions on one side, Suhayl ibn Amr and the Quraysh delegation on the other. In the middle, a document being written. Serious expressions on both sides. Dominant colors: white and dark red. Mood: tense yet hopeful. Composition: symmetrical, two groups facing each other with the treaty in the center.

---

## Lessons from Hudaybiyyah

Why did this seemingly unfair treaty turn out to be a victory?

🕊️ **Peace opened the door to dawah** — Without war, many Arabs could learn about Islam. In just two years, the number of Muslims multiplied!

🤝 **The Quraysh recognized the Muslims** — By sitting down to negotiate, the Quraysh acknowledged that the Muslims were a strong and legitimate group.

💪 **Patience is strength** — Prophet Muhammad ﷺ taught that patience and wisdom are stronger than anger. Sometimes stepping back one pace can take you forward ten!

📨 **Dawah to the whole world** — With peace secured, the Prophet could send letters to the great kings: Rome, Persia, Egypt, and Abyssinia!
e22-hudaibiyah.children-id:
# Perjanjian Hudaibiyah 🕊️

---

## Rindu ke Makkah

Sudah enam tahun kaum Muslimin tinggal di Madijauh dari Makkah — kota tempat mereka dilahirkan dan tempat Ka'bah berdiri. Mereka sangat rindu ingin kembali ke sana.

Suatu malam, **Nabi Muhammad ﷺ** bermimpi sedang melakukan ibadah umrah di Ka'bah. Mimpi seorang nabi itu pasti benar! Maka beliau mengajak sekitar **1.400 sahabat** untuk berangkat ke Makkah. Mereka memakai pakaian putih *ihram* dan membawa hewan kurban — tanda bahwa mereka datang untuk beribadah, bukan untuk berperang.

Tapi Quraisy tidak senang. Mereka mengirim pasukan berkuda untuk menghalangi jalan kaum Muslimin!

> 🎨 **Ilustrasi**: Padang pasir luas dengan rombongan besar berpakaian putih berjalan menuju Makkah, unta-unta membawa bekal, langit cerah keemasan. Nabi Muhammad ﷺ di depan rombongan. Warna dominan: putih, emas pasir, biru langit. Mood: penuh harap. Komposisi: panorama lebar, rombongan membentang dari kiri ke kanan.

---

## Berhenti di Hudaibiyah

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ memilih jalan memutar lewat tebing-tebing berbatu agar tidak bertemu pasukan Quraisy. Di sebuah tempat bernama **Hudaibiyah**, unta Nabi yang bernama **al-Qashwa'** tiba-tiba berlutut dan tidak mau jalan lagi!

Beberapa sahabat berkata: "Untanya membandel!" Tapi Nabi tersenyum dan berkata: *"Al-Qashwa' tidak membandel. Ia ditahan oleh Dzat yang menahan pasukan gajah."*

Ternyata Allah ingin mereka berhenti di situ! Di Hudaibiyah, air sumur hampir habis. Nabi menancapkan anak panah ke sumur — dan ajaibnya, air langsung memancar deras! Semua orang bisa minum sepuasnya.

> 🎨 **Ilustrasi**: Unta al-Qashwa' berlutut di lembah berbatu, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ berdiri di sampingnya dengan ekspresi tenang. Di belakang, rombongan berhenti menunggu. Di samping, sumur kecil dengan air yang mulai memancar. Warna dominan: cokelat batu, putih pakaian. Mood: penuh keajaiban. Komposisi: fokus pada unta dan Nabi di tengah, lembah membentang di latar.

---

## Janji Setia di Bawah Pohon

Nabi mengirim **Utsman bin Affan** ke Makkah untuk berbicara dengan Quraisy. Tapi Utsman tidak kembali-kembali, dan tersebar kabar bahwa ia telah dibunuh!

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ duduk di bawah sebuah pohon besar dan meminta semua sahabat untuk berjanji setia — berjanji tidak akan mundur apa pun yang terjadi. Satu per satu, **1.400 sahabat** mengulurkan tangan dan berjanji. Ini disebut **Bai'at al-Ridwan**.

Allah sangat senang dengan keberanian mereka. Dalam al-Quran, Allah berfirman: **"Sungguh, Allah telah ridha terhadap orang-orang mukmin ketika mereka berjanji setia kepadamu di bawah pohon."** (QS Al-Fath: 18)

Untungnya, kabar itu tidak benar — Utsman ternyata selamat!

> 🎨 **Ilustrasi**: Pohon besar rindang di tengah padang pasir, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ duduk di bawahnya, para sahabat berjajar mengulurkan tangan untuk berbaiat. Ekspresi wajah penuh tekad. Warna dominan: hijau dedaunan, putih pakaian, cokelat tanah. Mood: khidmat dan berani. Komposisi: pohon besar sebagai titik pusat, sahabat melingkar di sekitarnya.

---

## Perjanjian yang Sulit

Akhirnya Quraisy mengirim **Suhail bin Amr** untuk bernegosiasi. Hasilnya adalah perjanjian yang membuat banyak sahabat sedih:

- Kaum Muslimin harus **pulang tanpa umrah** tahun ini
- Mereka boleh kembali **tahun depan** dan tinggal tiga hari
- **Gencatan senjata** selama sepuluh tahun

**Umar bin Khattab** sangat kesal. Ia bertanya: "Mengapa kita menerima ini?" Nabi menjawab dengan tenang: *"Aku adalah utusan Allah, dan Dia pasti akan menolongku."*

Momen paling menyedihkan adalah ketika **Abu Jandal** — seorang Muslim yang disiksa di Makkah — datang meminta tolong, tapi harus dikembalikan sesuai perjanjian. Banyak sahabat menangis melihatnya.

Tapi ternyata.. perjanjian ini adalah **kemenangan besar**! Allah berfirman: **"Sesungguhnya Kami telah memberikan kepadamu kemenangan yang nyata."** (QS Al-Fath: 1)

> 🎨 **Ilustrasi**: Dua kelompok berhadapan — Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan sahabat di satu sisi, Suhail bin Amr dan delegasi Quraisy di sisi lain. Di tengah, sebuah lembaran perjanjian sedang ditulis. Ekspresi serius di kedua sisi. Warna dominan: putih dan merah gelap. Mood: tegang tapi penuh harap. Komposisi: simetris, dua kelompok berhadapan dengan perjanjian di tengah.

---

## Hikmah dari Hudaibiyah

Kenapa perjanjian yang terlihat merugikan ini justru menjadi kemenangan?

🕊️ **Perdamaian membuka pintu dakwah** — Tanpa perang, banyak orang Arab bisa mengenal Islam. Dalam dua tahun saja, jumlah Muslim berlipat ganda!

🤝 **Quraisy mengakui kaum Muslimin** — Dengan duduk bernegosiasi, Quraisy mengakui bahwa kaum Muslimin adalah kelompok yang kuat dan sah.

💪 **Sabar itu kuat** — Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengajarkan bahwa sabar dan bijaksana itu lebih kuat daripada marah. Kadang mundur satu langkah bisa membawa kita maju sepuluh langkah!

📨 **Dakwah ke seluruh dunia** — Dengan suasana damai, Nabi bisa mengirim surat ke raja-raja besar: Romawi, Persia, Mesir, dan Habasyah!
e22-hudaibiyah.general-en:
# The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah: Diplomacy and Peace

## Historical Context: Longing for the Sacred Land

Six years had passed since the Muslims left Makkah. Those who were born and raised in the sacred city now lived in Madinah, building a new society, enduring war after war. Yet their longing for the Ka'bah — the direction of prayer, the sacred house built by Ibrahim and Ismail — never faded.^1

In the month of Dhul-Qa'dah of the 6th year of Hijrah (628 CE), Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had a dream of entering the Sacred Mosque and performing *tawaf* around the Ka'bah. In the prophetic tradition, the dream of a prophet is revelation. He announced to his companions: they would travel to Makkah to perform *umrah*, not to wage war.^1 ^2

Approximately 1,400 Muslims joined the caravan. They wore *ihram* — the seamless white garments of pilgrimage — and brought sacrificial animals. No weapons of war were carried, only sheathed swords as was customary for travelers.^2 ^3 This was a clear declaration of peace: they came as pilgrims, not soldiers.

The Quraysh, however, saw it differently. To them, the arrival of Muhammad and his followers — even in pilgrim garments — was a threat to their pride and political dominance. They dispatched Khalid ibn al-Walid with 200 cavalry toward Kura' al-Ghamim to intercept the Muslim caravan.^2

## The Journey to Hudaybiyyah

When news of the Quraysh blockade reached them, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ made a brilliant strategic decision. Rather than continuing on the main road guarded by Khalid's forces, he chose an alternative route through the difficult rocky terrain of *Thaniyyat al-Murar*, navigating steep cliffs rarely traversed.^2 The caravan of 1,400, complete with sacrificial animals, had to struggle through narrow and precipitous paths.

At this point occurred a sign of divine will: the Prophet's camel, al-Qaswa', knelt down and refused to move further at a place called Hudaybiyyah, a barren valley on the edge of the sacred territory of Makkah. Some companions said: "Al-Qaswa' is being stubborn!" But the Prophet replied:^1

> مَا خَلَأَتِ الْقَصْوَاءُ، وَمَا ذَاكَ لَهَا بِخُلُقٍ، وَلَكِنْ حَبَسَهَا حَابِسُ الْفِيلِ
>
> *"Al-Qaswa' has not become stubborn, for that is not her nature. Rather, she has been stopped by the One who stopped the elephant."*
> — Sahih al-Bukhari, no. 2731, Kitab al-Shurut

The phrase "the One who stopped the elephant" referred to the event of the Prophet's own birth year, when Allah destroyed the army of Abrahah that sought to demolish the Ka'bah. The Prophet's message was clear: a divine will had halted their journey at this point, and they must comply.

The caravan set up camp at Hudaybiyyah. Water was extremely scarce — the existing well was nearly dry. The companions reported the drought to the Prophet. He took an arrow from his quiver and ordered it placed at the bottom of the well. Ibn Hisham and al-Bukhari record that water then gushed forth abundantly, enough for the entire caravan to drink and perform ablution.^1 ^3

### Diplomatic Negotiations

From Hudaybiyyah began a series of long and tense negotiations. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ sent **Kharash ibn Umayyah al-Khuza'i** as his first envoy to Makkah, but the Quraysh slaughtered his camel and nearly killed him.^2 This was a grave violation of the Arab tradition of honoring peace envoys.

Several envoys then came from the Quraysh side. First was **Budayl ibn Warqa'** of the Khuza'ah, who served as an initial mediator. Then came **Urwah ibn Mas'ud al-Thaqafi**, a respected leader from Ta'if. Urwah carefully observed how the companions treated the Prophet — they competed for the water of his ablution, did not let his hair fall to the ground, and lowered their gaze when speaking to him. Urwah returned to the Quraysh and declared:^2

*"O my people, by Allah, I have been an envoy to kings — the Caesar of Rome, the Chosroes of Persia, and the Negus of Abyssinia — and I have never seen a king revered by his people as Muhammad is revered by his companions. "*

Finally, the Prophet sent **Uthman ibn Affan** to Makkah, as Uthman had strong kinship ties with the Banu Umayyah that would better ensure his safety. However, Uthman was detained longer than expected, and rumors spread that he had been killed.^2

### The Pledge of Ridwan

The report of Uthman's death — which proved to be false — triggered the most dramatic moment of the entire Hudaybiyyah episode. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ sat beneath a tree and called upon his companions to pledge allegiance — to vow not to retreat even in the face of death. One by one, all 1,400 companions extended their hands and made their pledge.^1 ^2

This event was so momentous in the sight of Allah that it was immortalized in the Quran:

> لَّقَدْ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبَايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ فَعَلِمَ مَا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ فَأَنزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَأَثَابَهُمْ فَتْحًا قَرِيبًا ﴿١٨﴾
>
> *"Certainly was Allah pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree, and He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down tranquility upon them and rewarded them with an imminent victory. ﴾18﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Fath (48): 18

The resolute determination of the Muslims changed the Quraysh's political calculus. They realized that attacking a group prepared to die was not a wise option. So they sent **Suhayl ibn Amr**, their most skilled negotiator, to deal directly with Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

## The Terms of the Treaty and Their Controversy

The negotiation between Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and Suhayl ibn Amr was intense. Every clause was fiercely debated. The final agreement appeared, on the surface, heavily disadvantageous to the Muslims:^1 ^2 ^3

**First**, a ceasefire for ten years. Neither side would attack the other, and Arab tribes were free to ally with whichever party they chose.

**Second**, the Muslims must return to Madinah without performing umrah that year. They could return the following year and stay in Makkah for three days.

**Third**, the most controversial clause: if any Makkan converted to Islam and fled to Madinah, Muhammad must return them to the Quraysh. However, if any Madinan defected to Makkah, the Quraysh had no obligation to return them.

The third clause provoked enormous anger among the companions. **Umar ibn al-Khattab**, known for his firmness and courage, approached the Prophet with a reddened face:^1

*"O Messenger of Allah, are you not truly the Prophet of Allah?"* — "Yes." — *"Are we not upon the truth and our enemy upon falsehood?"* — "Yes." — *"Then why do we accept humiliation in our religion?"*

The Prophet answered calmly: *"I am the Messenger of Allah, and I will not disobey Him. He will surely give me victory."*

Even during the writing of the treaty, tensions flared. When Ali ibn Abi Talib wrote *"Bismillahir-Rahmanir-Rahim"*, Suhayl objected and demanded it be written as *"Bismika Allahumma"* (the pre-Islamic formula). When Ali wrote *"Muhammad Rasulullah"*, Suhayl objected again: "If we acknowledged you as a messenger, we would not fight you." The Prophet instructed Ali to erase the phrase and replace it with *"Muhammad ibn Abdullah"*.^1 Ali found it difficult to do so, and the Prophet himself pointed to the words and erased them.

The real test of the treaty came almost immediately. **Abu Jandal ibn Suhayl ibn Amr** — Suhayl's own son — appeared stumbling into the negotiations, still in chains, his body bearing the marks of torture. He had secretly embraced Islam and was being tortured by his father. He pleaded for asylum with the Muslims. Suhayl insisted: "This is the first person you must return to me." With a heavy heart, the Prophet honored the treaty and asked Abu Jandal to be patient. The sight brought many companions to tears.^1 ^2

## Analysis: Why Hudaybiyyah Was a Victory

The Quran called this event a *fath mubin* — a clear victory:

> إِنَّا فَتَحْنَا لَكَ فَتْحًا مُّبِينًا ﴿١﴾
>
> *"Indeed, We have granted you a clear victory."*
> — QS. Al-Fath (48): 1

Many companions were initially bewildered: where was the victory? They returned without performing umrah, accepted apparently one-sided terms, and had to return Muslims who sought asylum. Yet history proved that Hudaybiyyah was the true strategic turning point.^1 ^2 ^3

**First, de facto recognition.** By sitting down to negotiate as equals with Muhammad, the Quraysh implicitly acknowledged the Muslims as a legitimate political entity. For years, the Quraysh had portrayed Muhammad as a rebel and troublemaker. The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah permanently altered that narrative.

**Second, the field of dawah opened.** The ceasefire removed the threat of war, allowing the Prophet to focus on preaching. In the two years between Hudaybiyyah and the Opening of Makkah, the number of Muslims multiplied — some sources suggest more people embraced Islam in those two years than in the entire preceding period.^2 ^3

**Third, the seemingly disadvantageous clause backfired.** Makkan Muslims who could not go to Madinah formed an independent armed group along the Quraysh trade routes, led by **Abu Basir**. They raided Quraysh caravans so effectively that the Quraysh themselves asked the Prophet to annul the return clause and accept these men in Madinah.^2 ^3

**Fourth, freedom of tribal alliance.** The Khuza'ah immediately joined the Prophet's alliance, while the Banu Bakr allied with the Quraysh. When the Banu Bakr attacked the Khuza'ah with tacit Quraysh support, they violated the treaty — and this became the direct cause for the Opening of Makkah two years later.

**Fifth, international diplomacy.** With peace secured, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ could send letters of invitation to the great rulers of the world — Heraclius (Rome), Chosroes (Persia), Muqawqis (Egypt), and the Negus (Abyssinia). This transformed Islam from a regional movement into a universal call.^2

## Lessons: What Hudaybiyyah Teaches

Hudaybiyyah teaches several fundamental principles:

**Strategic patience.** Prophet Muhammad ﷺ demonstrated that a true leader is not enslaved by momentary emotions. When all his companions desired confrontation, he chose a path that was far more advantageous in the long term. This is a lesson about the difference between courage and recklessness.

**The priority of peace.** Islam is often misunderstood as a religion that glorifies war. Hudaybiyyah proves the opposite: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was willing to accept terms that appeared humiliating in order to avoid bloodshed and pave the way for peace.

**Obedience to leadership.** Although the companions held different opinions — and some expressed them forcefully — they ultimately submitted to the Prophet's decision. Umar ibn al-Khattab himself later regretted his impatience and performed many good deeds in atonement.^3

**Long-term vision.** Hudaybiyyah teaches that what appears to be defeat in the present can become the foundation of victory in the future. Sometimes stepping back one pace is the way to advance ten.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih al-Bukhari). Hadith no. 2731-2732 (Kitab al-Shurut), Chapter on the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. Extended narration from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwan ibn al-Hakam.

2. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (The Prophetic Biography). Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa et al. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955. Vol. 3, Chapter on the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah.

3. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safi al-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976. Chapter on the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah.
