# The Destruction of Idols at the Ka'bah 🕋✨

---

## 🌍 What Was the Ka'bah Like Before?

Who built the Ka'bah? **Prophet Ibrahim** and his son **Prophet Ismail** *'alayhimas salam*! They built it as a house of worship for **Allah alone**.

But over hundreds of years, the people of Arabia forgot the Ka'bah's true purpose. Little by little, they brought statues and idols into the Ka'bah. They started worshipping these idols and forgot about Allah.

Eventually, there were **360 idols** in and around the Ka'bah! Some were made of stone, some of wood. The biggest one was called **Hubal** — made of red stone and shaped like a person. — a house built for Allah, filled with statues!

> 🎨 *Illustration: The Ka'bah surrounded by idols of various shapes and sizes. A gloomy atmosphere, overcast sky. In the corner, a thin beam of light piercing through the clouds — a symbol of hope. Colors: gray, dark brown, with a sliver of gold. Mood: sad but hopeful. Composition: Ka'bah in the center, idols surrounding it, thin light from above.*

---

## 🕋 The Great Cleansing!

When Prophet Muhammad ﷺ liberated Makkah, the first thing he did was go to the Ka'bah.

He circled the Ka'bah seven times on his camel. In his hand was a staff. Each time his staff pointed at an idol — **the idol fell!** Some fell flat on their faces, some tumbled backwards. One by one, all 360 idols came crashing down!

While doing this, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ recited a verse from the Quran: **"Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is ever bound to vanish!"**

After that, all the idols were taken out and destroyed. The stone ones were smashed. The wooden ones were burned. The Ka'bah was cleaned of every image and statue.

Finally, the Ka'bah was clean and pure again — just as it was when Prophet Ibrahim first built it!

> 🎨 *Illustration: Idols toppling around the Ka'bah, stone fragments scattered. The Ka'bah looks increasingly clean and radiant. The sky transitions from overcast to clear. Colors: transition from dark gray to bright gold. Mood: triumphant and purifying. Composition: Ka'bah glowing in the center, broken idols surrounding it.*

---

## 🔊 Bilal's Voice from the Top of the Ka'bah

After the Ka'bah was cleansed, a very special moment happened.

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ chose **Bilal ibn Rabah** to climb to the top of the Ka'bah and call the *adhan* — the very first call to prayer ever heard from atop the Ka'bah!

Who was Bilal? He was a **former slave** from Abyssinia (present-day Ethiopia). He had been tortured by his master for believing in Allah. A huge boulder was placed on his chest under the burning sun. But Bilal stayed strong and only said: **"Ahad! Ahad!"** (The One! The One!)

And now... the man who was once tortured stood at the **highest and most noble place** in the entire world, calling out the name of Allah! His voice echoed across all of Makkah.

This is one of Islam's most powerful messages: **no person is better than another because of their skin color, wealth, or family name**. The most honored is the one with the strongest faith and best character.

> 🎨 *Illustration: Bilal standing atop the Ka'bah, hands to his ears calling the adhan. The sun shines behind him. Below, people look up listening. Colors: gold, white, bright sky blue. Mood: grand, emotional, full of pride. Composition: Bilal on top, silhouetted against the sun, small crowd below.*

---

## 💡 What Can We Learn?

- **Restore things to their true purpose** 🏠 — The Ka'bah was built to worship Allah. When it was polluted, it had to be cleaned. Our hearts are the same — they should always be clean for Allah.

- **Truth always wins** ✨ — For hundreds of years, idols stood in the Ka'bah. But in the end, truth came and swept them all away. Be patient and trust in Allah!

- **All people are equal** 🤝 — Bilal, who was once a slave, stood at the highest point of the Ka'bah. Islam teaches that all people are equal — what matters is their faith and their goodness.

- **Prophet Muhammad continued Prophet Ibrahim's mission** 🔗 — Prophet Ibrahim built the Ka'bah, Prophet Muhammad purified it. Both shared the same mission: teaching monotheism!

> 🎨 *Illustration: Two children cleaning a room together — one sweeping, one wiping. The room transforms from messy to clean and bright. On the wall, Arabic calligraphy "La ilaha illallah." Colors: fresh green, white, yellow. Mood: cheerful and energetic. Composition: before-and-after, left side messy, right side clean and glowing.*
e27-penghancuran-berhala.children-id:
# Penghancuran Berhala di Ka'bah 🕋✨

---

## 🌍 Dulu Ka'bah Itu Seperti Apa?

Siapa yang membangun Ka'bah? **Nabi Ibrahim** dan putranya **Nabi Ismail** *'alaihimas salam*! Mereka membangunnya sebagai rumah ibadah untuk menyembah **Allah Yang Maha Esa**.

Tapi setelah berabad-abad, orang-orang Arab mulai lupa tujuan Ka'bah. Sedikit demi sedikit, mereka membawa patung-patung ke Ka'bah. Mereka mulai menyembah patung-patung itu dan melupakan Allah.

Lama-kelamaan, ada **360 berhala** di dalam dan sekitar Ka'bah! Ada yang terbuat dari batu, ada yang dari kayu. Yang paling besar namanya **Hubal** — terbuat dari batu merah, berbentuk seperti manusia. — rumah yang dibangun untuk Allah, malah dipenuhi patung-patung!

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Ka'bah dikelilingi berhala-berhala aneka bentuk dan ukuran. Suasana suram, langit mendung. Di pojok, sebuah cahaya tipis menembus awan — simbol harapan. Warna: abu-abu, coklat gelap, dengan secercah emas. Mood: sedih tapi penuh harapan. Komposisi: Ka'bah di tengah, berhala mengelilinginya, cahaya tipis dari atas.*

---

## 🕋 Hari Pembersihan Besar!

Ketika Nabi Muhammad ﷺ berhasil membebaskan Makkah, hal pertama yang beliau lakukan adalah pergi ke Ka'bah.

Beliau mengelilingi Ka'bah tujuh kali di atas untanya. Di tangannya ada tongkat. Setiap kali tongkatnya menunjuk sebuah berhala — **berhala itu jatuh!** Ada yang jatuh tertelungkup, ada yang terjungkal ke belakang. Satu per satu, semua 360 berhala roboh!

Sambil melakukan ini, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ membaca ayat al-Quran: **"Kebenaran telah datang dan kebatilan telah lenyap. Sesungguhnya kebatilan itu pasti lenyap!"**

Setelah itu, semua berhala dikeluarkan dan dihancurkan. Yang dari batu dipecahkan. Yang dari kayu dibakar. Ka'bah dibersihkan dari semua gambar dan patung.

Akhirnya, Ka'bah kembali bersih dan suci — seperti saat pertama kali dibangun oleh Nabi Ibrahim!

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Berhala-berhala berjatuhan di sekitar Ka'bah, pecahan batu berserakan. Ka'bah tampak semakin bersih dan bercahaya. Langit berubah dari mendung menjadi cerah. Warna: transisi dari abu-abu gelap ke emas terang. Mood: kemenangan dan pemurnian. Komposisi: Ka'bah bercahaya di tengah, berhala-berhala pecah di sekelilingnya.*

---

## 🔊 Suara Bilal dari Atas Ka'bah

Setelah Ka'bah bersih, terjadi momen yang sangat istimewa.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ memilih **Bilal bin Rabah** untuk naik ke atas Ka'bah dan mengumandangkan adzan — panggilan shalat yang pertama kali terdengar dari atas Ka'bah!

Siapa Bilal? Dia adalah seorang **mantan budak** dari Habasyah (Ethiopia sekarang). Dulu, dia disiksa oleh tuannya karena beriman kepada Allah. Batu besar ditaruh di dadanya di bawah terik matahari. Tapi Bilal tetap teguh dan hanya berkata: **"Ahad! Ahad!"** (Yang Maha Esa! Yang Maha Esa!)

Dan sekarang... orang yang dulu disiksa itu berdiri di **tempat paling tinggi dan paling mulia** di seluruh dunia, menyerukan nama Allah! Suaranya bergema ke seluruh Makkah.

Ini adalah pesan Islam yang sangat penting: **tidak ada manusia yang lebih mulia dari yang lain karena warna kulit, kekayaan, atau keturunan**. Yang paling mulia adalah yang paling bertakwa.

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Bilal berdiri di atas Ka'bah, tangan di telinga mengumandangkan adzan. Matahari bersinar di belakangnya. Di bawah, orang-orang menengadah mendengarkan. Warna: emas, putih, biru langit cerah. Mood: megah, emosional, penuh kebanggaan. Komposisi: Bilal di atas, siluet melawan matahari, kerumunan kecil di bawah.*

---

## 💡 Apa yang Bisa Kita Pelajari?

- **Kembalikan sesuatu pada tujuan aslinya** 🏠 — Ka'bah dibangun untuk menyembah Allah. Ketika ada yang mengotorinya, Ka'bah harus dibersihkan. Begitu juga hati kita — harus selalu bersih untuk Allah.

- **Kebenaran pasti menang** ✨ — Selama ratusan tahun berhala ada di Ka'bah. Tapi akhirnya, kebenaran datang dan menghapus semuanya. Sabar dan percaya kepada Allah!

- **Semua manusia setara** 🤝 — Bilal yang dulunya budak, berdiri paling tinggi di Ka'bah. Islam mengajarkan bahwa semua manusia sama — yang membedakan hanya keimanan dan kebaikannya.

- **Nabi Muhammad melanjutkan misi Nabi Ibrahim** 🔗 — Nabi Ibrahim membangun Ka'bah, Nabi Muhammad membersihkannya. Mereka berdua punya misi yang sama: mengajarkan tauhid!

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Dua anak membersihkan ruangan bersama — satu menyapu, satu mengelap. Ruangan berubah dari berantakan jadi bersih bercahaya. Di dinding, tulisan Arab "La ilaha illallah". Warna: hijau segar, putih, kuning. Mood: ceria dan semangat. Komposisi: sebelum-sesudah, kiri berantakan, kanan bersih bercahaya.*
e27-penghancuran-berhala.general-en:
# The Destruction of Idols at the Ka'bah: Purification of Monotheism

## Context: A Sacred House Defiled

The Ka'bah was built by **Prophet Ibrahim** and his son **Ismail** *'alayhimas salam* as a house of worship dedicated to Allah alone.^1 Over the centuries that followed, the Arabian Peninsula underwent a prolonged spiritual decline. Generation after generation, idol worship crept into Arab tradition until the Ka'bah — the house of monotheism — was filled with 360 idols.^1 ^2

Ibn Hisham in his *Sirah Nabawiyyah* records that the first person to introduce idol worship to Makkah was **Amr ibn Luhay al-Khuza'i**, the leader of Banu Khuza'ah, who encountered idol worship during a journey to al-Sham and brought back an idol named Hubal.^1 The Prophet ﷺ said, in a hadith narrated by Imam al-Bukhari:^3

> رَأَيْتُ عَمْرَو بْنَ لُحَيٍّ يَجُرُّ قُصْبَهُ فِي النَّارِ
>
> *"I saw Amr ibn Luhay dragging his intestines in the Fire."*
> — Sahih al-Bukhari, no. 3521, Kitab Ahadith al-Anbiya

Among the chief idols worshipped by the Arabs were:
- **Hubal** — the largest idol inside the Ka'bah, made of red carnelian stone, shaped like a man
- **Al-Lat** — in Ta'if, worshipped by the Thaqif tribe
- **Al-Uzza** — in the valley of Nakhlah between Makkah and Ta'if, worshipped by Quraysh
- **Manat** — on the coast between Makkah and Madinah, worshipped by the Aws and Khazraj

The Quran mentions the last three explicitly:

> أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللَّاتَ وَالْعُزَّىٰ ﴿١٩﴾ وَمَنَاةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الْأُخْرَىٰ ﴿٢٠﴾
>
> *"Have you then considered al-Lat and al-Uzza, ﴾19﴿ and Manat, the third, the other one? ﴾20﴿"*
> — QS. An-Najm (53): 19-20

Beyond statues, the Ka'bah also contained paintings. Ibn Hisham records that among the paintings inside the Ka'bah were depictions of Prophet Ibrahim and Ismail casting lots with divination arrows — a historical distortion portraying two prophets of monotheism as though engaged in pre-Islamic practices.^1 There were also images of Maryam and Isa *'alayhimas salam*.

## The Event: The Day of Purification

The destruction of the idols took place on the day of Makkah's liberation, 20 Ramadan 8 AH (January 11, 630 CE). After entering Makkah peacefully, the Prophet ﷺ went directly to the Ka'bah.

Al-Bukhari records that the Prophet ﷺ performed *tawaf* (circumambulation) seven times on his camel. With each circuit, he pointed at the idols arrayed around the Ka'bah with a staff or bow in his hand.^3 Each time his staff pointed at an idol, it fell — some toppling forward on their faces, others falling on their backs.^2

As he pointed and toppled the idols, he recited two verses of the Quran:

> جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ إِنَّ الْبَاطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًا ﴿٨١﴾
>
> *"Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is ever bound to vanish. ﴾81﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Isra (17): 81

> قُلْ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَمَا يُبْدِئُ الْبَاطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ ﴿٤٩﴾
>
> *"Say: Truth has come, and falsehood can neither originate anything nor restore it. ﴾49﴿"*
> — QS. Saba (34): 49

After the *tawaf*, the Prophet ﷺ ordered all 360 idols to be removed from the Ka'bah and destroyed.^1 ^3 Stone idols were smashed; wooden ones were burned. Hubal — the largest and most venerated idol of Quraysh — was broken in front of the Ka'bah's entrance.

Ibn Hisham records that when the Prophet ﷺ entered the Ka'bah, he found paintings on its walls. He ordered all paintings removed. According to one account by al-Azraqi in *Akhbar Makkah*, he initially shielded the paintings of Ibrahim and Maryam with his hands before ultimately ordering all images erased without exception.^1 This demonstrates that the destruction was not animosity toward art, but purification from all intermediaries in worship.

After the Ka'bah was cleansed, the Prophet ﷺ stood at its entrance and declared:^1 ^2

*"There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. He has fulfilled His promise, aided His servant, and defeated the confederates by Himself."*

He then entered the Ka'bah and prayed two *rak'ahs* inside it. Upon emerging, he ordered **Bilal ibn Rabah** to climb atop the Ka'bah and call the *adhan* — the first *adhan* ever to sound from its roof.^3 The voice of Bilal, a former slave once tortured by Quraysh for his faith, now echoed from the most sacred site on earth. This moment became a symbol of Islam's social revolution: no superiority based on race, social status, or lineage.

## Key Dialogues and Evidence: Voices on the Day of Purification

The recently defeated Qurayshi leaders reacted in varied ways. **Abu Sufyan**, who had embraced Islam only hours earlier, watched in silence. Others were stunned.

Ibn Hisham records that when Bilal called the *adhan* from atop the Ka'bah, several Qurayshi leaders commented.^1 **Attab ibn Asid** said: *"Praise be to Allah that He honored my father by not letting him witness this day."* Yet he later embraced Islam sincerely and was appointed governor of Makkah by the Prophet ﷺ.

The destruction of idols at the Ka'bah had precedent in prophetic history. Prophet Ibrahim *'alayhis salam* had also destroyed the idols of his people, as the Quran recounts:

> فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰ آلِهَتِهِمْ فَقَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ ﴿٩١﴾ مَا لَكُمْ لَا تَنطِقُونَ ﴿٩٢﴾ فَرَاغَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضَرْبًا بِالْيَمِينِ ﴿٩٣﴾
>
> *"Then he turned to their gods and said: 'Will you not eat? ﴾91﴿ What is the matter with you that you do not speak?' ﴾92﴿ Then he turned upon them, striking with his right hand. ﴾93﴿"*
> — QS. As-Saffat (37): 91-93

What Prophet Muhammad ﷺ did was the continuation and completion of Prophet Ibrahim's mission — restoring the Ka'bah to its original purpose: a house of worship for Allah alone.

## Analysis and Lessons

### Purification, Not Destruction

It is essential to understand that the destruction of idols at the Ka'bah was not vandalism or the ruin of cultural heritage — it was purification (*tathir*). The Ka'bah was built for monotheism; the idols were foreign objects that defiled it. Destroying them was restoring the Ka'bah to its original function.^1

The Quran explains the original mission of the Ka'bah's construction:

> وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنَا لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ مَكَانَ الْبَيْتِ أَن لَّا تُشْرِكْ بِي شَيْئًا وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْقَائِمِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ ﴿٢٦﴾
>
> *"And when We designated for Ibrahim the site of the House, saying: 'Do not associate anything with Me, and purify My House for those who circumambulate it, and those who stand, and those who bow and prostrate.' ﴾26﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Hajj (22): 26

### Symbol of an Old Order's Collapse

The destruction of 360 idols in a single day was a declaration that the entire pre-Islamic belief system had ended. Each idol represented an aspect of the old social order — tribal hierarchies, spiritual servitude, and injustice legitimized by "ancestral tradition." When the idols fell, the intellectual and spiritual foundations of that entire system collapsed with them.

### Bilal Atop the Ka'bah

The choice of Bilal — a Black former slave from Abyssinia — to call the *adhan* from atop the Ka'bah was no coincidence. It was a revolutionary statement: Islam recognizes no superiority based on skin color, wealth, or lineage. In the place that had for centuries symbolized Qurayshi elitism, a freed slave now stood tallest.^3

### Long-Term Impact

The destruction of idols at the Ka'bah marked a permanent turning point in the history of the Arabian Peninsula. After that day, no idol was ever erected at the Ka'bah again. Makkah, which had for centuries served as the center of Arab polytheism, became the center of monotheism that endures to this day — fourteen centuries later.

The event also inspired expeditions to destroy major idols elsewhere: **Khalid ibn al-Walid** was sent to destroy al-Uzza, **Amr ibn al-As** to destroy Suwa', and **Sa'd ibn Zayd al-Ashhali** to destroy Manat.^1 ^2 Within months, every center of idol worship in the Arabian Peninsula had vanished.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Edited by Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safi al-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih al-Bukhari). Hadith no. 3521 (Amr ibn Luhay), no. 4280-4289 (Conquest of Makkah and destruction of idols).
e27-penghancuran-berhala.general-id:
# Penghancuran Berhala di Ka'bah: Pemurnian Tauhid

## Konteks: Ka'bah yang Ternoda

Ka'bah dibangun oleh **Nabi Ibrahim** dan putranya **Ismail** *'alaihimas salam* sebagai rumah ibadah kepada Allah Yang Maha Esa.^1 Selama berabad-abad setelahnya, Jazirah Arab mengalami degradasi spiritual yang panjang. Generasi demi generasi, praktik penyembahan berhala merayap masuk ke dalam tradisi masyarakat Arab hingga akhirnya Ka'bah — rumah tauhid — justru dipenuhi oleh 360 berhala.^1 ^2

Ibn Hisham dalam *Sirah Nabawiyyah* meriwayatkan bahwa orang pertama yang memperkenalkan penyembahan berhala ke Makkah adalah **Amr bin Luhay al-Khuza'i**, pemimpin Bani Khuza'ah yang melihat praktik penyembahan berhala dalam perjalanannya ke Syam dan membawa pulang sebuah berhala bernama Hubal.^1 Rasulullah ﷺ pernah bersabda dalam hadits yang diriwayatkan Imam al-Bukhari:^3

> رَأَيْتُ عَمْرَو بْنَ لُحَيٍّ يَجُرُّ قُصْبَهُ فِي النَّارِ
>
> *"Aku melihat Amr bin Luhay menyeret ususnya di neraka."*
> — HR Bukhari, no. 3521, Kitab Ahadits al-Anbiya

Di antara berhala-berhala utama yang disembah bangsa Arab:
- **Hubal** — berhala terbesar di dalam Ka'bah, terbuat dari batu akik merah, berbentuk manusia
- **Al-Lata** — di Thaif, disembah oleh suku Tsaqif
- **Al-Uzza** — di lembah Nakhlah antara Makkah dan Thaif, disembah oleh Quraisy
- **Manat** — di pantai antara Makkah dan Madinah, disembah oleh Aus dan Khazraj

Al-Quran menyebut tiga berhala terakhir secara eksplisit:

> أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللَّاتَ وَالْعُزَّىٰ ﴿١٩﴾ وَمَنَاةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الْأُخْرَىٰ ﴿٢٠﴾
>
> *"Maka apakah kalian memperhatikan al-Lata dan al-Uzza, ﴾19﴿ dan Manat yang ketiga, yang lainnya itu? ﴾20﴿"*
> — QS. An-Najm (53): 19-20

Selain patung, di dalam Ka'bah juga terdapat lukisan-lukisan. Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa di antara lukisan di dalam Ka'bah terdapat gambar Nabi Ibrahim dan Ismail yang sedang mengundi dengan anak panah — sebuah distorsi sejarah yang menggambarkan dua nabi tauhid seolah-olah terlibat dalam praktik jahiliah.^1 Juga terdapat lukisan Maryam dan Isa *'alaihimas salam*.

## Peristiwa: Hari Pemurnian

Peristiwa penghancuran berhala terjadi pada hari pembebasan Makkah, 20 Ramadhan tahun 8 Hijriah (11 Januari 630 M). Setelah memasuki Makkah dengan damai, Rasulullah ﷺ langsung menuju Ka'bah.

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan thawaf (mengelilingi Ka'bah) tujuh kali di atas untanya. Pada setiap putaran, beliau menunjuk berhala-berhala yang berjajar di sekeliling Ka'bah dengan tongkat atau busur di tangannya.^3 Setiap kali tongkatnya menunjuk sebuah berhala, berhala itu jatuh — ada yang jatuh tertelungkup, ada yang jatuh terlentang.^2

Seraya menunjuk dan menjatuhkan berhala-berhala itu, beliau membaca dua ayat al-Quran:

> جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ إِنَّ الْبَاطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًا ﴿٨١﴾
>
> *"Kebenaran telah datang dan kebatilan telah lenyap. Sesungguhnya kebatilan itu pasti lenyap. ﴾81﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Isra (17): 81

> قُلْ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَمَا يُبْدِئُ الْبَاطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ ﴿٤٩﴾
>
> *"Katakanlah: Kebenaran telah datang, dan kebatilan tidak akan mampu memulai sesuatu dan tidak pula mengulanginya. ﴾49﴿"*
> — QS. Saba (34): 49

Setelah thawaf, Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan agar seluruh 360 berhala dikeluarkan dari Ka'bah dan dihancurkan.^1 ^3 Berhala-berhala yang terbuat dari batu dihancurkan, yang terbuat dari kayu dibakar. Hubal — berhala terbesar dan paling diagungkan oleh Quraisy — dipecahkan di depan pintu Ka'bah.

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ masuk ke dalam Ka'bah, beliau menemukan lukisan-lukisan di dindingnya. Beliau memerintahkan agar semua lukisan dihapus, kecuali — menurut satu riwayat dari al-Azraqi dalam *Akhbar Makkah* — beliau melindungi lukisan Ibrahim dan Maryam dengan tangannya sebelum akhirnya memerintahkan penghapusan semua gambar tanpa kecuali.^1 Ini menunjukkan bahwa penghancuran berhala bukan kebencian terhadap seni, melainkan pemurnian dari segala bentuk perantara dalam ibadah kepada Allah.

Setelah Ka'bah dibersihkan, Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri di pintu Ka'bah dan bersabda:^1 ^2

*"Tidak ada tuhan selain Allah semata, tidak ada sekutu bagi-Nya. Dia telah menepati janji-Nya, menolong hamba-Nya, dan mengalahkan golongan-golongan yang bersekutu sendirian."*

Beliau kemudian memasuki Ka'bah dan shalat dua rakaat di dalamnya. Ketika keluar, beliau memerintahkan **Bilal bin Rabah** untuk naik ke atas Ka'bah dan mengumandangkan adzan — azan pertama yang pernah berkumandang dari atap Ka'bah.^3 Suara Bilal, seorang mantan budak yang dulu disiksa oleh Quraisy karena keislamannya, kini bergema dari tempat paling suci di muka bumi. Momen ini menjadi simbol revolusi sosial Islam: tidak ada keunggulan berdasarkan ras, status sosial, atau keturunan.

## Dialog dan Dalil: Suara-Suara di Hari Pemurnian

Para pemimpin Quraisy yang baru saja dikalahkan memiliki reaksi beragam terhadap peristiwa ini. **Abu Sufyan**, yang baru masuk Islam beberapa jam sebelumnya, menyaksikan dalam diam. Tapi sebagian yang lain terkejut.

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Bilal mengumandangkan adzan dari atas Ka'bah, beberapa pemimpin Quraisy berkomentar.^1 **Attab bin Asid** berkata: *"Alhamdulillah, Allah telah memuliakan ayahku sehingga tidak menyaksikan hari ini."* Namun ia kemudian masuk Islam dengan tulus dan diangkat menjadi gubernur Makkah oleh Rasulullah ﷺ.

Peristiwa penghancuran berhala di Ka'bah memiliki preseden dalam sejarah kenabian. Nabi Ibrahim *'alaihis salam* juga pernah menghancurkan berhala-berhala kaumnya, sebagaimana dikisahkan dalam al-Quran:

> فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰ آلِهَتِهِمْ فَقَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ ﴿٩١﴾ مَا لَكُمْ لَا تَنطِقُونَ ﴿٩٢﴾ فَرَاغَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضَرْبًا بِالْيَمِينِ ﴿٩٣﴾
>
> *"Lalu ia pergi mendapatkan tuhan-tuhan mereka, seraya berkata: 'Apakah kamu tidak makan? ﴾91﴿ Mengapa kamu tidak berbicara?' ﴾92﴿ Kemudian ia menghancurkan mereka dengan pukulan tangan kanannya. ﴾93﴿"*
> — QS. Ash-Shaffat (37): 91-93

Apa yang dilakukan Nabi Muhammad ﷺ adalah kelanjutan dan penyempurnaan misi Nabi Ibrahim — mengembalikan Ka'bah kepada fungsi aslinya: rumah ibadah kepada Allah Yang Maha Esa.

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Pemurnian, Bukan Perusakan

Penting untuk memahami bahwa penghancuran berhala di Ka'bah bukan aksi vandal atau perusakan warisan budaya — ini adalah pemurnian*tathir*. Ka'bah dibangun untuk tauhid; berhala-berhala adalah benda asing yang mencemarinya. Menghancurkan berhala adalah mengembalikan Ka'bah kepada fungsi aslinya.^1

Al-Quran menjelaskan misi awal pembangunan Ka'bah:

> وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنَا لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ مَكَانَ الْبَيْتِ أَن لَّا تُشْرِكْ بِي شَيْئًا وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْقَائِمِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ ﴿٢٦﴾
>
> *"Dan ingatlah ketika Kami tempatkan Ibrahim di tempat Baitullah, seraya berfirman: 'Janganlah engkau mempersekutukan Aku dengan apa pun, dan sucikanlah rumah-Ku untuk orang-orang yang thawaf, yang berdiri, yang rukuk, dan yang sujud.' ﴾26﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Hajj (22): 26

### Simbol Runtuhnya Sistem Lama

Penghancuran 360 berhala dalam satu hari adalah deklarasi bahwa seluruh sistem kepercayaan jahiliah telah berakhir. Setiap berhala mewakili satu aspek dari tatanan sosial lama — hierarki kesukuan, sistem perbudakan spiritual, dan ketidakadilan yang dilegitimasi oleh "tradisi nenek moyang. " Ketika berhala-berhala itu jatuh, fondasi intelektual dan spiritual dari seluruh sistem itu runtuh bersamanya.

### Bilal di Atas Ka'bah

Pemilihan Bilal — seorang mantan budak berkulit hitam dari Habasyah — untuk mengumandangkan adzan dari atas Ka'bah bukan kebetulan. Ini adalah pernyataan revolusioner: Islam tidak mengenal superioritas berdasarkan warna kulit, kekayaan, atau garis keturunan. Di tempat yang selama berabad-abad menjadi simbol elitisme Quraisy, kini seorang budak yang dimerdekakan berdiri paling tinggi.^3

### Dampak Jangka Panjang

Penghancuran berhala di Ka'bah menjadi titik balik permanen dalam sejarah Jazirah Arab. Setelah hari itu, tidak pernah lagi berhala didirikan di Ka'bah. Makkah, yang selama berabad-abad menjadi pusat politeisme Arab, kini menjadi pusat tauhid yang tetap bertahan hingga hari ini — empat belas abad kemudian.

Peristiwa ini juga menginspirasi pengiriman ekspedisi-ekspedisi kecil untuk menghancurkan berhala-berhala besar di tempat lain: **Khalid bin al-Walid** dikirim untuk menghancurkan al-Uzza, **Amr bin al-Ash** untuk menghancurkan Suwa', dan **Sa'd bin Zaid al-Asyhali** untuk menghancurkan Manat.^1 ^2 Dalam hitungan bulan, seluruh pusat penyembahan berhala di Jazirah Arab lenyap.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Sirah Nabawiyah). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Bukhari). Hadits no. 3521 (Amr bin Luhay), no. 4280-4289 (Fath Makkah dan penghancuran berhala).

e28-perang-hunain.children-en:
# The Battle of Hunayn ⚔️

---

## ⚔️ Why Did It Happen?

After Makkah was liberated, two powerful tribes refused to accept Islam's victory — they were called **Hawazin** and **Thaqif**. Their leader, **Malik ibn Awf**, gathered a massive army to attack the Muslims.

Here's the unusual part: Malik ordered his soldiers to bring their **families, animals, and all their wealth** to the battlefield. He thought that if warriors saw their families behind them, they would fight more bravely.

An old, very experienced commander named **Duraid** warned him: *"Don't bring families! If you lose, they'll all be captured!"* But Malik wouldn't listen. Hmm... who do you think was right?

> 🎨 *Illustration: Two figures debating in front of a tent in the desert — a young, strong man (Malik) and a wise old man on a litter (Duraid). Behind them, lines of camels and families. Colors: sandy brown, sunset orange. Mood: tense, a big decision. Composition: two figures in center, rows of people and animals behind.*

---

## 🛡 What Happened?

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ set out with **12,000 soldiers** — the biggest army he had ever led! Some people felt very confident. One even said: *"There's no way we can lose with this many soldiers!"*

But then... something terrible happened!

When the Muslim army entered the **Valley of Hunayn** in the dark early morning — suddenly — **WHOOSH! WHOOSH!** — arrows rained down from every direction! The enemy had been hiding behind the hills and rocks! The Muslim soldiers panicked and ran!

But Prophet Muhammad ﷺ **did not run**. He rode forward instead! His uncle **al-Abbas**, who had an incredibly loud voice, shouted to call the soldiers back: *"O Ansar! Come back!"*

And they came back! One by one, the soldiers who had fled turned around and fought again. Allah sent peace and calm into their hearts. The battle completely turned around — Hawazin lost badly!

Duraid was right all along — the families and wealth that Hawazin brought to the battlefield were all captured. If only Malik had listened...

> 🎨 *Illustration: A narrow valley at dawn, arrows flying from hilltops. In the chaos, one glowing figure (the Prophet ﷺ, no face shown) stands firm on his mount, while a thick-bearded man (al-Abbas) shouts with hands raised. Colors: dawn gray, golden flashes. Mood: chaotic but hopeful. Composition: Prophet's figure in center, arrows from left and right, soldiers beginning to turn back.*

---

## 🕊 What Happened Next?

After winning at Hunayn, the Muslim army chased the remaining enemy to the city of **Ta'if** — a walled city high in the mountains. They besieged Ta'if for twenty days, but the city walls were too strong.

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ made a wise decision: **lift the siege and go home**. Some companions protested, but the Prophet prayed: *"O Allah, guide the people of Thaqif!"*

Happened? A few months later, the people of Ta'if **came to Madinah on their own** and accepted Islam! No war, no force. The Prophet's prayer was answered!

There was one very touching moment. When dividing the war spoils, the Prophet gave generous amounts to people who had **just recently become Muslim**, to soften their hearts. The Ansar — who had been with the Prophet for years — felt a little disappointed.

The Prophet gathered them and said: *"Are you not content that others go home with sheep and camels, while you go home with the Messenger of Allah?"*

The Ansar cried and said: **"We are content, O Messenger of Allah!"**

> 🎨 *Illustration: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (no face shown, glowing silhouette) standing among a crowd of Ansar who weep with emotion. A warm sunset setting. Some people wiping tears. Colors: warm orange, gold, soft brown. Mood: emotional and full of love. Composition: Prophet in center, surrounded by moved faces.*

---

## 💪 What Can We Learn?

- **Don't be proud of numbers** 🙏 — 12,000 soldiers nearly lost because they were overconfident. Victory comes from Allah, not from numbers!

- **True leaders step forward** 🦁 — When everyone ran, the Prophet moved forward. A good leader doesn't hide behind others when things get tough.

- **Listen to experienced advice** 👂 — Malik refused to listen to old, wise Duraid. The result? His people's families were captured. Always listen to those with more experience!

- **Sometimes stepping back is wiser** 🧠 — The Prophet lifted the siege of Ta'if because he knew it was the best decision. And the result? The people of Ta'if came to Islam on their own! Not every problem needs to be solved by force.

> 🎨 *Illustration: A child listening to their grandfather sitting in a chair. Grandfather smiles wisely, child nods seriously. In the background, a bookshelf and window light. Colors: warm brown, soft yellow, green. Mood: wise and warm. Composition: two figures on the left, light from window on the right.*
e28-perang-hunain.children-id:
# Perang Hunain ⚔️

---

## ⚔️ Kenapa?

Setelah Makkah berhasil dibebaskan, ada dua suku besar yang tidak mau menerima kemenangan Islam — namanya **Hawazin** dan **Tsaqif**. Pemimpin mereka, **Malik bin Auf**, mengumpulkan pasukan besar-besaran untuk menyerang kaum muslimin.

Yang unik, Malik memerintahkan pasukannya membawa **keluarga, hewan ternak, dan semua harta** mereka ke medan perang. Dia pikir, kalau prajurit melihat keluarganya di belakang, mereka akan bertempur lebih berani.

Seorang panglima tua yang sangat berpengalaman bernama **Duraid** menasihati: *"Jangan bawa keluarga! Kalau kalah, keluarga dan harta akan jadi tawanan!"* Tapi Malik tidak mau dengar. Hmm... siapa yang benar ya?

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Dua figur berdebat di depan tenda di padang pasir — seorang muda gagah (Malik) dan seorang tua bijak di atas tandu (Duraid). Di belakang mereka, barisan unta dan keluarga-keluarga. Warna: coklat pasir, oranye senja. Mood: tegang, keputusan besar. Komposisi: dua figur di tengah, barisan orang dan hewan di belakang.*

---

## 🛡 Ceritanya

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ berangkat dengan **12.000 pasukan** — jumlah paling besar yang pernah beliau pimpin! Beberapa orang merasa sangat percaya diri. Ada yang bahkan berkata: *"Kita tidak mungkin kalah dengan jumlah sebanyak ini!"*

Tapi ternyata... hal buruk terjadi!

Ketika pasukan muslimin memasuki **Lembah Hunain** di waktu subuh yang masih gelap, tiba-tiba — **SYUUUT! SYUUUT!** — hujan anak panah dari segala arah! Musuh sudah bersembunyi di balik bukit dan batu! Pasukan muslimin panik dan lari berhamburan!

Tapi Nabi Muhammad ﷺ **tidak lari**. Beliau justru maju ke depan! Paman beliau, **al-Abbas**, yang punya suara sangat keras, berteriak memanggil pasukan: *"Wahai kaum Anshar! Kembalilah!"*

Dan mereka kembali! Satu per satu, pasukan yang tadi lari berbalik arah dan bertempur kembali. Allah menurunkan ketenangan ke hati mereka. Pertempuran berbalik arah — Hawazin kalah telak!

Ternyata benar kata Duraid — keluarga dan harta Hawazin yang dibawa ke medan perang jadi tawanan. Kalau saja Malik mendengarkan nasihat...

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Lembah sempit di waktu fajar, anak panah beterbangan dari atas bukit. Di tengah kekacauan, satu figur bercahaya (Nabi ﷺ, tanpa wajah) tetap tegak di atas tunggangan, sementara seorang berjanggut tebal (al-Abbas) berteriak dengan tangan terangkat. Warna: abu-abu fajar, kilatan emas. Mood: kacau tapi ada harapan. Komposisi: figur Nabi di tengah, panah dari kiri-kanan, pasukan mulai berbalik.*

---

## 🕊 Setelahnya

Setelah menang di Hunain, pasukan muslimin mengejar sisa musuh ke kota **Thaif** — kota bertembok tinggi di pegunungan. Mereka mengepung Thaif selama dua puluh hari, tapi tembok kotanya terlalu kuat.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ membuat keputusan bijak: **mengangkat pengepungan dan pulang**. Beberapa sahabat protes, tapi Nabi berdoa: *"Ya Allah, berikanlah hidayah kepada Tsaqif!"*

Apa yang terjadi? Beberapa bulan kemudian, penduduk Thaif **datang sendiri** ke Madinah dan masuk Islam! Tanpa perang, tanpa paksaan. Doa Nabi terkabul!

Ada satu momen yang sangat mengharukan. Saat membagi harta rampasan perang, Nabi memberikan banyak kepada orang-orang Quraisy yang **baru masuk Islam**, agar hati mereka lembut. Kaum Anshar — yang sudah lama bersama Nabi — merasa agak kecewa.

Nabi mengumpulkan mereka dan berkata: *"Tidakkah kalian rela bahwa orang lain pulang membawa kambing dan unta, sementara kalian pulang membawa Rasulullah?"*

Kaum Anshar menangis dan berkata: **"Kami rela, ya Rasulullah!"**

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Nabi Muhammad ﷺ (tanpa wajah, siluet bercahaya) berdiri di tengah kerumunan kaum Anshar yang menangis haru. Suasana senja yang hangat. Beberapa orang mengelap air mata. Warna: jingga hangat, emas, coklat lembut. Mood: haru dan penuh cinta. Komposisi: Nabi di tengah, dikelilingi wajah-wajah yang terharu.*

---

## 💪 Teladannya

- **Jangan sombong karena jumlah** 🙏 — 12.000 pasukan hampir kalah karena terlalu percaya diri. Kemenangan datangnya dari Allah, bukan dari jumlah!

- **Pemimpin sejati maju ke depan** 🦁 — Saat semua lari, Nabi justru maju. Pemimpin yang baik tidak bersembunyi di belakang saat ada masalah.

- **Dengarkan nasihat orang berpengalaman** 👂 — Malik tidak mau dengar nasihat Duraid yang sudah tua dan bijak. Akibatnya, keluarganya jadi tawanan. Selalu dengarkan nasihat orang yang lebih berpengalaman!

- **Kadang mundur itu lebih bijak** 🧠 — Nabi mengangkat pengepungan Thaif karena tahu itu keputusan terbaik. Dan hasilnya? Penduduk Thaif datang sendiri masuk Islam! Tidak semua masalah harus diselesaikan dengan paksa.

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Seorang anak mendengarkan nasihat kakeknya yang duduk di kursi. Kakek tersenyum bijak, anak mengangguk serius. Di latar belakang, rak buku dan cahaya jendela. Warna: coklat hangat, kuning lembut, hijau. Mood: bijak dan hangat. Komposisi: dua figur di kiri, cahaya dari jendela kanan.*
e28-perang-hunain.general-en:
# The Battle of Hunayn and the Siege of Ta'if

## Context: Threat from the South

The liberation of Makkah in Ramadan 8 AH (630 CE) fundamentally altered the balance of power in the Arabian Peninsula. But not all tribes accepted this new reality. Two major tribal confederations south and southeast of Makkah — **Hawazin** and **Thaqif** — saw the rise of Islam as a direct threat to their power and way of life.^1

**Malik ibn Awf al-Nasri**, a young leader of approximately thirty years who commanded Hawazin, made a bold decision: he mobilized the full strength of Hawazin and their allies to attack the Muslims before they could consolidate their power after Fath Makkah.^1 ^2 Malik employed an unusual psychological tactic — he ordered his entire army to bring their families, wealth, and livestock to the battlefield. His logic: if the warriors saw their families behind them, they would fight to the death and none would dare flee.

This decision was harshly criticized by **Duraid ibn al-Simmah**, an ancient and vastly experienced Hawazin war commander. Duraid — carried to the battlefield on a litter due to his age exceeding a hundred years — told Malik:^1

*"By Allah, you are merely a herder of camels and sheep! Can anything stop a man who is fleeing? If your army wins, only men with swords and spears matter. But if you lose, you will have shamed your families and surrendered your wealth."*

Malik rejected Duraid's counsel — a decision that would prove catastrophic.

## The Event: Ambush in the Valley of Hunayn

The Prophet ﷺ departed from Makkah with approximately 12,000 men — the 10,000 who had participated in Fath Makkah plus 2,000 Makkans who had recently embraced Islam.^1 ^2 This was the largest army the Prophet had ever led. The sheer numbers bred overconfidence among some. Someone even declared: *"We will not be defeated today due to our small numbers."*^2

The Quran recorded this attitude and its consequences:

> لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي مَوَاطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ ۙ وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ ۙ إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ فَلَمْ تُغْنِ عَنكُمْ شَيْئًا وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ ثُمَّ وَلَّيْتُم مُّدْبِرِينَ ﴿٢٥﴾
>
> *"Allah has already given you victory in many regions and on the day of Hunayn, when your great numbers pleased you, but they did not avail you at all, and the earth was confining for you despite its vastness; then you turned back, fleeing. ﴾25﴿"*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 25

Malik ibn Awf had prepared a cunning trap. He positioned his forces in the ravines and slopes of the Hunayn Valley — a narrow valley surrounded by steep hills.^1 When the Muslim forces entered the valley at the still-dark hour of dawn, arrows suddenly rained upon them from every direction, followed by brutal cavalry charges from the flanks.

The Muslims, unprepared for the ambush, panicked and scattered.^1 ^2 Within minutes, the great army of 12,000 broke apart. Only a handful of companions remained at the Prophet's side — among them **Abu Bakr**, **Umar**, **Ali ibn Abi Talib**, **al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib**, and **Abu Sufyan ibn al-Harith** (the Prophet's cousin).

In this critical moment, the Prophet ﷺ displayed extraordinary courage. Rather than retreat, he spurred his mule toward the enemy. **Al-Abbas**, known for his thunderous voice, was ordered to rally the fleeing troops:^1 ^2

*"O Ansar! O companions of the Samurah tree (the Pledge of Ridwan)!"*

The call echoed across the entire valley. The companions who heard it immediately turned and rushed back to the Prophet's side, answering: *"Labbayk! Labbayk!"* (Here we are! Here we are!) Imam Muslim records that the battle then reversed — Hawazin, who had initially dominated, were now driven back.^3

The Quran describes the divine aid at this moment:

> ثُمَّ أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ وَعَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَنزَلَ جُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا وَعَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ﴿٢٦﴾
>
> *"Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Messenger and upon the believers, and sent down soldiers you did not see, and punished those who disbelieved."*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 26

Hawazin suffered a decisive defeat. Their warriors fled, abandoning their families, wealth, and livestock — exactly as Duraid ibn al-Simmah had feared. The Muslims captured 6,000 prisoners and seized 24,000 camels, 40,000 sheep, and a large quantity of silver.^1 ^2

## The Siege of Ta'if

The remnants of Hawazin and Thaqif retreated to the city of Ta'if — a fortress city surrounded by high walls in the Hijaz highlands.^1 The Prophet ﷺ besieged Ta'if for approximately twenty days. The Muslims employed a *dabbabah* (a leather-covered siege vehicle to protect soldiers) and a *manjaniq* (a large catapult) — the first time such siege technology was used by the Muslim army.^2

However, Ta'if's fortifications proved too formidable, and its inhabitants had prepared supplies for a prolonged siege. After consulting with his companions, the Prophet ﷺ decided to lift the siege and withdraw.^1

When **Umar ibn al-Khattab** announced the withdrawal, some companions protested. The Prophet ﷺ said: *"Then fight tomorrow."* The next day, many were wounded, and the Prophet ﷺ again said: *"We shall return tomorrow, God willing."* This time they accepted.^2 The Prophet then prayed for the people of Ta'if: *"O Allah, guide Thaqif and bring them to us."*

This prayer was answered — months later, a delegation from Thaqif came to Madinah and declared their acceptance of Islam without any fighting.^2

## Key Dialogues and Evidence: The Distribution of Spoils

One of the most compelling episodes after Hunayn was the distribution of war spoils at Ji'ranah. The Prophet ﷺ gave exceptionally large shares to the recently converted Qurayshi leaders — such as Abu Sufyan, his son Mu'awiyah, and Safwan ibn Umayyah — to soften their hearts toward Islam (*ta'lif al-qulub*).^1 ^2

This decision disappointed some of the Ansar. They felt sidelined — those who had stood with the Prophet from the earliest days received less than those who had only just embraced Islam. When word of this reached the Prophet ﷺ, he gathered the Ansar and delivered a deeply moving address:^1 ^2

*"O Ansar, did I not come to you when you were astray, and Allah guided you through me? Were you not poor, and Allah enriched you? Were you not divided, and Allah united your hearts?"*

They answered: *"Allah and His Messenger are the most gracious."*

The Prophet ﷺ continued:

*"O Ansar, could you not reply — and you would be truthful — that you came to us rejected, and we believed you; that you came abandoned, and we helped you; that you came expelled, and we sheltered you?"*

Then he spoke in a voice that shook their hearts:

*"Are you not content that people go home with sheep and camels, while you go home with the Messenger of Allah? By Him in Whose hand is Muhammad's soul, were it not for the Hijrah, I would be one of the Ansar. If mankind took one valley and the Ansar took another, I would take the valley of the Ansar."*^1 ^2

The Ansar wept until their beards were soaked, and they said: *"We are content with the Messenger of Allah as our share."*

## Analysis and Lessons

### The Lesson of the Initial Defeat

Hunayn teaches a vital lesson: numbers and material strength do not guarantee victory. When the Muslims took pride in their 12,000 warriors, they nearly lost. Victory came not from numbers, but from divine aid and steadfast faith.

### Courage in Leadership

At the most critical moment — when nearly the entire army had fled — the Prophet ﷺ advanced toward the front line. This is the highest principle of leadership: the leader stands at the front in times of danger, not at the rear. His courage inspired the troops to return and reverse the tide of battle.

### Diplomacy of the Heart

The unequal distribution of spoils was not injustice — it was a strategic investment. By softening the hearts of recently converted Qurayshi leaders, the Prophet ﷺ secured their long-term loyalty. Meanwhile, the Ansar — whose faith was already firm — received something far more precious: closeness to the Prophet of Allah.

### The Wisdom of Lifting the Siege

The decision to withdraw from Ta'if demonstrates mature military wisdom. The Prophet ﷺ was not trapped by ego or prestige — when the situation was unfavorable, he withdrew with honor. And the result proved superior: Thaqif ultimately embraced Islam voluntarily, without resentment or coercion.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Edited by Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safi al-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Muslim, Abu al-Husayn Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih Muslim). Kitab al-Jihad wa al-Siyar, chapter on Ghazwah Hunayn.
e28-perang-hunain.general-id:
# Perang Hunain dan Pengepungan Thaif

## Konteks: Ancaman dari Selatan

Pembebasan Makkah pada Ramadhan tahun 8 Hijriah (630 M) mengubah keseimbangan kekuatan di Jazirah Arab secara fundamental. Namun tidak semua suku menerima kenyataan baru ini. Dua konfederasi suku besar di sebelah selatan dan tenggara Makkah — **Hawazin** dan **Tsaqif** — melihat kebangkitan Islam sebagai ancaman langsung terhadap kekuasaan dan cara hidup mereka.^1

**Malik bin Auf al-Nashri**, seorang pemuda berusia sekitar tiga puluh tahun yang memimpin Hawazin, mengambil keputusan berani: ia menghimpun seluruh kekuatan Hawazin dan sekutunya untuk menyerang kaum muslimin sebelum mereka sempat mengonsolidasi kekuatan pasca-Fath Makkah.^1 ^2 Malik menggunakan taktik psikologis yang tidak lazim — ia memerintahkan seluruh pasukannya membawa keluarga, harta, dan ternak mereka ke medan perang. Logikanya: jika para prajurit melihat keluarga mereka di belakang, mereka akan bertempur mati-matian dan tidak ada yang berani melarikan diri.

Namun keputusan ini dikritik keras oleh **Duraid bin al-Shimmah**, seorang panglima perang Hawazin yang sudah sangat tua dan berpengalaman. Duraid — yang dibawa ke medan perang di atas tandu karena usianya yang sudah lebih dari seratus tahun — berkata kepada Malik:^1

*"Demi Allah, engkau hanyalah penggembala unta dan kambing! Apakah ada sesuatu yang bisa menghentikan orang yang melarikan diri? Jika pasukanmu menang, hanya prajurit berpedang dan bertombak yang berguna. Tapi jika kalah, engkau telah mempermalukan keluargamu dan menyerahkan hartamu."*

Malik menolak nasihat Duraid, sebuah keputusan yang kelak terbukti mahal.

## Peristiwa: Penyergapan di Lembah Hunain

Rasulullah ﷺ berangkat dari Makkah dengan pasukan berjumlah sekitar 12.000 orang — 10.000 pasukan yang ikut Fath Makkah ditambah 2.000 penduduk Makkah yang baru masuk Islam.^1 ^2 Ini adalah pasukan terbesar yang pernah dipimpin beliau. Jumlah yang besar ini membuat sebagian sahabat merasa percaya diri berlebihan. Seseorang bahkan berkata: *"Kita tidak akan kalah hari ini karena sedikitnya jumlah kita."*^2

Al-Quran merekam sikap ini dan konsekuensinya:

> لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي مَوَاطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ ۙ وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ ۙ إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ فَلَمْ تُغْنِ عَنكُمْ شَيْئًا وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ ثُمَّ وَلَّيْتُم مُّدْبِرِينَ ﴿٢٥﴾
>
> *"Sesungguhnya Allah telah menolong kamu dalam banyak medan perang, dan pada Hari Hunain, ketika jumlahmu yang besar itu membanggakanmu, ternyata jumlah itu tidak berguna bagimu sedikit pun, dan bumi yang luas itu terasa sempit bagimu, kemudian kamu berbalik melarikan diri. ﴾25﴿"*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 25

Malik bin Auf telah menyiapkan perangkap yang cerdik. Ia menyusun pasukannya di celah-celah dan lereng-lereng Lembah Hunain — sebuah lembah sempit yang dikelilingi bukit-bukit curam.^1 Ketika pasukan muslim memasuki lembah di waktu fajar yang masih gelap, tiba-tiba hujan anak panah menghujani mereka dari segala arah, disusul serangan kavaleri yang brutal dari samping.

Pasukan muslimin yang tidak siap menghadapi penyergapan ini langsung panik dan lari cerai-berai.^1 ^2 Dalam hitungan menit, pasukan besar yang berjumlah 12.000 itu buyar. Hanya segelintir sahabat yang tetap bertahan di sisi Rasulullah ﷺ — di antaranya **Abu Bakr**, **Umar**, **Ali bin Abi Thalib**, **al-Abbas bin Abdul Muthalib**, dan **Abu Sufyan bin al-Harits** (sepupu Nabi).

Dalam momen kritis itu, Rasulullah ﷺ menunjukkan keberanian luar biasa. Alih-alih mundur, beliau justru memacu bighalnya (keledai besar) ke arah musuh. **Al-Abbas**, yang dikenal memiliki suara yang sangat keras, diperintahkan untuk memanggil pasukan yang melarikan diri:^1 ^2

*"Wahai kaum Anshar! Wahai para sahabat pohon Samurah (Bai'at Ridwan)!"*

Seruan ini menggema di seluruh lembah. Para sahabat yang mendengarnya segera berbalik dan bergegas kembali ke sisi Rasulullah ﷺ, menjawab: *"Labbaik! Labbaik!"* (Kami penuhi! Kami penuhi!) Imam Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa pertempuran kemudian berbalik arah — pasukan Hawazin yang semula mendominasi kini terdesak mundur.^3

Al-Quran menggambarkan pertolongan ilahi di momen ini:

> ثُمَّ أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ وَعَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَنزَلَ جُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا وَعَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ﴿٢٦﴾
>
> *"Kemudian Allah menurunkan ketenangan-Nya kepada Rasul-Nya dan kepada orang-orang yang beriman, dan menurunkan tentara yang tidak kamu lihat, dan Dia mengazab orang-orang kafir."*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 26

Hawazin mengalami kekalahan telak. Pasukan mereka melarikan diri meninggalkan keluarga, harta, dan ternak mereka — persis seperti yang dikhawatirkan Duraid bin al-Shimmah. Kaum muslimin menawan 6.000 orang dan merampas 24.000 unta, 40.000 kambing, dan sejumlah besar perak.^1 ^2

## Pengepungan Thaif

Sisa pasukan Hawazin dan Tsaqif mundur ke kota Thaif — sebuah kota yang dikelilingi benteng tinggi di dataran tinggi Hijaz.^1 Rasulullah ﷺ mengepung Thaif selama sekitar dua puluh hari. Kaum muslimin menggunakan *dababah* (kendaraan perang berlapis kulit untuk melindungi prajurit) dan *manjaniq* (ketapel besar) — pertama kalinya teknologi pengepungan semacam ini digunakan oleh pasukan muslim.^2

Namun benteng Thaif terlalu kokoh, dan penduduknya telah menyiapkan perbekalan untuk bertahan lama. Setelah berkonsultasi dengan para sahabat, Rasulullah ﷺ memutuskan untuk mengangkat pengepungan dan kembali.^1

Ketika **Umar bin al-Khaththab** mengumumkan bahwa pasukan akan kembali, beberapa sahabat protes. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: *"Maka berperanglah besok."* Keesokan harinya, banyak yang terluka, dan Rasulullah ﷺ kembali berkata: *"Kita akan kembali besok insya Allah."* Kali ini mereka menerima keputusan itu.^2 Nabi kemudian mendoakan penduduk Thaif: *"Ya Allah, berikanlah hidayah kepada Tsaqif dan datangkanlah mereka."*

Doa ini terkabul — beberapa bulan kemudian, delegasi Tsaqif datang ke Madinah dan menyatakan masuk Islam tanpa pertempuran.^2

## Dialog dan Dalil: Pembagian Rampasan Perang

Salah satu episode paling menarik setelah Hunain adalah pembagian harta rampasan di Ji'ranah. Rasulullah ﷺ memberikan bagian yang sangat besar kepada para pemimpin Quraisy yang baru masuk Islam — seperti Abu Sufyan, anaknya Mu'awiyah, dan Shafwan bin Umayyah — untuk melunakkan hati mereka (*ta'lif al-qulub*).^1 ^2

Keputusan ini membuat sebagian kaum Anshar kecewa. Mereka merasa dipinggirkan — mereka yang telah berjuang bersama Nabi sejak awal justru mendapat lebih sedikit daripada orang-orang yang baru saja masuk Islam. Kabar ini sampai kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, yang kemudian mengumpulkan kaum Anshar dan menyampaikan khutbah yang mengharukan:^1 ^2

*"Wahai kaum Anshar, bukankah aku mendatangi kalian dalam keadaan sesat lalu Allah memberi kalian hidayah melaluiku? Bukankah kalian dalam keadaan miskin lalu Allah menjadikan kalian kecukupan? Bukankah kalian bercerai-berai lalu Allah menyatukan hati kalian?"*

Mereka menjawab: *"Allah dan Rasul-Nya yang paling berjasa."*

Rasulullah ﷺ melanjutkan:

*"Wahai kaum Anshar, bukankah kalian bisa menjawab — dan kalian akan benar — bahwa engkau datang kepada kami dalam keadaan didustakan, lalu kami membenarkanmu; dalam keadaan ditolak, lalu kami menolongmu; dalam keadaan diusir, lalu kami memberikanmu tempat?"*

Kemudian beliau berkata dengan suara yang menggetarkan:

*"Tidakkah kalian rela bahwa orang-orang itu pulang membawa kambing dan unta, sementara kalian pulang membawa Rasulullah ke rumah-rumah kalian? Demi Yang jiwa Muhammad di tangan-Nya, seandainya bukan karena hijrah, niscaya aku adalah seorang Anshari. Jika manusia melewati satu lembah dan kaum Anshar melewati lembah yang lain, niscaya aku akan melewati lembah kaum Anshar."*^1 ^2

Kaum Anshar menangis hingga jenggot mereka basah, dan mereka berkata: *"Kami rela dengan Rasulullah sebagai bagian kami."*

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Pelajaran dari Kekalahan Awal

Hunain mengajarkan pelajaran yang sangat penting: jumlah dan kekuatan material tidak menjamin kemenangan. Ketika kaum muslimin bangga dengan jumlah mereka yang 12.000, mereka justru hampir kalah. Kemenangan datang bukan dari jumlah, melainkan dari pertolongan Allah dan keteguhan iman.

### Keberanian Pemimpin

Dalam momen paling kritis — ketika hampir seluruh pasukan melarikan diri — Rasulullah ﷺ justru maju ke garis depan. Ini adalah prinsip kepemimpinan tertinggi: pemimpin berdiri di depan saat bahaya, bukan di belakang. Keberaniannya menginspirasi pasukan untuk kembali dan membalikkan keadaan.

### Diplomasi Hati

Pembagian rampasan perang yang tidak merata bukan ketidakadilan — itu adalah investasi strategis. Dengan melunakkan hati para pemimpin Quraisy yang baru masuk Islam, Rasulullah ﷺ mengamankan loyalitas mereka dalam jangka panjang. Sementara kaum Anshar — yang imannya sudah kokoh — mendapat sesuatu yang jauh lebih berharga: kedekatan dengan Rasulullah ﷺ.

### Kebijaksanaan Mengangkat Pengepungan

Keputusan untuk mengangkat pengepungan Thaif menunjukkan kebijaksanaan militer yang matang. Rasulullah ﷺ tidak terjebak dalam ego atau gengsi — ketika situasi tidak menguntungkan, beliau mundur dengan terhormat. Dan hasilnya terbukti lebih baik: Tsaqif akhirnya masuk Islam secara sukarela, tanpa dendam dan tanpa paksaan.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Sirah Nabawiyah). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Muslim, Abu al-Husain Muslim bin al-Hajjaj.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Muslim). Kitab al-Jihad wa al-Siyar, bab Ghazwah Hunain.

e29-amul-wufud.children-en:
# The Year of Delegations 🕌

---

## Makkah Now Belongs to Islam

After Prophet Muhammad ﷺ entered the city of Makkah peacefully, the entire Arabian Peninsula was amazed. Makkah was the most important city for the Arabs — and now its people had embraced Islam!

Tribes that had been watching from afar began to think: *"If the Quraysh — the most powerful tribe — have accepted Islam, then this religion must be true."*

And so began a very special year called **'Am al-Wufud** — the Year of Delegations. Tribes from every corner of Arabia sent their representatives to Madinah to meet Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

🎨 *Illustration: A bustling city of Madinah, with groups on horses and camels arriving from different directions. A clear blue sky, dominant colors of gold and green. Festive and hopeful atmosphere. Wide panoramic composition showing the lively streets of Madinah.*

---

## Guests from All Over Arabia

Delegations came one after another. Some traveled from the sandy deserts of Najd, others from the mountains of Yemen, and still others from the coast of Oman. They all wanted to meet the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in person.

One of the delegations that arrived was the **Banu Tamim** — a very large tribe. They came very confidently and called out to the Prophet from outside his room in loud voices. Allah reminded them in the Quran that this was not polite behavior.

There was also a delegation from **Ta'if** — the city whose people had once thrown stones at the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ until he bled! But now, they came in peace to declare their Islam. How great was the Prophet's heart — he welcomed them with a smile, without any grudge at all.

The most unique was the delegation from **Najran** — they were Christians. They discussed religion with the Prophet ﷺ. Even though they didn't agree, the Prophet ﷺ still respected them and made a peace treaty.

🎨 *Illustration: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (no face shown, depicted with light) sitting in the Madinah mosque receiving guests from various tribes in different clothing. Some wear white turbans, others colorful robes. Dominant colors of olive green and cream. Warm and friendly atmosphere. Circular composition with the Prophet at the center.*

---

## Letters to the Kings

Did you know? Before the delegations came, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had already sent **letters** to kings and rulers in various lands — the Roman Emperor, the Persian King, the Ruler of Egypt, and many more!

These letters contained an invitation to learn about Islam. The Prophet ﷺ did not force anyone — he invited them politely and wisely. This is what attracted many people to eventually send delegations to Madinah.

Allah says that there is no compulsion in religion: **"There shall be no compulsion in religion. The right course has become clear from the wrong."** (QS Al-Baqarah: 256)

🎨 *Illustration: A letter with the Prophet's seal stamp reading "Muhammad Rasulullah" on a wooden table. In the background, a simple map of the Arabian Peninsula. Dominant colors of warm brown and gold. An atmosphere of dignity and grandeur. Close-up composition of the letter with blurred background.*

---

## Lessons from the Year of Delegations

*'Am al-Wufud* teaches us many important things:

🤝 **Respect everyone** — The Prophet ﷺ welcomed every delegation with honor, even those who came with arrogance.

🕊️ **Never force anyone** — Islam was spread through kind invitation and beautiful example, not compulsion.

🌍 **Unity in diversity** — Tribes that were once enemies could unite because of one shared belief.

💪 **Forgive and be open-hearted** — The Prophet ﷺ welcomed the delegation from Ta'if, who had once hurt him. What a great heart he had!

— in just one year, almost the entire Arabian Peninsula united under the banner of Islam. Not because of war, but because of the kindness and the truth of Prophet Muhammad's message!

🎨 *Illustration: A colorful map of the Arabian Peninsula, with arrows from different directions pointing toward Madinah in the center. Each arrow represents a different tribe. Dominant colors of green, blue, and gold. An atmosphere of energy and unity. Child-friendly map composition that is cheerful and easy to understand.*
e29-amul-wufud.children-id:
# Tahun Delegasi 🕌

---

## Makkah Sudah Menjadi Milik Islam

Setelah Nabi Muhammad ﷺ berhasil memasuki kota Makkah tanpa pertempuran besar, seluruh Jazirah Arab tersentak. Makkah adalah kota paling penting bagi bangsa Arab — dan sekarang penduduknya sudah memeluk Islam!

Suku-suku yang selama ini menunggu dan mengamati mulai berpikir: *"Kalau Quraisy — suku yang paling kuat — saja sudah masuk Islam, berarti agama ini memang benar."*

Maka dimulailah tahun yang sangat istimewa, yang disebut **'Am al-Wufud** — Tahun Delegasi. Suku-suku dari seluruh penjuru Jazirah Arab mengirimkan utusan mereka ke Madinah untuk menemui Nabi Muhammad ﷺ.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Kota Madinah yang ramai, dengan rombongan-rombongan berkuda dan berunta datang dari berbagai arah. Langit cerah berwarna biru, warna dominan emas dan hijau. Suasana meriah dan penuh harapan. Komposisi panorama lebar menunjukkan jalan-jalan Madinah yang hidup.*

---

## Tamu-Tamu dari Seluruh Jazirah Arab

Delegasi datang silih berganti. Ada yang datang dari gurun pasir Najd, ada yang dari pegunungan Yaman, ada juga yang dari pesisir Oman. Mereka semua ingin bertemu langsung dengan Nabi Muhammad ﷺ.

Salah satu delegasi yang datang adalah **Bani Tamim** — suku yang sangat besar. Mereka datang dengan percaya diri tinggi dan memanggil-manggil Nabi dari luar kamar beliau dengan suara keras. Allah mengingatkan mereka dalam al-Quran bahwa itu bukan cara yang sopan.

Ada juga delegasi dari **Thaif** — kota yang dulu pernah melempari Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dengan batu sampai berdarah! Tapi sekarang, mereka datang dengan damai untuk menyatakan keislaman. Betapa besarnya hati Nabi ﷺ — beliau menerima mereka dengan senyuman, tanpa dendam sama sekali.

Yang paling unik adalah delegasi dari **Najran** — mereka adalah orang Nasrani (Kristen). Mereka berdiskusi tentang agama dengan Nabi ﷺ. Meskipun tidak sepakat, Nabi ﷺ tetap menghormati mereka dan membuat perjanjian damai.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Nabi Muhammad ﷺ (tanpa wajah, digambarkan dengan cahaya) duduk di masjid Madinah menerima tamu-tamu dari berbagai suku berpakaian berbeda. Ada yang bersorban putih, ada yang memakai jubah berwarna-warni. Warna dominan hijau zaitun dan krem. Suasana hangat dan bersahabat. Komposisi melingkar dengan Nabi di tengah.*

---

## Surat-Surat untuk Para Raja

Sebelum para delegasi datang, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ sudah terlebih dahulu mengirim **surat-surat** kepada raja-raja dan pemimpin di berbagai negeri — Raja Romawi, Raja Persia, Penguasa Mesir, dan banyak lagi!

Surat-surat itu berisi ajakan untuk mengenal Islam. Nabi ﷺ tidak memaksa siapa pun — beliau mengajak dengan sopan dan penuh hikmah. Inilah yang membuat banyak orang tertarik dan akhirnya mengirim delegasi ke Madinah.

Allah berfirman bahwa tidak ada paksaan dalam beragama: **"Tidak ada paksaan dalam beragama. Sungguh, telah jelas jalan yang benar dari jalan yang sesat."** (QS Al-Baqarah: 256)

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Sebuah surat dengan cap stempel Nabi bertuliskan "Muhammad Rasulullah" di atas meja kayu. Di latar belakang terlihat peta sederhana Jazirah Arab. Warna dominan cokelat hangat dan emas. Suasana penuh wibawa dan keagungan. Komposisi close-up surat dengan latar belakang blur.*

---

## Pelajaran dari Tahun Delegasi

*'Am al-Wufud* mengajarkan kita banyak hal penting:

🤝 **Hormati semua orang** — Nabi ﷺ menyambut setiap delegasi dengan hormat, bahkan yang datang dengan sikap sombong.

🕊️ **Jangan memaksa** — Islam disebarkan melalui ajakan yang baik dan teladan yang indah, bukan paksaan.

🌍 **Persatuan dalam keberagaman** — Suku-suku yang tadinya saling bermusuhan bisa bersatu karena satu keyakinan yang sama.

💪 **Maafkan dan berlapang dada** — Nabi ﷺ menerima delegasi Thaif yang dulu pernah menyakiti beliau. Betapa besar hati beliau!

— dalam satu tahun saja, hampir seluruh Jazirah Arab bersatu di bawah panji Islam. Bukan karena perang, tapi karena kebaikan hati dan kebenaran risalah Nabi Muhammad ﷺ!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Peta Jazirah Arab yang berwarna-warni, dengan panah-panah dari berbagai arah menuju Madinah di tengah. Setiap panah mewakili suku yang berbeda. Warna dominan hijau, biru, dan emas. Suasana penuh semangat dan persatuan. Komposisi peta bergaya anak-anak yang ceria dan mudah dipahami.*
e29-amul-wufud.general-en:
# 'Am al-Wufud: The Year of Delegations

## Historical Context: Arabia After the Conquest of Makkah

The Conquest of Makkah in 8 AH (630 CE) fundamentally altered the political landscape of the Arabian Peninsula. Makkah — the spiritual, economic, and social center of the Arabs — now stood under the banner of Islam. For the tribes that had been watching from a distance, the fall of Makkah was a turning point that could no longer be ignored.^1

Ibn Hisham in his *Sirah Nabawiyyah* explains the reasoning that developed among the Arab tribes: they had said, *"Leave Muhammad and his people (the Quraysh). If he prevails over them, then he is indeed a true prophet."*^1 When Makkah was finally conquered with virtually no bloodshed, and the Quraysh — the most prestigious tribe in all of Arabia — accepted Islam, the other tribes lost their primary reason for resistance.

Al-Mubarakfuri in *al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* notes that the Conquest of Makkah was followed by victory at the Battle of Hunayn and the siege of Ta'if, which further consolidated Islamic authority in the Hijaz region.^2 No significant military force remained capable of challenging Madinah. It was this context that gave rise to the phenomenon of *'Am al-Wufud* — the year in which delegation after delegation traveled to Madinah to declare their acceptance of Islam.

The Quran itself immortalized this phenomenon in Surah An-Nasr:

> إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَالْفَتْحُ ﴿١﴾ وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا ﴿٢﴾ فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا ﴿٣﴾
>
> *"When the help of Allah comes and the conquest, ﴾1﴿ and you see the people entering the religion of Allah in multitudes, ﴾2﴿ then glorify the praises of your Lord and seek His forgiveness. Indeed, He is ever Accepting of repentance. ﴾3﴿"*
> — QS. An-Nasr (110): 1-3

## The Events: Waves of Delegations to Madinah

The year 9 AH (631 CE) became known as *'Am al-Wufud* (the Year of Delegations). Ibn Sa'd in *Tabaqat al-Kubra* records that the number of delegations arriving in Madinah was immense, involving dozens of tribes from every corner of the Arabian Peninsula.^3 Among the most significant:

### The Delegation of Banu Tamim

Banu Tamim was one of the largest tribes in Arabia, residing in the Najd region. They sent a delegation led by several of their prominent figures. Ibn Hisham relates that this delegation arrived with an arrogant disposition — they called out to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from behind his private chambers in loud voices.^1

This incident was recorded in the Quran:

> إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُنَادُونَكَ مِن وَرَاءِ الْحُجُرَاتِ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ ﴿٤﴾
>
> *"Indeed, those who call you from behind the chambers — most of them do not understand. ﴾4﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Hujurat (49): 4

Yet after dialogue with the Prophet ﷺ and hearing the Quran, they accepted Islam and returned to their people as Muslims.

### The Delegation of Banu 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'ah

This tribe sent its delegation to learn about Islam firsthand. Among them was **'Amir ibn al-Tufayl**, a leader with political ambitions — he wished to be granted authority after the Prophet ﷺ. When the Prophet declined this demand, 'Amir refused to accept Islam. However, the majority of the delegation's other members embraced the faith.^1 ^2

### The Delegation of Thaqif from Ta'if

The Thaqif delegation carried deep emotional significance. Ta'if was the city that had once expelled and injured the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ during the early period of his mission — its people pelted him with stones until he bled. Now, after the siege of Ta'if following the Battle of Hunayn and after witnessing all of Arabia gravitating toward Islam, the Thaqif finally dispatched a delegation to Madinah.^1 ^3

Al-Mubarakfuri records that the Thaqif delegation initially proposed several conditions — including that their idol, al-Lat, not be destroyed for three years, and that they be exempted from the obligation of prayer. The Prophet ﷺ rejected every condition that contradicted the principle of *tawhid* (monotheism), though he agreed that they would not have to destroy the idol with their own hands — he sent Abu Sufyan and al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah to accomplish the task.^2

### The Delegation from Najran (Christians)

One of the most remarkable delegations was that from Najran, an Arab Christian community. Ibn Hisham records that the delegation consisted of approximately sixty people, led by their bishop, **Abu Harithah**.^1

They debated with the Prophet ﷺ regarding the nature of Jesus Christ. When no theological agreement was reached, the verse of *mubahalah* (mutual invocation of divine curse) was revealed:

> فَمَنْ حَاجَّكَ فِيهِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ فَقُلْ تَعَالَوْا نَدْعُ أَبْنَاءَنَا وَأَبْنَاءَكُمْ وَنِسَاءَنَا وَنِسَاءَكُمْ وَأَنفُسَنَا وَأَنفُسَكُمْ ثُمَّ نَبْتَهِلْ فَنَجْعَل لَّعْنَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْكَاذِبِينَ ﴿٦١﴾
>
> *"Then whoever argues with you about it after knowledge has come to you — say, 'Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves, then supplicate earnestly and invoke the curse of Allah upon the liars.' ﴾61﴿"*
> — QS. Ali Imran (3): 61

The Najran delegation ultimately declined the *mubahalah* and chose instead to establish a peace treaty with the Prophet ﷺ — they maintained their faith while paying the *jizyah* and living under the protection of the Islamic state.^1 This event set a crucial precedent for interfaith relations in Islam.

### Other Delegations

Beyond those mentioned above, Ibn Sa'd in *Tabaqat al-Kubra* records dozens of additional delegations, including:^3

- **Banu Hanifah** from Yamamah, among whose members was Musaylimah, who would later claim prophethood
- **Banu Asad**, who sent a delegation announcing their conversion
- **Yemeni tribes** — including delegations from Himyar, Kindah, and Hadhramaut
- **A delegation from Oman** — led by the two brothers Jayfar and 'Abbad, sons of al-Julanda

Each delegation was received by the Prophet ﷺ with full hospitality. He provided them lodging, listened to their concerns, answered their questions about Islam, and dispatched teachers to accompany them home to instruct their people in the Quran and Islamic law.

## Diplomacy of the Prophet: Letters to Rulers

This wave of delegations did not occur purely spontaneously. Prior to *'Am al-Wufud*, the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had dispatched diplomatic letters to rulers and tribal leaders across the Arabian Peninsula and beyond. Ibn Sa'd records that he sent letters to the Roman Emperor (Heraclius), the Persian King (Khosrow), the ruler of Egypt (Muqawqis), the King of Abyssinia (Negus), as well as numerous Arab tribal chiefs.^3

These letters demonstrated that the Islamic call was not merely a local spiritual movement, but a universal mission. The Prophet ﷺ invited all of humanity to *tawhid* — regardless of race, tribe, or social standing.

## Analysis: Why Did the Tribes Accept Islam?

Historians identify several factors that drove the mass acceptance of Islam in 9 AH:

**First, the theological factor.** Many Arabs had long harbored doubts about idol worship. They were called *hunafa'* — seekers of truth who rejected polytheism but had not yet found a clear path. Islam offered a lucid and logical monotheism that resonated with the natural human disposition*fitrah*.

**Second, the political-military factor.** After the Conquest of Makkah and the victory at Hunayn, no military force in Arabia could challenge Madinah. Accepting Islam meant joining a rising power — not submitting to an oppressive conqueror, for Islam offered equality and brotherhood.

**Third, the socio-economic factor.** Islam abolished rigid tribal hierarchies. A believing former slave was nobler than an unbelieving nobleman. This message was profoundly revolutionary for a society that had been locked in rigid tribal stratification for centuries.

**Fourth, the moral factor.** The personal example of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ — his honesty, justice, gentleness, even toward his enemies — proved irresistibly compelling. Al-Mubarakfuri notes that many delegations accepted Islam not out of coercion or political calculation, but because they were moved by the Prophet's character.^2

## Lessons from 'Am al-Wufud

### Diplomacy and Dialogue, Not Coercion

*'Am al-Wufud* demonstrates that the spread of Islam fundamentally proceeded through diplomacy, dialogue, and personal example — not through the sword. The vast majority of Arab tribes accepted Islam voluntarily after sending delegations, engaging in dialogue with the Prophet ﷺ, and becoming convinced of his message's truth. This aligns with Allah's declaration:

> لَا إِكْرَاهَ فِي الدِّينِ ۖ قَد تَّبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِّ ﴿٢٥٦﴾
>
> *"There shall be no compulsion in religion. The right course has become clear from the wrong. ﴾256﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Baqarah (2): 256

### Respecting Diversity

The Prophet's treatment of the Najran delegation demonstrates that Islam acknowledges the reality of religious diversity. Those who declined to accept Islam were not coerced — they were granted protection and civil rights under a clear treaty. This was a remarkably advanced model of coexistence for its era.

### Unity Amid Tribal Diversity

Before Islam, the Arabian Peninsula was fragmented by ceaseless inter-tribal rivalries. *'Am al-Wufud* witnessed tribes from every direction — from Najd to Yemen, from Oman to Bahrain — uniting under a single faith. The brotherhood of belief replaced tribal fanaticism*'asabiyyah*. The Prophet ﷺ said:

> لَيْسَ مِنَّا مَنْ دَعَا إِلَى عَصَبِيَّةٍ
>
> *"He is not one of us who calls to tribal fanaticism."*
> — Narrated by Abu Dawud, no. 5121

### Servant Leadership

In receiving dozens of delegations from across Arabia, the Prophet ﷺ exemplified servant leadership — listening, understanding, respecting, and guiding. He did not compel, insult, or demean. Even toward delegations that arrived with arrogance, such as Banu Tamim, he remained patient and welcoming.

---

## References

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safiur-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Vols. 1-2. Beirut: Dar Sadir.
