# 'Am al-Wufud: Tahun Delegasi Suku-Suku Masuk Islam

## Konteks Historis: Jazirah Arab Setelah Fathu Makkah

Peristiwa Fathu Makkah pada tahun 8 Hijriyah (630 M) mengubah seluruh peta kekuatan politik di Jazirah Arab. Makkah — pusat spiritual, ekonomi, dan sosial bangsa Arab — kini berada di bawah panji Islam. Bagi suku-suku Arab yang selama ini menunggu dan mengamati dari kejauhan, jatuhnya Makkah adalah titik balik yang tidak bisa diabaikan.^1

Ibn Hisham dalam *Sirah Nabawiyyah* menjelaskan logika yang berkembang di kalangan suku-suku Arab saat itu: mereka mengatakan, *"Biarkan Muhammad dan kaumnya (Quraisy). Jika ia menang atas mereka, berarti ia memang seorang nabi yang benar."*^1 Ketika Makkah akhirnya ditaklukkan tanpa pertumpahan darah yang berarti, dan kaum Quraisy — suku paling berpengaruh di Jazirah Arab — menerima Islam, maka suku-suku lain pun kehilangan alasan untuk menolak.

Al-Mubarakfuri dalam *al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* menegaskan bahwa Fathu Makkah diikuti oleh kemenangan di Perang Hunain dan pengepungan Thaif, yang semakin mengonsolidasikan kekuatan Islam di wilayah Hijaz.^2 Tidak ada lagi kekuatan militer signifikan yang mampu menentang Madinah. Konteks inilah yang melahirkan fenomena *'Am al-Wufud* — tahun di mana delegasi demi delegasi datang ke Madinah untuk menyatakan keislaman mereka.

Al-Quran sendiri mengabadikan fenomena ini dalam Surah An-Nashr:

> إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَالْفَتْحُ ﴿١﴾ وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا ﴿٢﴾ فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا ﴿٣﴾
>
> *"Apabila telah datang pertolongan Allah dan kemenangan, ﴾1﴿ dan engkau melihat manusia masuk ke dalam agama Allah secara berbondong-bondong, ﴾2﴿ maka bertasbihlah dengan memuji Tuhanmu dan mohonlah ampunan kepada-Nya. Sungguh, Dia Maha Penerima tobat. ﴾3﴿"*
> — QS. An-Nashr (110): 1-3

## Peristiwa: Gelombang Delegasi ke Madinah

Tahun 9 Hijriyah (631 M) dikenal sebagai *'Am al-Wufud* (Tahun Delegasi). Ibn Sa'd dalam *Tabaqat al-Kubra* mencatat bahwa jumlah delegasi yang datang ke Madinah sangat banyak, melibatkan puluhan suku dari seluruh penjuru Jazirah Arab.^3 Beberapa delegasi yang paling penting di antaranya:

### Delegasi Bani Tamim

Bani Tamim adalah salah satu suku terbesar di Jazirah Arab, bermukim di wilayah Najd. Mereka mengirimkan delegasi yang dipimpin oleh sejumlah tokoh terkemuka mereka. Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa delegasi ini datang dengan sikap yang awalnya sombong — mereka memanggil Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dari balik kamar-kamar beliau dengan suara keras.^1

Peristiwa ini diabadikan dalam al-Quran:

> إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُنَادُونَكَ مِن وَرَاءِ الْحُجُرَاتِ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ ﴿٤﴾
>
> *"Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang memanggilmu dari luar kamar-kamarmu, kebanyakan mereka tidak mengerti. ﴾4﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Hujurat (49): 4

Namun setelah dialog dengan Nabi ﷺ dan mendengar al-Quran, mereka menerima Islam dan kembali ke kaumnya sebagai Muslim.

### Delegasi Bani 'Amir bin Sha'sha'ah

Suku ini mengirimkan delegasinya untuk mempelajari Islam secara langsung. Di antara mereka terdapat **'Amir bin al-Thufail**, seorang pemimpin yang awalnya memiliki ambisi politik — ia ingin menjadi penguasa setelah Nabi ﷺ. Ketika Nabi menolak tawaran ini, 'Amir menolak masuk Islam. Namun sebagian besar anggota delegasi lainnya menerima Islam.^1 ^2

### Delegasi Thaqif dari Thaif

Delegasi Thaqif memiliki signifikansi emosional yang mendalam. Thaif adalah kota yang pernah mengusir dan melukai Nabi Muhammad ﷺ pada masa awal dakwah — penduduknya melempari beliau dengan batu hingga berdarah. Kini, setelah pengepungan Thaif pasca-Perang Hunain dan setelah melihat seluruh Jazirah Arab bergerak menuju Islam, suku Thaqif akhirnya mengirimkan delegasi ke Madinah.^1 ^3

Al-Mubarakfuri mencatat bahwa delegasi Thaqif awalnya mengajukan sejumlah syarat — termasuk meminta agar berhala mereka, Lata, tidak dihancurkan selama tiga tahun, dan meminta pengecualian dari kewajiban shalat. Nabi ﷺ menolak semua syarat yang bertentangan dengan prinsip tauhid, namun menerima bahwa mereka tidak perlu menghancurkan berhala dengan tangan mereka sendiri — beliau mengutus Abu Sufyan dan al-Mughirah bin Syu'bah untuk melakukannya.^2

### Delegasi Najran (Nasrani)

Salah satu delegasi paling unik adalah delegasi dari Najran, yang merupakan komunitas Nasrani (Kristen) Arab. Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa delegasi ini terdiri dari sekitar enam puluh orang, dipimpin oleh uskup mereka, **Abu Haritsah**.^1

Mereka berdebat dengan Nabi ﷺ mengenai hakikat Isa al-Masih. Ketika tidak tercapai kesepakatan teologis, turun ayat *mubahalah*:

> فَمَنْ حَاجَّكَ فِيهِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ فَقُلْ تَعَالَوْا نَدْعُ أَبْنَاءَنَا وَأَبْنَاءَكُمْ وَنِسَاءَنَا وَنِسَاءَكُمْ وَأَنفُسَنَا وَأَنفُسَكُمْ ثُمَّ نَبْتَهِلْ فَنَجْعَل لَّعْنَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْكَاذِبِينَ ﴿٦١﴾
>
> *"Siapa yang membantahmu tentang hal ini setelah datangnya ilmu kepadamu, katakanlah: 'Marilah kita memanggil anak-anak kami dan anak-anak kamu, istri-istri kami dan istri-istri kamu, diri kami dan diri kamu, kemudian marilah kita bermubahalah, dan kita minta supaya laknat Allah ditimpakan kepada orang-orang yang dusta.' ﴾61﴿"*
> — QS. Ali Imran (3): 61

Delegasi Najran akhirnya menolak *mubahalah* dan memilih membuat perjanjian damai dengan Nabi ﷺ — mereka tetap pada agama mereka namun membayar *jizyah* dan hidup di bawah perlindungan negara Islam.^1 Peristiwa ini menjadi preseden penting bagi hubungan antaragama dalam Islam.

### Delegasi-Delegasi Lainnya

Selain yang disebutkan di atas, Ibn Sa'd dalam *Tabaqat al-Kubra* mencatat puluhan delegasi lain, di antaranya:^3

- **Bani Hanifah** dari Yamamah, yang di antara anggotanya terdapat Musailamah yang kelak mengaku sebagai nabi palsu
- **Bani Asad** yang mengirimkan delegasi untuk menyatakan keislaman mereka
- **Suku-suku Yaman** — termasuk delegasi dari Himyar, Kindah, dan Hadhramaut
- **Delegasi dari Oman** — yang dipimpin oleh dua bersaudara, Jaifar dan 'Abbad, putra al-Julanda

Setiap delegasi disambut oleh Nabi ﷺ dengan penuh kehormatan. Beliau menyediakan tempat tinggal bagi mereka, mendengarkan persoalan mereka, menjawab pertanyaan mereka tentang Islam, dan mengirimkan guru-guru bersama mereka untuk mengajarkan al-Quran dan syariat Islam.

## Diplomasi Nabi: Surat-Surat kepada Para Penguasa

Gelombang delegasi ini tidak terjadi secara spontan semata. Sebelum *'Am al-Wufud*, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ telah mengirimkan surat-surat diplomatik kepada para penguasa dan pemimpin suku di seluruh Jazirah Arab dan sekitarnya. Ibn Sa'd mencatat bahwa beliau mengirim surat kepada raja Romawi (Heraklius), raja Persia (Kisra), penguasa Mesir (Muqauqis), raja Habasyah (Najasyi), serta para pemimpin suku Arab.^3

Surat-surat ini menunjukkan bahwa dakwah Islam bukan sekadar gerakan spiritual lokal, melainkan sebuah misi universal. Nabi ﷺ mengundang seluruh umat manusia kepada tauhid — tanpa memandang ras, suku, atau status sosial.

## Analisis: Mengapa Suku-Suku Masuk Islam?

Para sejarawan mengidentifikasi beberapa faktor yang mendorong gelombang masuk Islam secara massal pada tahun 9 Hijriyah:

**Pertama, faktor teologis.** Banyak orang Arab yang sebenarnya sudah meragukan penyembahan berhala sejak lama. Mereka disebut *hunafa'* — para pencari kebenaran yang menolak politeisme namun belum menemukan jalan yang jelas. Islam menawarkan tauhid yang jernih dan logis, yang selaras dengan fitrah manusia.

**Kedua, faktor politik-militer.** Setelah Fathu Makkah dan kemenangan di Hunain, tidak ada lagi kekuatan militer di Jazirah Arab yang mampu menentang Madinah. Masuk Islam berarti bergabung dengan kekuatan yang sedang naik, bukan tunduk kepada penakluk yang menindas — karena Islam menawarkan kesetaraan dan persaudaraan.

**Ketiga, faktor sosial-ekonomi.** Islam menghapuskan hierarki kesukuan yang kaku. Seorang budak yang beriman lebih mulia dari seorang bangsawan yang kafir. Pesan ini sangat revolusioner bagi masyarakat Arab yang selama berabad-abad terkungkung dalam sistem kesukuan yang rigid.

**Keempat, faktor moral.** Teladan pribadi Nabi Muhammad ﷺ — kejujurannya, keadilannya, kelembutannya, bahkan terhadap musuh — menjadi daya tarik yang sulit ditolak. Al-Mubarakfuri mencatat bahwa banyak delegasi yang masuk Islam bukan karena paksaan atau kepentingan politik, melainkan karena tersentuh oleh akhlak Nabi ﷺ.^2

## Hikmah: Pelajaran dari 'Am al-Wufud

### Diplomasi dan Dialog, Bukan Paksaan

*'Am al-Wufud* menunjukkan bahwa penyebaran Islam pada dasarnya berjalan melalui diplomasi, dialog, dan teladan — bukan melalui pedang. Mayoritas suku Arab masuk Islam secara sukarela setelah mengirimkan delegasi, berdialog dengan Nabi ﷺ, dan meyakini kebenaran risalahnya. Prinsip ini sejalan dengan firman Allah:

> لَا إِكْرَاهَ فِي الدِّينِ ۖ قَد تَّبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِّ ﴿٢٥٦﴾
>
> *"Tidak ada paksaan dalam beragama. Sungguh, telah jelas jalan yang benar dari jalan yang sesat. ﴾256﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Baqarah (2): 256

### Menghormati Keragaman

Perlakuan Nabi ﷺ terhadap delegasi Najran menunjukkan bahwa Islam mengakui realitas keragaman agama. Mereka yang tidak mau masuk Islam tidak dipaksa — mereka diberikan perlindungan dan hak-hak sipil di bawah perjanjian yang jelas. Ini adalah model koeksistensi yang sangat maju untuk zamannya.

### Kesatuan dalam Keberagaman Suku

Sebelum Islam, Jazirah Arab terpecah-belah dalam persaingan antarsuku yang tiada henti. *'Am al-Wufud* menyaksikan seluruh suku — dari Najd hingga Yaman, dari Oman hingga Bahrain — bersatu di bawah satu keyakinan. Persaudaraan iman menggantikan fanatisme kesukuan*'ashabiyyah*. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

> لَيْسَ مِنَّا مَنْ دَعَا إِلَى عَصَبِيَّةٍ
>
> *"Bukan dari golongan kami orang yang menyeru kepada fanatisme kesukuan."*
> — HR Abu Dawud, no. 5121

### Kepemimpinan yang Melayani

Dalam menyambut puluhan delegasi dari seluruh Jazirah Arab, Nabi ﷺ menunjukkan model kepemimpinan yang melayani — mendengarkan, memahami, menghormati, dan membimbing. Beliau tidak memaksa, tidak menghina, dan tidak merendahkan. Bahkan kepada delegasi yang datang dengan sikap sombong seperti Bani Tamim, beliau tetap bersabar dan menyambut dengan lapang dada.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Sirah Nabawiyah). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Jilid 1-2. Beirut: Dar Shadir.

# The Farewell Pilgrimage 🕋

---

## The Great Journey to Makkah

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ announced that he would go on Hajj — the pilgrimage to Makkah. This was his first and last Hajj — though nobody knew it at the time.

The news spread across all of Arabia. People came from everywhere — from the deserts, mountains, and coasts. **More than a hundred thousand people** joined the Prophet ﷺ! — a sea of people as far as the eye could see, all chanting: *"Labbaik Allahumma labbaik!"* — "Here I am at Your service, O Allah!"

They all walked together toward Makkah, the holy city where the Ka'bah stands. The journey was filled with joy and gratitude.

🎨 *Illustration: A sea of people dressed in white ihram garments walking toward Makkah in the distance. Desert mountains in the background. Dominant colors of white and golden brown. A grand and reverent atmosphere. Wide panoramic composition with a small Ka'bah on the horizon.*

---

## Standing on the Plain of Arafah

The most important part of Hajj is **standing at Arafah** — standing on the vast plain while praying to Allah. The Prophet ﷺ said: **"Hajj is Arafah."**

On this enormous plain, mounted on his camel named al-Qaswa', Prophet Muhammad ﷺ delivered his **final sermon** — a speech of great importance for all of humanity.

More than a hundred thousand people listened. They stood in silence, their hearts full of attention. What the Prophet ﷺ said that day is still remembered now — more than 1,400 years later!

On that same day, Allah revealed a very special verse: **"This day I have perfected for you your religion."** (QS Al-Ma'idah: 3)

🎨 *Illustration: The vast plain of Arafah filled with people in white clothing. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (depicted with light, no face shown) on a camel amid the crowd. A golden evening sky. Dominant colors of white, gold, and sky blue. A reverent and meaningful atmosphere. Bird's eye view composition showing the vast sea of people.*

---

## The Important Messages in the Sermon

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ delivered many important messages. Here are some of them:

🩸 **Don't hurt others** — "Your blood, your property, and your honor are sacred — it is forbidden to harm them!" This means: we must not hurt anyone, must not steal, and must not insult others.

👩 **Treat women well** — "Be good to women!" The Prophet ﷺ reminded everyone that women have the right to be treated with respect and kindness.

🌈 **All people are equal** — "There is no superiority of an Arab over a non-Arab, nor of a white-skinned person over a dark-skinned person — except through piety." This means: what makes a person noble is not their skin color or tribe, but the goodness of their heart!

📖 **Hold on to the Quran** — "I have left for you the Book of Allah. If you hold fast to it, you will never go astray."

🎨 *Illustration: An open parchment scroll with beautiful Arabic writing, surrounded by symbols of the sermon's messages — hands shaking (brotherhood), a balance scale (justice), a heart (compassion). Dominant colors of cream and gold. An atmosphere of wisdom. Child-friendly decorative composition.*

---

## Lessons from the Farewell Hajj

The final sermon of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ teaches us things that are very important for all of us:

⚖️ **Justice for everyone** — Every person has equal rights, no matter where they come from.

🤝 **Respect and care for others** — Never hurt anyone, whether with actions or words.

👧 **Honor women** — Islam teaches that women must be treated with kindness and respect.

🌍 **All humans are brothers and sisters** — Skin color, language, or tribe does not determine a person's worth. What matters is the goodness of their heart.

— these messages were delivered more than **1,400 years ago**, but they are still deeply needed in our world today. That is the greatness of Prophet Muhammad's teachings!

🎨 *Illustration: Children from various backgrounds (different skin colors, different clothing) standing together hand in hand in front of the Ka'bah. Smiles on their faces. Dominant colors of green, white, and gold. A warm atmosphere full of brotherhood. Symmetrical composition with the Ka'bah in the background.*
e30-haji-wada.children-id:
# Haji Perpisahan 🕋

---

## Perjalanan Besar ke Makkah

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengumumkan bahwa beliau akan pergi haji ke Makkah. Ini adalah haji pertama dan terakhir beliau — meskipun saat itu belum ada yang tahu.

Kabar ini menyebar ke seluruh Jazirah Arab. Orang-orang berdatangan dari mana-mana — dari gurun, pegunungan, dan pesisir. **Lebih dari seratus ribu orang** bergabung dengan Nabi ﷺ! — lautan manusia sejauh mata memandang, semuanya mengucapkan: *"Labbaik Allahumma labbaik!"* — "Aku penuhi panggilan-Mu, ya Allah!"

Mereka semua berjalan bersama menuju Makkah, kota suci tempat Ka'bah berada. Perjalanan ini penuh kegembiraan dan rasa syukur.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Lautan manusia berpakaian ihram putih berjalan menuju Makkah dari kejauhan. Gunung-gunung padang pasir di latar belakang. Warna dominan putih dan emas kecokelatan. Suasana agung dan khusyuk. Komposisi panorama lebar dengan Ka'bah kecil di cakrawala.*

---

## Berdiri di Padang Arafah

Puncak haji adalah **wukuf di Arafah** — berdiri di padang yang luas sambil berdoa kepada Allah. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: **"Haji adalah Arafah."**

Di padang Arafah yang sangat luas ini, di atas unta beliau yang bernama al-Qashwa', Nabi Muhammad ﷺ menyampaikan **khutbah terakhir** — pidato yang sangat penting untuk seluruh umat manusia.

Lebih dari seratus ribu orang mendengarkan. Mereka diam, hati mereka penuh perhatian. Apa yang Nabi ﷺ sampaikan hari itu masih diingat hingga sekarang — lebih dari 1.400 tahun kemudian!

Di hari itu juga, Allah menurunkan ayat yang sangat istimewa: **"Pada hari ini telah Aku sempurnakan agamamu untukmu."** (QS Al-Ma'idah: 3)

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Padang Arafah yang luas dipenuhi manusia berpakaian putih. Nabi Muhammad ﷺ (digambarkan dengan cahaya, tanpa wajah) di atas unta di tengah kerumunan. Langit sore keemasan. Warna dominan putih, emas, dan biru langit. Suasana khusyuk dan penuh makna. Komposisi bird's eye view menunjukkan luasnya lautan manusia.*

---

## Pesan-Pesan Penting dalam Khutbah

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ menyampaikan banyak pesan penting. Inilah beberapa di antaranya:

🩸 **Jangan sakiti orang lain** — "Darah kalian, harta kalian, dan kehormatan kalian suci — haram untuk diganggu!" Artinya: kita tidak boleh menyakiti siapa pun, tidak boleh mencuri, dan tidak boleh menghina orang lain.

👩 **Perlakukan perempuan dengan baik** — "Berbuat baiklah terhadap perempuan!" Nabi ﷺ mengingatkan bahwa perempuan punya hak untuk diperlakukan dengan hormat dan kasih sayang.

🌈 **Semua manusia sama** — "Tidak ada kelebihan orang Arab atas non-Arab, tidak ada kelebihan orang berkulit putih atas orang berkulit hitam — kecuali dengan takwa." Artinya: yang membuat seseorang mulia bukan warna kulitnya atau sukunya, tapi kebaikan hatinya!

📖 **Pegang teguh Al-Quran** — "Aku tinggalkan untuk kalian Kitab Allah. Jika kalian berpegang padanya, kalian tidak akan tersesat."

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Sebuah gulungan perkamen terbuka dengan tulisan Arab yang indah, dikelilingi simbol-simbol pesan khutbah — tangan berjabat (persaudaraan), timbangan (keadilan), hati (kasih sayang). Warna dominan krem dan emas. Suasana penuh hikmah. Komposisi dekoratif bergaya anak-anak.*

---

## Pelajaran dari Haji Wada'

Khutbah terakhir Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengajarkan hal-hal yang sangat penting bagi kita semua:

⚖️ **Keadilan untuk semua** — Setiap orang punya hak yang sama, tidak peduli dari mana asalnya.

🤝 **Hormat dan sayangi sesama** — Jangan pernah menyakiti orang lain, baik dengan perbuatan maupun perkataan.

👧 **Muliakan perempuan** — Islam mengajarkan bahwa perempuan harus diperlakukan dengan baik dan hormat.

🌍 **Semua manusia bersaudara** — Warna kulit, bahasa, atau suku tidak menentukan nilai seseorang. Yang penting adalah kebaikan hatinya.

— pesan-pesan ini disampaikan lebih dari **1.400 tahun yang lalu**, tapi masih sangat penting dan dibutuhkan dunia hari ini. Itulah kehebatan ajaran Nabi Muhammad ﷺ!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Anak-anak dari berbagai latar belakang (berkulit berbeda-beda, berpakaian berbeda-beda) berdiri bersama sambil bergandengan tangan di depan Ka'bah. Senyum di wajah mereka. Warna dominan hijau, putih, dan emas. Suasana hangat dan penuh persaudaraan. Komposisi simetris dengan Ka'bah di latar belakang.*

# The Farewell Hajj and Final Sermon: Human Rights in Islam

## Historical Context: The First and Last Hajj of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

In the month of Dhul-Qa'dah in the year 10 AH (632 CE), Prophet Muhammad ﷺ announced his intention to perform the Hajj pilgrimage. This would be the first and only Hajj he performed after the migration to Madinah — and, unbeknown to the companions, it would also be his last. Hence, this pilgrimage became known as *Hajjatul Wada'* (the Farewell Pilgrimage).^1

Imam Muslim in his *Sahih* narrates from Ja'far ibn Muhammad, from his father, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, a lengthy and detailed hadith covering the entire sequence of the Prophet's Hajj — from departure from Madinah to the completion of all rites.^1 This hadith of Jabir remains the primary reference for scholars of Islamic jurisprudence in determining the procedures of Hajj.

News of the Prophet's departure spread across the entire Arabian Peninsula. Al-Mubarakfuri in *al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* records that the number of pilgrims accompanying him was enormous — historians estimate between one hundred thousand and one hundred forty thousand people.^2 ^4 This was the largest gathering of people the Arabian Peninsula had ever witnessed.

## The Events: Journey to Makkah

### Departure from Madinah

Imam Muslim narrates that the Prophet ﷺ departed from Madinah on the 25th of Dhul-Qa'dah. He prayed Zuhr at Dhul-Hulayfah (the *miqat* for the people of Madinah), then entered the state of *ihram* after the prayer.^1 Jabir describes that he could see an ocean of people in every direction — in front, behind, to the right, and to the left of the Prophet ﷺ — as far as the eye could see.

Ahmad ibn Hanbal in his *Musnad* narrates that the Prophet ﷺ recited the *talbiyah*:^3

> لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
>
> *"Here I am at Your service, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Indeed, all praise, grace, and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner. ﴾125﴿"*
> — Narrated by Ahmad, Musnad, vol. 3

### Arrival in Makkah and Tawaf

Upon arriving in Makkah on the 4th of Dhul-Hijjah, the Prophet ﷺ proceeded directly to the Sacred Mosque. Imam Muslim narrates that he kissed the Black Stone, then performed seven circuits of *tawaf* — the first three at a brisk pace*raml* and the remaining four at a normal stride.^1 After the *tawaf*, he prayed two units behind the Station of Ibrahim and recited:

> وَاتَّخِذُوا مِن مَّقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى ﴿١٢٥﴾
>
> *"And take the station of Ibrahim as a place of prayer. ﴾125﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Baqarah (2): 125

### Standing at Arafah — The Pinnacle of Hajj

The ninth day of Dhul-Hijjah is the Day of Arafah — the very core of the Hajj pilgrimage. The Prophet ﷺ said:

> الْحَجُّ عَرَفَةُ
>
> *"Hajj is Arafah."*
> — Narrated by Ahmad, no. 18774

On the vast plain of Arafah, mounted upon his camel al-Qaswa', with more than a hundred thousand pairs of eyes fixed upon him, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ delivered a sermon that would alter the course of human civilization.

## The Sermon at Arafah: A Declaration of Human Rights

The Prophet's sermon at Arafah stands as one of the most influential speeches in human history. Delivered more than a thousand years before the United Nations Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948), its principles transcended its era. Imam Muslim and Imam Ahmad narrate its contents through multiple chains of transmission:^1 ^3

### The Sanctity of Life, Property, and Honor

The Prophet ﷺ opened his sermon with a definitive declaration:

> إِنَّ دِمَاءَكُمْ وَأَمْوَالَكُمْ وَأَعْرَاضَكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ حَرَامٌ كَحُرْمَةِ يَوْمِكُمْ هَذَا فِي شَهْرِكُمْ هَذَا فِي بَلَدِكُمْ هَذَا
>
> *"Indeed, your blood, your property, and your honor are sacred to you, as sacred as this day of yours, in this month of yours, in this land of yours."*
> — Narrated by Muslim, no. 1218

This statement established three inviolable rights: the right to life, the right to property, and the right to honor. In a society accustomed to inter-tribal warfare, blood vengeance, and plunder, this declaration was a fundamental moral revolution.

### Abolition of Usury and Pre-Islamic Practices

The Prophet ﷺ then announced the abolition of two pillars of pre-Islamic economic and social injustice:

> أَلَا وَإِنَّ كُلَّ رِبًا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ مَوْضُوعٌ ... وَإِنَّ كُلَّ دَمٍ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ مَوْضُوعٌ
>
> *"Behold, every usury from the Age of Ignorance is abolished ... And every blood-claim from the Age of Ignorance is abolished."*
> — Narrated by Muslim, no. 1218

He began with his own family — abolishing the usury of his uncle Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib and the blood-claims for the death of his kinsman. This demonstrated that a leader must be the first to exemplify justice.

### The Rights of Women

In a society that treated women as property, the Prophet ﷺ declared:

> اسْتَوْصُوا بِالنِّسَاءِ خَيْرًا فَإِنَّهُنَّ عِنْدَكُمْ عَوَانٍ ... وَلَهُنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ رِزْقُهُنَّ وَكِسْوَتُهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ
>
> *"Treat women well, for they are your partners ... And they have the right to provision and clothing from you in a reasonable manner."*
> — Narrated by Muslim, no. 1218

This established women's rights to financial support, good treatment, and protection from spousal abuse — rights that would not be formally recognized in the West until centuries later.

### The Equality of All Humanity

Perhaps the most revolutionary passage of the sermon was the declaration of universal human equality:

> يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ وَاحِدٌ وَإِنَّ أَبَاكُمْ وَاحِدٌ أَلَا لَا فَضْلَ لِعَرَبِيٍّ عَلَى أَعْجَمِيٍّ وَلَا لِعَجَمِيٍّ عَلَى عَرَبِيٍّ وَلَا لِأَحْمَرَ عَلَى أَسْوَدَ وَلَا أَسْوَدَ عَلَى أَحْمَرَ إِلَّا بِالتَّقْوَى
>
> *"O people, your Lord is one and your father is one. There is no superiority of an Arab over a non-Arab, nor of a non-Arab over an Arab, nor of a red-skinned person over a dark-skinned person, nor of a dark-skinned person over a red-skinned person — except through piety."*
> — Narrated by Ahmad, no. 23489

In a single declaration, the Prophet ﷺ demolished the foundations of racism, tribalism, and ethnic supremacy that had been entrenched for centuries. This was proclaimed before more than a hundred thousand witnesses — fourteen centuries before the modern civil rights movement.

### Holding Fast to the Quran and Sunnah

The Prophet ﷺ closed his sermon with an enduring counsel:

> وَقَدْ تَرَكْتُ فِيكُمْ مَا لَنْ تَضِلُّوا بَعْدَهُ إِنِ اعْتَصَمْتُمْ بِهِ كِتَابَ اللَّهِ
>
> *"And I have left among you that which, if you hold fast to it, you will never go astray — the Book of Allah. ﴾3﴿"*
> — Narrated by Muslim, no. 1218

## The Revelation of the Verse of Completion

On that very Day of Arafah, one of the most momentous verses of the Quran was revealed:

> الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلَامَ دِينًا ﴿٣﴾
>
> *"This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion. ﴾3﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Ma'idah (5): 3

Imam al-Bukhari narrates that when this verse was revealed, Umar ibn al-Khattab wept. When asked why, he replied: *"After perfection, there is nothing but decline."* Umar understood that this verse signaled the nearing end of the prophetic mission — and the approaching death of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

## Analysis: A Sermon That Transcended Its Era

### Human Rights Before the Modern Declarations

The principles declared in the Arafah Sermon have direct parallels with various modern human rights documents:

- **The right to life** (Article 3, UDHR) — declared at Arafah 1,316 years earlier
- **Equality without racial discrimination** (Article 2, UDHR) — proclaimed on the plain of Arafah when the world was still fully in the grip of tribalism and racism
- **The right to property** (Article 17, UDHR) — affirmed through the prohibition of seizing others' wealth
- **Women's rights** — declared amid a society that buried infant girls alive

The fundamental difference: the Arafah Sermon was grounded not in political consensus that can shift, but in divine authority that is absolute and universal.

### Justice Begins with Oneself

One of the most striking aspects of this sermon is how the Prophet ﷺ began reform with his own family. He abolished his uncle's usury and his kinsman's blood-claims — not those of strangers or rival tribes. This established the principle that justice must begin with oneself and one's closest kin.

### Trust and Accountability

At the conclusion of his sermon, the Prophet ﷺ asked the entire congregation: *"Have I conveyed the message?"* They answered: *"Yes, you have conveyed it."* He then raised his index finger toward the sky and said: *"O Allah, bear witness."*^1

This was an extraordinary moment — a leader requesting confirmation from his people that he had fulfilled his trust, then calling upon Allah as witness. There is no higher model of leadership accountability.

## Lessons: The Eternal Message from the Plain of Arafah

The Arafah Sermon is not merely a historical speech — it is the charter of Islamic civilization that remains relevant to this day. Every principle the Prophet ﷺ articulated on the plain of Arafah provides an answer to the challenges humanity still faces in the twenty-first century: racism, economic inequality, the oppression of women, and violations of human rights.

The core message is unmistakable: human dignity is determined not by skin color, tribal affiliation, or wealth — but by piety and goodness. And the fundamental rights of every human being — life, property, and honor — are sacred and inviolable.

---

## References

1. **Muslim, Abu al-Husayn Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih Muslim). Hadith no. 1218, Kitab al-Hajj (Hadith of Jabir on the Prophet's Hajj).

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safiur-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Ahmad ibn Hanbal.** *Al-Musnad*. Hadith no. 23489 (sermon on human equality), no. 18774 (Hajj is Arafah), and narrations of the *talbiyah*.

4. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

# Haji Wada' dan Khutbah Terakhir: Hak Asasi Manusia dalam Islam

## Konteks Historis: Haji Pertama dan Terakhir Nabi Muhammad ﷺ

Pada bulan Dzulqa'dah tahun 10 Hijriyah (632 M), Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengumumkan niatnya untuk menunaikan ibadah haji. Ini adalah haji pertama dan satu-satunya yang beliau laksanakan setelah hijrah ke Madinah — dan tanpa disadari oleh para sahabat, ini pula yang akan menjadi haji terakhir beliau. Oleh karena itu, ibadah haji ini dikenal sebagai *Hajjatul Wada'* (Haji Perpisahan).^1

Imam Muslim dalam *Shahih*-nya meriwayatkan dari Ja'far bin Muhammad, dari ayahnya, dari Jabir bin Abdullah, sebuah hadits panjang yang mendetail tentang seluruh rangkaian haji Nabi ﷺ — mulai dari keberangkatan dari Madinah hingga selesainya seluruh manasik.^1 Hadits Jabir ini menjadi rujukan utama ulama fikih dalam menentukan tata cara haji.

Kabar keberangkatan Nabi ﷺ menyebar ke seluruh Jazirah Arab. Al-Mubarakfuri dalam *al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* mencatat bahwa jumlah jamaah yang menyertai beliau sangat besar — para sejarawan memperkirakan antara seratus ribu hingga seratus empat puluh ribu orang.^2 ^4 Ini adalah pertemuan manusia terbesar yang pernah disaksikan oleh Jazirah Arab hingga saat itu.

## Peristiwa: Perjalanan Menuju Makkah

### Keberangkatan dari Madinah

Imam Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berangkat dari Madinah pada tanggal 25 Dzulqa'dah. Beliau shalat Zhuhur di Dzul Hulaifah (miqat penduduk Madinah), lalu memasuki ihram setelah shalat.^1 Jabir menggambarkan bahwa beliau bisa melihat lautan manusia ke segala arah — di depan, di belakang, di kanan, dan di kiri Nabi ﷺ — sejauh mata memandang.

Ahmad bin Hanbal dalam *Musnad*-nya meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bertalbiyah dengan kalimat:^3

> لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
>
> *"Aku penuhi panggilan-Mu, ya Allah, aku penuhi panggilan-Mu. Aku penuhi panggilan-Mu, tiada sekutu bagi-Mu, aku penuhi panggilan-Mu. Sesungguhnya segala puji, nikmat, dan kerajaan hanya milik-Mu. Tiada sekutu bagi-Mu. ﴾125﴿"*
> — HR Ahmad, Musnad, jilid 3

### Tiba di Makkah dan Tawaf

Setibanya di Makkah pada tanggal 4 Dzulhijjah, Nabi ﷺ langsung menuju Masjidil Haram. Imam Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa beliau mencium Hajar Aswad, lalu melakukan tawaf tujuh kali putaran — tiga putaran pertama dengan langkah cepat*raml* dan empat putaran berikutnya dengan langkah biasa.^1 Setelah tawaf, beliau shalat dua rakaat di belakang Maqam Ibrahim dan membaca:

> وَاتَّخِذُوا مِن مَّقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى ﴿١٢٥﴾
>
> *"Dan jadikanlah maqam Ibrahim sebagai tempat shalat. ﴾125﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Baqarah (2): 125

### Wukuf di Arafah — Puncak Haji

Hari kesembilan Dzulhijjah adalah hari Arafah — inti dari seluruh ibadah haji. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

> الْحَجُّ عَرَفَةُ
>
> *"Haji adalah Arafah."*
> — HR Ahmad, no. 18774

Di padang Arafah yang luas inilah, di atas untanya yang bernama al-Qashwa', dengan lebih dari seratus ribu pasang mata tertuju kepadanya, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ menyampaikan khutbah yang akan mengubah sejarah peradaban manusia.

## Khutbah Arafah: Deklarasi Hak Asasi Manusia

Khutbah Nabi ﷺ di Arafah adalah salah satu pidato paling berpengaruh dalam sejarah umat manusia. Ia disampaikan lebih dari seribu tahun sebelum Deklarasi Hak Asasi Manusia PBB (1948), namun prinsip-prinsip yang dikandungnya melampaui zamannya. Imam Muslim dan Imam Ahmad meriwayatkan isi khutbah ini dari berbagai jalur periwayatan:^1 ^3

### Kesucian Jiwa, Harta, dan Kehormatan

Nabi ﷺ memulai khutbahnya dengan deklarasi yang tegas:

> إِنَّ دِمَاءَكُمْ وَأَمْوَالَكُمْ وَأَعْرَاضَكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ حَرَامٌ كَحُرْمَةِ يَوْمِكُمْ هَذَا فِي شَهْرِكُمْ هَذَا فِي بَلَدِكُمْ هَذَا
>
> *"Sesungguhnya darah kalian, harta kalian, dan kehormatan kalian haram atas kalian, sebagaimana haramnya hari ini, di bulan ini, di negeri ini."*
> — HR Muslim, no. 1218

Pernyataan ini menetapkan tiga hak asasi yang tidak boleh dilanggar oleh siapa pun: hak atas kehidupan, hak atas harta benda, dan hak atas kehormatan. Dalam konteks masyarakat Arab yang terbiasa dengan peperangan antarsuku, pembunuhan balas dendam, dan perampasan harta, deklarasi ini adalah revolusi moral yang fundamental.

### Penghapusan Riba dan Kebiasaan Jahiliyah

Nabi ﷺ kemudian mengumumkan penghapusan dua pilar ketidakadilan ekonomi dan sosial jahiliyah:

> أَلَا وَإِنَّ كُلَّ رِبًا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ مَوْضُوعٌ ... وَإِنَّ كُلَّ دَمٍ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ مَوْضُوعٌ
>
> *"Ketahuilah, sesungguhnya setiap riba dari masa Jahiliyah dihapuskan ... Dan setiap tuntutan darah dari masa Jahiliyah dihapuskan."*
> — HR Muslim, no. 1218

Beliau memulai dari keluarganya sendiri — menghapuskan riba pamannya Abbas bin Abdul Muthalib dan menghapuskan tuntutan darah atas kematian kerabatnya. Ini menunjukkan bahwa pemimpin harus menjadi teladan pertama dalam menerapkan keadilan.

### Hak-Hak Perempuan

Dalam sebuah masyarakat yang memperlakukan perempuan sebagai properti, Nabi ﷺ mendeklarasikan:

> اسْتَوْصُوا بِالنِّسَاءِ خَيْرًا فَإِنَّهُنَّ عِنْدَكُمْ عَوَانٍ ... وَلَهُنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ رِزْقُهُنَّ وَكِسْوَتُهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ
>
> *"Berbuat baiklah terhadap perempuan. Sesungguhnya mereka di sisi kalian adalah pasangan ... Dan mereka berhak mendapatkan nafkah dan pakaian dari kalian dengan cara yang baik."*
> — HR Muslim, no. 1218

Pernyataan ini menetapkan hak perempuan atas nafkah, perlakuan yang baik, dan perlindungan dari kezaliman suami — hak-hak yang baru diakui secara formal di Barat berabad-abad kemudian.

### Persamaan Manusia

Salah satu bagian paling revolusioner dari khutbah ini adalah deklarasi persamaan seluruh manusia:

> يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ وَاحِدٌ وَإِنَّ أَبَاكُمْ وَاحِدٌ أَلَا لَا فَضْلَ لِعَرَبِيٍّ عَلَى أَعْجَمِيٍّ وَلَا لِعَجَمِيٍّ عَلَى عَرَبِيٍّ وَلَا لِأَحْمَرَ عَلَى أَسْوَدَ وَلَا أَسْوَدَ عَلَى أَحْمَرَ إِلَّا بِالتَّقْوَى
>
> *"Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya Tuhan kalian satu dan ayah kalian satu. Ketahuilah, tidak ada kelebihan orang Arab atas non-Arab, tidak ada kelebihan non-Arab atas orang Arab, tidak ada kelebihan orang berkulit merah atas orang berkulit hitam, dan tidak ada kelebihan orang berkulit hitam atas orang berkulit merah — kecuali dengan takwa."*
> — HR Ahmad, no. 23489

Dalam satu kalimat, Nabi ﷺ menghancurkan fondasi rasisme, tribalisme, dan supremasi etnis yang telah mengakar selama berabad-abad. Deklarasi ini disampaikan di hadapan lebih dari seratus ribu saksi — empat belas abad sebelum gerakan hak-hak sipil modern.

### Berpegang pada Al-Quran dan Sunnah

Nabi ﷺ menutup khutbahnya dengan wasiat yang abadi:

> وَقَدْ تَرَكْتُ فِيكُمْ مَا لَنْ تَضِلُّوا بَعْدَهُ إِنِ اعْتَصَمْتُمْ بِهِ كِتَابَ اللَّهِ
>
> *"Dan sungguh telah aku tinggalkan kepada kalian sesuatu yang jika kalian berpegang teguh padanya, kalian tidak akan pernah tersesat setelahnya — Kitab Allah. ﴾3﴿"*
> — HR Muslim, no. 1218

## Turunnya Ayat Kesempurnaan Agama

Di hari Arafah itu pula, turunlah salah satu ayat al-Quran yang paling monumental:

> الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلَامَ دِينًا ﴿٣﴾
>
> *"Pada hari ini telah Aku sempurnakan agamamu untukmu, dan telah Aku cukupkan nikmat-Ku bagimu, dan telah Aku ridhai Islam sebagai agamamu. ﴾3﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Ma'idah (5): 3

Imam al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa ketika ayat ini turun, Umar bin Khattab menangis. Ketika ditanya mengapa, ia menjawab: *"Setelah kesempurnaan, tidak ada lagi kecuali kekurangan."* Umar memahami bahwa ayat ini mengisyaratkan dekatnya akhir misi kenabian — dan dekatnya wafat Rasulullah ﷺ.

## Analisis: Khutbah yang Melampaui Zaman

### Hak Asasi Manusia Sebelum Deklarasi Modern

Prinsip-prinsip yang dideklarasikan dalam Khutbah Arafah memiliki paralel langsung dengan berbagai dokumen hak asasi manusia modern:

- **Hak atas kehidupan** (Pasal 3 DUHAM) — dideklarasikan di Arafah 1.316 tahun lebih awal
- **Persamaan tanpa diskriminasi ras** (Pasal 2 DUHAM) — disampaikan di padang Arafah ketika dunia masih sepenuhnya berada dalam cengkeraman tribalisme dan rasisme
- **Hak atas properti** (Pasal 17 DUHAM) — ditegaskan melalui pengharaman merampas harta orang lain
- **Hak-hak perempuan** — dideklarasikan di tengah masyarakat yang menguburkan bayi perempuan hidup-hidup

Perbedaan mendasarnya: Khutbah Arafah tidak didasarkan pada konsensus politik yang bisa berubah, melainkan pada otoritas ilahi yang absolut dan universal.

### Keadilan Dimulai dari Diri Sendiri

Salah satu aspek paling menonjol dari khutbah ini adalah bagaimana Nabi ﷺ memulai reformasi dari keluarganya sendiri. Beliau menghapuskan riba pamannya dan tuntutan darah kerabatnya — bukan riba orang lain dan tuntutan darah suku lain. Ini menetapkan prinsip bahwa keadilan dimulai dari diri sendiri dan keluarga terdekat.

### Amanah dan Tanggung Jawab

Di akhir khutbahnya, Nabi ﷺ bertanya kepada seluruh jamaah: *"Apakah aku sudah menyampaikan?"* Mereka menjawab: *"Ya, engkau sudah menyampaikan."* Beliau kemudian mengangkat jari telunjuknya ke langit dan berkata: *"Ya Allah, saksikanlah."*^1

Ini adalah momen yang luar biasa — seorang pemimpin yang meminta konfirmasi dari rakyatnya bahwa ia telah menunaikan amanahnya, lalu meminta Allah sebagai saksi. Tidak ada model akuntabilitas kepemimpinan yang lebih tinggi dari ini.

## Hikmah: Pesan Abadi dari Padang Arafah

Khutbah Arafah bukan sekadar pidato bersejarah — ia adalah piagam peradaban Islam yang terus relevan hingga hari ini. Setiap prinsip yang disampaikan Nabi ﷺ di padang Arafah merupakan jawaban atas persoalan-persoalan yang masih dihadapi umat manusia di abad ke-21: rasisme, ketimpangan ekonomi, penindasan terhadap perempuan, dan pelanggaran hak asasi manusia.

Pesan utamanya jelas: kemuliaan manusia bukan ditentukan oleh warna kulit, suku bangsa, atau kekayaan — melainkan oleh ketakwaan dan kebaikan. Dan hak-hak dasar setiap manusia — hidup, harta, dan kehormatan — adalah suci dan tidak boleh dilanggar oleh siapa pun.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Muslim, Abu al-Husain Muslim bin al-Hajjaj.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Muslim). Hadits no. 1218, Kitab al-Hajj (Hadits Jabir tentang Haji Nabi).

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Sirah Nabawiyah). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Ahmad bin Hanbal.** *Al-Musnad*. Hadits no. 23489 (khutbah persamaan manusia), no. 18774 (haji adalah Arafah), serta riwayat talbiyah.

4. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

e31-wafat-rasulullah.children-en:
# The Death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ 🌙

---

## The Prophet Falls Ill

After completing the Farewell Hajj, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ began to feel unwell. He suffered from a **very high fever** — even worse than what ordinary people experience.

As the illness grew worse, the Prophet ﷺ wanted to stay in the room of his wife **Aisha**. There, Aisha took care of him with great love and tenderness. She would recite protective prayers while gently passing the Prophet's own hand over his body.

Even though he was very ill, the Prophet ﷺ still climbed up to the mosque pulpit to speak to his companions. He told them about "a servant whom Allah gave a choice between this world and meeting Allah — and he chose to meet Allah." **Abu Bakr** immediately began to cry because he understood that the Prophet ﷺ was talking about himself.

🎨 *Illustration: A simple room with dark curtains. Soft light enters through a small window. A modest bed with a blanket. A calm yet melancholic atmosphere. Dominant colors of dark blue, cream, and soft golden light. An intimate and emotional interior composition.*

---

## The Last Dawn

On **Monday morning**, Abu Bakr was leading the Fajr (dawn) prayer in the mosque. Suddenly, the Prophet ﷺ drew aside the curtain of Aisha's room that faced the mosque.

The companions saw his face — **bright and radiant** like a page of the Quran. They were overjoyed! They thought the Prophet ﷺ had recovered.

The Prophet ﷺ smiled at the sight of the neat rows of worshippers, then gestured for them to continue their prayer. He lowered the curtain again.

That was the **very last time** the companions saw the face of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

🎨 *Illustration: The interior of Masjid Nabawi. Rows of worshippers praying Fajr in the bluish dawn light. A curtain slightly open with a golden glow behind it (representing the Prophet ﷺ without showing his face). Dominant colors of dawn blue and gold. An atmosphere of tenderness and beauty. Composition pointing toward the glowing curtain.*

---

## The Final Moments

The Prophet ﷺ was resting against Aisha's chest. Aisha's brother entered carrying a **siwak** (a wooden toothbrush). The Prophet ﷺ looked at it, and Aisha knew he wanted it. She softened the siwak for him, then gave it to him. The Prophet ﷺ cleaned his teeth very thoroughly.

Then, the Prophet ﷺ raised his hand upward and said:

**"O Allah, forgive me, have mercy on me, and join me with al-Rafiq al-A'la (the Highest Companion)."**

He repeated those words three times. Then his hand fell, and he **returned to Allah**. Aisha said: "He passed away between my chin and my chest."

It was Monday, **12 Rabi al-Awwal, 11 AH** — or June 8, 632 CE. The Prophet ﷺ was 63 years old.

🎨 *Illustration: A hand raised upward toward bright golden light streaming from the sky. Below, the silhouette of the peaceful city of Madinah. Dominant colors of gold, white, and sky blue. An atmosphere of peace and majesty. Vertical composition from bottom to top toward the light.*

---

## Abu Bakr and the Words That Changed Everything

When the news of the Prophet's death spread, the companions were devastated. **Umar ibn al-Khattab** — a man known for his great strength — could not accept reality. He stood up and declared that the Prophet ﷺ could not possibly have died and would surely return.

Then **Abu Bakr** arrived. He entered Aisha's room, kissed the Prophet's forehead, and said: *"You are good in life and good in death."*

Abu Bakr then came out to the mosque and spoke calmly but firmly:

**"Whoever among you worshipped Muhammad — then Muhammad has died. And whoever worshipped Allah — then Allah is Ever-Living and shall never die."**

Then he recited a verse from the Quran: **"Muhammad is not but a messenger. Other messengers have passed before him."** (QS Ali Imran: 144)

Hearing this, everyone wept. Umar collapsed to the ground — his legs could no longer hold him. He said: "It was as though I heard this verse for the first time."

Abu Bakr's words taught everyone that **Islam is not about one person** — no matter how great — but about Allah and His eternal teachings.

🎨 *Illustration: Abu Bakr (an elderly man with a white beard) standing firm in the mosque, one hand raised. Before him, weeping congregants. Light streams from the direction of the mihrab. Dominant colors of dark green and gold. An emotional atmosphere yet calm radiating from Abu Bakr. Composition with Abu Bakr at center, congregants around him.*

---

## Lessons from the Saddest Day

The death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ teaches us many important things:

💫 **All creatures return to Allah** — Even the Prophet ﷺ, the most beloved of Allah, also died. Nothing in this world is eternal except Allah.

📖 **The Quran and Sunnah are the most beautiful legacy** — The Prophet ﷺ left behind two things that will guide the community forever: the Quran and the example of his life.

💪 **Be strong even when sad** — Abu Bakr was deeply grieved, but he was still able to lead the community. He wept, then rose and took action.

❤️ **Love the Prophet ﷺ by following his teachings** — The best way to love the Prophet ﷺ is not just to mourn his departure, but to practice what he taught.

🎨 *Illustration: Masjid Nabawi in Madinah with its iconic green dome. A sunset sky of orange and purple. Birds flying overhead. Dominant colors of green, gold, and purple dusk. A peaceful atmosphere of longing. Wide landscape composition with the green dome at center.*
e31-wafat-rasulullah.children-id:
# Wafat Rasulullah ﷺ 🌙

---

## Nabi Muhammad ﷺ Jatuh Sakit

Setelah menunaikan Haji Wada', Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mulai sering merasa tidak enak badan. Beliau menderita **demam yang sangat tinggi** — bahkan lebih berat dari demam orang biasa.

Saat sakit semakin berat, Nabi ﷺ ingin selalu berada di kamar istrinya **Aisyah**. Di sanalah Aisyah merawat beliau dengan penuh kasih sayang. Aisyah membacakan doa-doa pelindung sambil mengusapkan tangan Nabi ﷺ ke tubuh beliau.

Meskipun sakit berat, Nabi ﷺ masih naik ke mimbar masjid untuk berbicara kepada para sahabat. Beliau bercerita tentang "seorang hamba yang diberi pilihan oleh Allah antara dunia dan bertemu Allah — dan ia memilih bertemu Allah." **Abu Bakr** langsung menangis karena ia memahami bahwa Nabi ﷺ sedang berbicara tentang diri beliau sendiri.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Kamar sederhana dengan tirai berwarna gelap. Cahaya lembut masuk dari jendela kecil. Sebuah tempat tidur sederhana dengan selimut. Suasana tenang namun melankolis. Warna dominan biru tua, krem, dan cahaya emas lembut. Komposisi interior yang intim dan penuh perasaan.*

---

## Subuh Terakhir

Pada hari **Senin pagi**, Abu Bakr sedang mengimami shalat Subuh di masjid. Tiba-tiba, Nabi ﷺ membuka tabir kamar Aisyah yang menghadap ke masjid.

Para sahabat melihat wajah beliau — **cerah bersinar** seperti halaman mushaf. Mereka sangat gembira! Mereka mengira Nabi ﷺ sudah sembuh.

Nabi ﷺ tersenyum melihat barisan shalat yang rapi, lalu memberi isyarat agar mereka melanjutkan shalat. Beliau menutup kembali tabir kamarnya.

Itu adalah **saat terakhir** para sahabat melihat wajah Nabi Muhammad ﷺ.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Masjid Nabawi tampak dari dalam. Barisan jamaah shalat Subuh dalam cahaya fajar kebiruan. Sebuah tabir terbuka sedikit dengan pancaran cahaya keemasan di belakangnya (melambangkan Nabi ﷺ tanpa menampilkan wajah). Warna dominan biru fajar dan emas. Suasana penuh haru dan keindahan. Komposisi mengarah ke tabir cahaya.*

---

## Detik-Detik Terakhir

Nabi ﷺ bersandar di dada Aisyah. Saudara Aisyah masuk membawa **siwak** (sikat gigi dari kayu). Nabi ﷺ memandangnya, dan Aisyah tahu beliau menginginkannya. Aisyah melembutkan siwak itu, lalu memberikannya. Nabi ﷺ membersihkan giginya dengan sangat bersih.

Kemudian, Nabi ﷺ mengangkat tangannya ke atas dan berkata:

**"Ya Allah, ampunilah aku, rahmatilah aku, dan pertemukanlah aku dengan al-Rafiq al-A'la (Kawan Yang Mahatinggi)."**

Beliau mengulangi kalimat itu tiga kali. Lalu tangan beliau terkulai, dan beliau **kembali kepada Allah**. Aisyah berkata: "Beliau wafat antara dagu dan dadaku."

Saat itu hari Senin, **12 Rabiul Awal tahun 11 Hijriyah** — atau 8 Juni 632 M. Nabi ﷺ berusia 63 tahun.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Tangan yang terangkat ke atas menuju cahaya keemasan yang terang dari langit. Di bawahnya, bayangan kota Madinah yang tenang. Warna dominan emas, putih, dan biru langit. Suasana penuh kedamaian dan keagungan. Komposisi vertikal dari bawah ke atas menuju cahaya.*

---

## Abu Bakr dan Kata-Kata yang Mengubah Segalanya

Ketika kabar wafatnya Nabi ﷺ tersebar, para sahabat sangat terpukul. **Umar bin Khattab** — seorang yang sangat kuat — tidak bisa menerima kenyataan. Ia berdiri dan berseru bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak mungkin wafat dan pasti akan kembali.

Lalu datanglah **Abu Bakr**. Ia masuk ke kamar Aisyah, mencium kening Nabi ﷺ, dan berkata: *"Engkau baik saat hidup dan baik saat wafat."*

Kemudian Abu Bakr keluar ke masjid dan berkata dengan tenang namun tegas:

**"Barangsiapa di antara kalian yang menyembah Muhammad — maka Muhammad telah wafat. Dan barangsiapa yang menyembah Allah — maka Allah Maha Hidup dan tidak akan pernah mati."**

Lalu ia membacakan ayat al-Quran: **"Muhammad itu tidak lain hanyalah seorang rasul; sesungguhnya telah berlalu sebelumnya beberapa orang rasul."** (QS Ali Imran: 144)

Mendengar ini, semua orang menangis. Umar jatuh terduduk — kakinya tidak kuat lagi. Ia berkata: "Seolah aku baru pertama kali mendengar ayat ini."

Kata-kata Abu Bakr mengajarkan bahwa **Islam bukan tentang satu orang** — betapapun agungnya orang itu — melainkan tentang Allah dan ajaran-Nya yang abadi.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Abu Bakr (pria tua berjenggot putih) berdiri tegap di masjid, satu tangan terangkat ke atas. Di hadapannya jamaah yang menangis. Cahaya datang dari arah mihrab. Warna dominan hijau tua dan emas. Suasana penuh emosi namun penuh ketenangan dari Abu Bakr. Komposisi Abu Bakr di tengah, jamaah di sekelilingnya.*

---

## Pelajaran dari Hari yang Paling Sedih

Wafatnya Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengajarkan kita banyak hal penting:

💫 **Semua makhluk akan kembali kepada Allah** — Bahkan Nabi ﷺ yang paling dicintai Allah juga wafat. Tidak ada yang kekal di dunia ini kecuali Allah.

📖 **Al-Quran dan Sunnah adalah warisan terindah** — Nabi ﷺ meninggalkan dua hal yang akan membimbing umat selamanya: al-Quran dan contoh kehidupan beliau.

💪 **Jadilah kuat saat sedih** — Abu Bakr sangat sedih, tapi ia tetap bisa memimpin umat. Ia menangis, lalu bangkit dan bertindak.

❤️ **Cintai Nabi ﷺ dengan mengikuti ajarannya** — Cara terbaik mencintai Nabi ﷺ bukan hanya menangisi kepergiannya, tapi mengamalkan apa yang beliau ajarkan.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Masjid Nabawi di Madinah dengan kubah hijau yang ikonik. Langit senja berwarna oranye dan ungu. Burung-burung terbang di langit. Warna dominan hijau, emas, dan ungu senja. Suasana damai dan penuh kerinduan. Komposisi lanskap lebar dengan kubah hijau di tengah.*
e31-wafat-rasulullah.general-en:
# The Death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ — 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH

## Context: Signs of Farewell

In the months preceding his death, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ gave subtle indications that his prophetic mission was nearing its end. The revelation of Surah An-Nasr during the Farewell Hajj was understood by several senior companions — particularly **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** and Abdullah ibn Abbas — as a notification of the Prophet's approaching death.^1

Imam al-Bukhari narrates that Abdullah ibn Abbas said: Umar ibn al-Khattab once invited me to a gathering of the veterans of Badr — though some objected due to my young age. Umar asked about the meaning of Surah An-Nasr. When the others were silent, I answered: *"It is a notification of the approaching death of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ."* Umar said: *"I know nothing of its meaning except what you have stated."*^1

During Ramadan of 10 AH, the Prophet ﷺ performed *i'tikaf* (spiritual retreat) for twenty days — twice his usual ten days. He also reviewed the Quran with Jibril twice in his final year — whereas he normally reviewed it once each Ramadan. Al-Bukhari narrates that the Prophet ﷺ told his daughter **Fatimah**: *"Jibril used to review the Quran with me once every year, but this year he reviewed it twice. I can only see this as a sign that my death is near."*^1

Fatimah wept upon hearing this. But the Prophet ﷺ then whispered to her again, and this time she smiled. When **Aisha** later asked what he had said, Fatimah revealed that the second whisper was news that she would be the foremost among the women of Paradise and the first of his family to join him.^1

## The Onset of Illness: The Night at Baqi'

Imam al-Bukhari and Ibn Hisham narrate that the Prophet's illness began at the end of the month of Safar, 11 AH. One night, the Prophet ﷺ awoke his servant Abu Muwayhib and said: *"I have been commanded to seek forgiveness for the people of Baqi'."*^1 ^2

They went to the Baqi' cemetery in the darkness of night. There, the Prophet ﷺ stood among the graves of the companions who had preceded him and prayed:

> السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَا أَهْلَ الْمَقَابِرِ لِيَهْنِ لَكُمْ مَا أَصْبَحْتُمْ فِيهِ مِمَّا أَصْبَحَ فِيهِ النَّاسُ أَقْبَلَتِ الْفِتَنُ كَقِطَعِ اللَّيْلِ الْمُظْلِمِ
>
> *"Peace be upon you, O dwellers of the graves. Glad tidings to you for the state you are in compared to what the living face. Trials approach like patches of dark night."*
> — Narrated by Muslim, Kitab al-Jana'iz

Then he told Abu Muwayhib: *"I have been offered the keys to the treasures of this world and eternal life therein followed by Paradise, or to meet my Lord and Paradise. I have chosen to meet my Lord and Paradise."*^2 After that night, the Prophet ﷺ began to suffer severe headaches.

## Days of Illness in Aisha's Chamber

### Moving to Aisha's Room

The Prophet ﷺ maintained a rotating schedule among the rooms of his wives. As the illness grew worse, he began asking repeatedly: *"Where am I tomorrow? Where am I tomorrow?"* His wives understood that he wished to be in Aisha's room, so they all permitted him to remain there.^1

**Aisha bint Abu Bakr** narrated her experience of caring for the Prophet ﷺ during his illness. She recounted that the Prophet ﷺ would lean against her chest or rest in her lap, and she would recite the *mu'awwidhat* (protective surahs) while passing his own hand over his body — for the Prophet's hand carried more blessing than anyone else's.^1

### Severe Fever

Al-Bukhari narrates that the Prophet ﷺ suffered from extremely high fever. Abdullah ibn Mas'ud once touched the Prophet's body and exclaimed: *"O Messenger of Allah, you are suffering a very severe fever!"* The Prophet ﷺ replied:

> إِنِّي أُوعَكُ كَمَا يُوعَكُ رَجُلَانِ مِنْكُمْ
>
> *"Indeed, I suffer from fever as severely as two men among you."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 5648

Ibn Mas'ud asked: *"Is that because you receive a double reward?"* The Prophet ﷺ answered: *"Yes, that is so."*^1 Even in his most acute suffering, the Prophet ﷺ transformed every moment into a lesson for his community.

### The Final Address in the Mosque

Despite his severe illness, the Prophet ﷺ exerted himself to ascend the mosque pulpit a few days before his death. Al-Bukhari narrates that he said:

> إِنَّ عَبْدًا خَيَّرَهُ اللَّهُ بَيْنَ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ مِنْ زَهْرَةِ الدُّنْيَا مَا شَاءَ وَبَيْنَ مَا عِنْدَهُ فَاخْتَارَ مَا عِنْدَهُ
>
> *"Indeed, there is a servant whom Allah has given the choice between the splendor of this world, as much as he wishes, and what is with Allah — and he has chosen what is with Allah."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 466

**Abu Bakr** immediately wept upon hearing this — he alone understood that the "servant" was the Prophet ﷺ himself, and that this meant farewell was imminent.^1 The other companions wondered at Abu Bakr's tears — why would he weep over mention of "a servant"? They would understand the meaning only later.

The Prophet ﷺ then ordered that all doors opening onto the mosque be sealed, except the door of Abu Bakr — a signal that Abu Bakr would be the one to lead the community after him.^1

### The Command for Abu Bakr to Lead the Prayer

When the Prophet ﷺ could no longer lead the prayer himself, he commanded: *"Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer."* Aisha tried to redirect this duty to Umar, knowing her father Abu Bakr would weep. But the Prophet ﷺ insisted:

> مُرُوا أَبَا بَكْرٍ فَلْيُصَلِّ بِالنَّاسِ ... إِنَّكُنَّ لَأَنْتُنَّ صَوَاحِبُ يُوسُفَ
>
> *"Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer ... You are indeed like the female companions of Yusuf."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 713

His meaning was that, just as the women in the story of Prophet Yusuf displayed one thing while concealing another intention, Aisha's real reason was personal — not that Abu Bakr was unworthy.^1

Abu Bakr led the prayer seventeen times during the Prophet's illness.^3 During one prayer, the Prophet ﷺ felt slightly better and came out to the mosque supported by Ali ibn Abi Talib and al-Fadl ibn Abbas. Seeing the Prophet ﷺ approach, Abu Bakr stepped back from the imam's position, but the Prophet ﷺ gestured for him to remain. The Prophet ﷺ sat beside Abu Bakr and prayed together — Abu Bakr followed the Prophet's movements, and the congregation followed Abu Bakr.^1

## The Final Day: Monday, 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH

### The Last Dawn

Imam al-Bukhari narrates from Anas ibn Malik: on Monday morning, as Abu Bakr was leading the Fajr prayer, the Prophet ﷺ drew aside the curtain of Aisha's room that faced the mosque. The companions saw his face — radiant as a page of the Quran. They nearly abandoned their prayer from sheer joy, believing the Prophet ﷺ had recovered.^1

The Prophet ﷺ smiled at the sight of the neatly ordered rows, then gestured for them to continue their prayer. He let the curtain fall once more. That was the last time the companions saw the face of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in full.

### The Final Moments

Aisha narrated the final moments of the Prophet's life in vivid detail. He was resting against her chest. Abdurrahman ibn Abu Bakr (Aisha's brother) entered carrying a *siwak* (tooth stick). The Prophet ﷺ gazed at the *siwak*, and Aisha understood that he wanted it. She took it, chewed it to soften it, then gave it to the Prophet ﷺ. He cleaned his teeth thoroughly — more thoroughly than Aisha had ever seen.^1

Then the Prophet ﷺ raised his hand — or his finger — and looked upward. Aisha heard him say:

> مَعَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ وَالصِّدِّيقِينَ وَالشُّهَدَاءِ وَالصَّالِحِينَ
>
> *"With those upon whom Allah has bestowed favor among the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous."*

Then he said:

> اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَارْحَمْنِي وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالرَّفِيقِ الْأَعْلَى
>
> *"O Allah, forgive me, have mercy on me, and join me with al-Rafiq al-A'la (the Highest Companion)."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 4440

He repeated "al-Rafiq al-A'la" three times. Then his hand fell, and he departed to be with Allah. Aisha said: *"He passed away between my chin and my chest."*^1

Ibn Sa'd in *Tabaqat al-Kubra* records that it was the time of Duha (mid-morning) on Monday, 12 Rabi al-Awwal, 11 AH — corresponding to June 8, 632 CE.^3 He was sixty-three years old.

## The Companions' Reactions

### Umar ibn al-Khattab: Denial and Anguish

The news of the Prophet's death struck the companions with unimaginable force. **Umar ibn al-Khattab** — a man renowned for his strength and resolve — refused to accept reality. Al-Bukhari narrates that Umar stood before the people and swore:

*"By Allah, the Messenger of Allah has not died! He has gone to meet his Lord just as Musa went to meet his Lord and was absent from his people for forty nights. By Allah, the Messenger of Allah will return and sever the hands and feet of those who claim he has died!"*^1

Umar continued to speak passionately, threatening anyone who said the Prophet ﷺ had died. The listeners were confused — some believed him, others doubted.

### Abu Bakr: Steadfastness in Crisis

While Umar was still speaking, **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** arrived at the mosque. He had been at his residence in Sunh (on the outskirts of Madinah) when he heard the news. He went directly to Aisha's room, uncovered the cloth from the Prophet's face, kissed his forehead, and said: *"By my father and mother, O Messenger of Allah. You are good in life and good in death."*^1

Abu Bakr then came out to the mosque. Umar was still speaking. Abu Bakr asked him to be silent, but Umar continued shouting. Abu Bakr did not force the matter — he began speaking on his own, and the people left Umar to listen to Abu Bakr.

Abu Bakr praised Allah, then spoke the words that would echo through the ages of Islamic history:

> مَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ يَعْبُدُ مُحَمَّدًا فَإِنَّ مُحَمَّدًا قَدْ مَاتَ وَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ حَيٌّ لَا يَمُوتُ
>
> *"Whoever among you worshipped Muhammad — then Muhammad has died. And whoever among you worshipped Allah — then Allah is Ever-Living and shall never die."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 3667

Then Abu Bakr recited the Quranic verse:

> وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰ أَعْقَابِكُمْ ﴿١٤٤﴾
>
> *"Muhammad is not but a messenger. Other messengers have passed before him. So if he were to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels?"*
> — QS. Ali Imran (3): 144

Al-Bukhari narrates that when Abu Bakr recited this verse, the people felt as though they were hearing it for the first time — even though they had memorized it years ago. Umar collapsed to the ground — his legs could no longer support him. He said: *"By Allah, it was as though I had never heard this verse until Abu Bakr recited it."*^1

### Grief Engulfing Madinah

Ibn Sa'd in *Tabaqat al-Kubra* describes Madinah on that day as a city submerged in grief unlike anything it had known before. Anas ibn Malik said: *"The day the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Madinah, everything was radiant. And the day he died, everything turned dark. We had scarcely dusted off our hands from burying him before we felt our very hearts had changed."*^3

## Washing, Funeral Prayer, and Burial

### Washing the Body

Ibn Sa'd narrates that the Prophet's body was washed by Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib (the Prophet's uncle), al-Fadl and Qutham (both sons of al-Abbas), Usamah ibn Zayd, and Shuqran (the Prophet's servant). Ali performed the actual washing while al-Abbas and his sons helped turn the body.^3

Ali remarked during the washing: *"By my father and mother, how fragrant you are — in life and in death."*^3 The Prophet ﷺ was washed without his garments being removed — a distinction unique to him.

### Funeral Prayer

What was unique about the Prophet's funeral was that **no single imam led the prayer**. The companions entered in waves — group after group — and each prayed individually over his body. Men first, then women, then children.^1 ^3

This process continued from Monday until the middle of the night between Tuesday and Wednesday.

### Burial

The companions initially disagreed about the burial site. Some proposed the mosque, others proposed Baqi'. Abu Bakr resolved the matter by conveying a hadith he had heard from the Prophet ﷺ:

> مَا قَبَضَ اللَّهُ نَبِيًّا إِلَّا فِي الْمَوْضِعِ الَّذِي يُحِبُّ أَنْ يُدْفَنَ فِيهِ
>
> *"Allah does not take the soul of a prophet except in the place where he wishes to be buried."*
> — Narrated by al-Tirmidhi

Thus the Prophet ﷺ was buried in Aisha's room — the place where he had died — beneath his bed. The grave was dug by Abu Talhah al-Ansari, who constructed a *lahd* (a lateral niche in the side of the grave, the burial style of Madinah).^3

The burial took place in the middle of the night on Wednesday. The last person to descend into the grave was Qutham ibn al-Abbas.

## Analysis: The Meaning of the Prophet's Death for Islamic Civilization

### The End of Revelation, the Beginning of Responsibility

With the death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, revelation from heaven was severed forever. No prophet would come after him — this is a foundational belief of Islam. Yet the end of revelation did not mean the end of guidance; the Quran and Sunnah he left behind became an eternal compass for humanity.

### The Test of Leadership

The moments following the Prophet's death constituted the most severe test of leadership in Islamic history. The contrast between Umar's reaction (emotional, in denial) and Abu Bakr's response (composed, decisive, rooted in the Quran) illustrates why Abu Bakr was destined to become the first caliph. True leadership is tested not when all is well, but when crisis strikes.

### The Humanity of the Prophet ﷺ

One of the most important lessons from the Prophet's death is the affirmation of his humanity. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was not God — he was a human being who fell ill, suffered, and ultimately died like all others. Yet the way he faced death — with prayer, surrender to Allah, and compassion for those closest to him — made his very death a final lesson for his community.

### Eternal Love

The relationship between the Prophet ﷺ and his companions — especially Aisha who nursed him with devoted tenderness, Abu Bakr who demonstrated unwavering faith in the darkest moment, and Umar whose emotional reaction revealed the depth of his love — represents the most beautiful portrait of human bonds in Islamic history.

## Lessons: Teachings from the Darkest Day

The death of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ teaches Muslims several fundamental lessons:

**First**, that Islam does not depend on a single individual — however great that individual may be — but on the teachings he brought. Abu Bakr reminded: *"Whoever worshipped Muhammad — Muhammad has died. Whoever worshipped Allah — Allah is Ever-Living and shall never die."*

**Second**, that death is a certainty for every creature. If the Prophet ﷺ, the most beloved of Allah, died, then no one is immortal in this world.

**Third**, that how a person faces crisis defines their character. Abu Bakr became the model for managing grief — he wept, kissed the Prophet's forehead, then rose and led the community. Grief did not destroy him; he transformed his sorrow into strength.

**Fourth**, that the Prophet's final words — choosing "al-Rafiq al-A'la" (the Highest Companion) — demonstrate that for the believer, death is not an ending, but a reunion with Allah and His beloved ones.

---

## References

1. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih Bukhari). Hadith no. 4440 (death of the Prophet), no. 3667 (Abu Bakr's address), no. 5648 (the Prophet's fever), no. 466 (sermon of the servant given a choice), no. 713 (command for Abu Bakr to lead prayer).

2. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Vol. 2 (chapter on the death of the Messenger of Allah). Beirut: Dar Sadir.
